zh-tw.title: 健身房語錄
zh-tw.description: 獻給每一個在 Smith 機前排隊、被教練說「再一下」的人——精選最有共感的重訓厭世語錄、補劑迷思自嘲、鏡子前的內心小劇場，以及暑假前最誠實的健身房觀察筆記

en.title: Gym Quotes
en.description: For everyone who's queued at the Smith machine and heard 'just one more rep' — the most relatable lifting confessions, supplement-budget self-mockery, mirror-room monologues, and the most honest pre-summer gym-floor observations you'll ever read

items.sourceNote: 健身房三個月成果 + 媽媽否定 + 教練吐槽 三段格式重寫

zh-tw.content:
我重訓三個月了。
媽媽：怎麼看起來還是一樣胖。
教練：那是因為你從來沒去。

會員費跟蛋白粉錢，原來是繳給良心的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 暑假前傳給每週說「明天去健身房」的朋友，他應該還沒換衣服
variations: 更新：第四個月我終於去了，教練說：你是新會員嗎？

en.content:
Three months of lifting.
Mom: You look the same kind of chubby.
Trainer: That's because you've never actually come.

Turns out the membership fee was a guilt tax.
en.editorNote: Send before summer to the friend who keeps saying 'tomorrow I'm going' — they probably haven't changed yet
variations: Update: I finally showed up in month four and the trainer asked if I was a new member.

items.sourceNote: 「健身房鏡子是世界上最嚴格的鏡子」原創角度展開

zh-tw.content:
健身房的鏡子是世界上最嚴格的鏡子。
也是角度最多的。

所以你永遠可以找到一個你不喜歡的自己，
然後再花九十分鐘確認它真的存在。
zh-tw.editorNote: 在你做完核心訓練後當下打開限動，配一張側拍
variations: 結論：我不是來練的，我是來和五個版本的自己內耗的。

en.content:
Gym mirrors are the strictest mirrors in the world.
And the most angled.

Which means you can always find a version of yourself you don't like,
then spend ninety minutes confirming it's real.
en.editorNote: Post on your story right after core day, paired with a candid side shot
variations: Conclusion: I'm not here to train, I'm here to argue with five versions of myself.

items.sourceNote: 補劑預算 vs 廢吃預算 比例對照格式

zh-tw.content:
補劑一個月：6000。
訓練費一個月：1500。
外送跟宵夜一個月：30000。

結論：我不是在練身材，我是在養補劑。它們過得比我還好。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合月底打開帳本後傳給健身房戰友，搭配一張剛收到的蝦皮箱子
variations: 升級版：補劑桌已經比我的健身桌還壯。

en.content:
Supplements: $200 a month.
Training fees: $50 a month.
Late-night takeout: $1000 a month.

Conclusion: I'm not building my body, I'm raising my supplements. They live better than I do.
en.editorNote: Best sent at month-end with a photo of your latest Amazon haul
variations: Update: my supplement shelf is now visibly more jacked than I am.

items.sourceNote: 「練一年體重沒變但衣服變緊」教練 vs 媽媽 雙觀點格式

zh-tw.content:
重訓一年。
體重沒變。
衣服變緊。

教練說：恭喜，這叫進步。
媽媽說：你就是變胖。

我選擇相信收我會員費的那一邊。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合配一張過年回家被親戚念的合照
variations: 中年版：第二年衣服繼續變緊，教練改口說：你該換衣服了。

en.content:
One year of lifting.
Weight: unchanged.
Shirts: tighter.

Trainer: Congrats, that's progress.
Mom: You just got fat.

I choose to believe the one collecting my membership fee.
en.editorNote: Pairs perfectly with a Lunar New Year family photo where your aunt commented
variations: Year two: shirts still tightening, trainer now says I 'need to size up'.

items.sourceNote: Smith 機 vs 啞鈴區 空間政治 觀察笑話

zh-tw.content:
Smith 機前面：永遠排隊。
啞鈴區地上：永遠沒人收。
滑輪下拉旁邊：永遠有人滑手機坐三十分鐘。

健身房不是運動空間，是大型行為藝術展。
而我就是長期觀察員。
zh-tw.editorNote: 尖峰時段在跑步機上偷拍背影限動用
variations: 備註：身為觀察員的代價是，我也從來沒收過啞鈴。

en.content:
Smith machine: line of six.
Dumbbell racks: a graveyard of orphans.
Lat pulldown bench: someone scrolling for thirty minutes.

The gym isn't a fitness facility. It's a long-running performance art exhibit.
And I'm just the resident critic.
en.editorNote: Best used during 6pm peak hour with a stealth shot from the treadmill
variations: Disclosure: as the critic, I have also never re-racked a dumbbell.

items.sourceNote: 「再一下」教練 vs 學員 期望落差 格式

zh-tw.content:
教練說「再一下」的時候，
指的不是再一下，
是再五下、再一組、加重、加組數，
然後說「下次我們再加一點」。

「下次」這兩個字，是健身房裡最危險的詞。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合做完腿之後癱在地上時發限動
variations: 翻譯小辭典：教練說「很輕」＝你之後三天爬不了樓梯。

en.content:
When my trainer says 'one more',
he doesn't mean one more.
He means five more, then another set, then more weight,
then 'we'll add a little next time'.

'Next time' is the most dangerous phrase in the gym.
en.editorNote: Post-leg-day, posted from the floor
variations: Glossary: 'this is light' means you won't take stairs for three days.

items.sourceNote: 練胸日 / 練腿日 / 休息日 三段反差結構

zh-tw.content:
練胸日：人很多，我很爽。
練腿日：人很少，我很累。
休息日：人最多的是我外送的餐點。

所謂一週分化，是把痛苦平均分散到七天。
zh-tw.editorNote: 週日晚上整理下週課表時，順手傳給健身房群組
variations: 進階版：練背日永遠卡課，因為我從來找不到自己的背。

en.content:
Chest day: crowded, glorious.
Leg day: empty, brutal.
Rest day: my delivery driver works overtime.

A split routine is just pain spread evenly across seven days.
en.editorNote: Send to the gym group chat on Sunday night while planning the week
variations: Advanced version: back day always gets skipped because I can't locate my own back.

items.sourceNote: 「健身使我快落」反諷 register — 訓練後真實狀態 對比

zh-tw.content:
貼文：「健身使我快落，揮汗如雨真療癒。」

配圖：剛訓練完，半癱在地，水壺翻倒，毛巾濕成一團，腿在抖。

快樂的部分，是我終於可以坐下了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合做完高強度訓練後直接發限動，文案越燦爛照片越誠實越好
variations: 後續：朋友按愛心問我是不是哭了。我說那是汗，但其實兩者都有。

en.content:
Caption: 'Working out makes me so happy, sweat is therapy.'

Photo: post-set collapse on the floor, water bottle tipped over, towel soaked, legs trembling.

The happy part is that I finally get to sit down.
en.editorNote: Best deployed right after a brutal session — the more glowing the caption, the more honest the photo should be
variations: Follow-up: a friend asked if I was crying. I said it was sweat. (It was both.)

items.sourceNote: 蛋白粉迷思 vs 雞胸肉現實 對比格式

zh-tw.content:
我以為我會因為蛋白粉變強。
後來才知道，
讓人變強的是雞胸肉、睡覺、跟不要每三天放棄。

蛋白粉只是讓我心安理得地多吃一頓早餐。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在 IG 配一張早餐有兩顆蛋一個地瓜的照片
variations: 進階心得：第二罐喝完才發現，我的弱點不是蛋白質，是意志力。

en.content:
I thought protein powder would make me strong.
Turns out what makes you strong is chicken breast, sleep, and not quitting every three days.

Protein powder just lets me eat a second breakfast guilt-free.
en.editorNote: Best paired with an IG shot of two eggs, oatmeal, and a sweet potato
variations: Lesson learned by tub two: my weak link wasn't protein. It was discipline.

items.sourceNote: 健身愛好者自我推銷 = CrossFit 經典梗 在地化

zh-tw.content:
怎麼知道一個人有在健身？
別擔心，他會自己跟你說。
第一次見面說、開會中說、結婚喜宴上也說。

如果他沒說，那不是他沒在練，是他剛吃完，沒力氣講。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合健身房群組裡有同事新加入時拋一句調侃
variations: 備註：本人經多年驗證，是同一種人。

en.content:
How do you spot someone who lifts?
Don't worry. They'll tell you.
First date, team meeting, their cousin's wedding.

If they don't, it's not that they don't train. They just ate, and they're too tired to talk.
en.editorNote: Drop into the gym group chat when a coworker just joined
variations: Disclosure: years of testing confirm I am exactly this person.

items.sourceNote: 「明天開始」 拖延文化 在地化 重訓 register

zh-tw.content:
我從春天開始說要練夏天的身材。
夏天到了，我說要練秋天的。
秋天到了，我說反正穿很多。
冬天到了，我說過完年再來。

我練的不是身材，是季節輪替的耐受力。
zh-tw.editorNote: 暑假前一週傳給每年都在「下個月開始」的朋友
variations: 結論：我的身材跟我一樣，四季如一。

en.content:
In spring I said I'd build a summer body.
In summer I said I'd build an autumn one.
In autumn I said the layers will cover it.
In winter I said after New Year.

I'm not building a body. I'm building tolerance for the changing seasons.
en.editorNote: Send a week before summer to the friend who's started 'next month' for three years
variations: Conclusion: my body, like me, stays the same all four seasons.

items.sourceNote: 「練完吃更多」熱量赤字反諷 在地化

zh-tw.content:
教練說：練完一定要補蛋白質。
我說：好。

於是我練了五百大卡，補了一千八百大卡，
還順便補了一杯珍奶，因為練完當然要喝。

熱量赤字赤了個寂寞。
zh-tw.editorNote: 練完當天晚上點消夜時自動觸發的內心 OS
variations: 教練後續：那不叫補蛋白質，那叫慶功宴。

en.content:
Trainer: Refuel with protein after training.
Me: Got it.

So I burned 500 calories, refueled with 1,800,
and threw in a milk tea because earned-it logic.

Calorie deficit was achieved, in spirit only.
en.editorNote: Internal monologue when ordering late-night snacks post-workout
variations: Trainer's follow-up: that's not refueling, that's a victory dinner.

items.sourceNote: 「再三組就好」教練 vs 體感 對比 register

zh-tw.content:
「再三組就好」——教練講的時候，語氣像是在點奶茶。

做到第二組，我已經在心裡幫自己寫遺囑。
做完第三組，我發現遺囑漏了健身房會員費的退款條款。

教練微笑：很棒，下次加重。
zh-tw.editorNote: 尤其適合做完深蹲後的限動，配一張電梯按鈕的特寫
variations: 後續：第四組是教練給的小驚喜，他叫它『熱身完成』。

en.content:
'Just three more sets' — said in the tone of ordering boba.

By set two, I'm drafting my will.
By set three, I realize I forgot to mention the gym refund clause.

Trainer smiles: Great. We'll go heavier next time.
en.editorNote: Perfect for a post-squat story, paired with a close-up of the elevator button
variations: Bonus: a fourth set he called 'finishing the warm-up'.

items.sourceNote: 「休息也是訓練的一部分」合理化 register

zh-tw.content:
他們說：休息也是訓練的一部分。

所以我這週，認真休息了五天，
非常用力地不練了兩天，
然後在週日深度休息了一整天。

本週訓練完成度：100%。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合週日晚上回顧週計畫時，配一張遙控器跟泡麵的合照
variations: 進階版：下週我要挑戰連續休息七天的進階課表。

en.content:
They say rest is part of training.

So this week I rested seriously for five days,
intensely did not lift for two,
and on Sunday I rested at an advanced level.

Weekly training completion: 100%.
en.editorNote: Best on a Sunday night while reviewing your weekly plan, paired with a remote-and-ramen flat lay
variations: Next week's challenge: a full seven-day rest cycle.

items.sourceNote: 「身體 ego vs 鏡子 ego」內心戲 register

zh-tw.content:
在健身房，我有兩個自我。

一個說：今天輕一點，受傷划不來。
另一個說：旁邊那個人在看，加重。

結果是，我加了重，受傷了，而對方根本沒在看我。
他在看自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在等鎖匙櫃時順手發限動，搭配剛拉傷的肩膀照
variations: 結論：健身房沒有觀眾，只有同事業的演員。

en.content:
At the gym, I have two selves.

One says: Go light today, an injury isn't worth it.
The other says: That guy is watching. Load up.

Result: I loaded up, I got hurt, and that guy wasn't watching me.
He was watching himself.
en.editorNote: Story-worthy while waiting at the locker, paired with a freshly tweaked shoulder
variations: Conclusion: the gym has no audience. Only fellow performers.

items.sourceNote: 跑步機 vs 操場 體感落差 在地化

zh-tw.content:
跑步機上跑六公里：氣不喘、心情好、邊看 Netflix。
操場上跑兩公里：感覺人生在跟我告別。

結論：我練的不是體能，是冷氣跟 Wi-Fi。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合夏天剛從室外跑回來、癱在健身房門口時發限動
variations: 進階版：跑步機壞掉那天，我也跟著壞掉了。

en.content:
Six km on the treadmill: easy breathing, good mood, mid-Netflix episode.
Two km on an outdoor track: my life is flashing before my eyes.

Conclusion: I'm not training cardio. I'm training AC and Wi-Fi.
en.editorNote: Best deployed after stumbling back from an outdoor run, collapsed at the gym entrance
variations: Update: the day the treadmill broke, so did I.

items.sourceNote: pre-workout 過量 心悸 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
教練說：一匙就夠。
我：一匙看起來很少。

二十分鐘後，我在更衣室裡，
心跳像在打鼓，手在抖，眼睛瞪得很大，
而我還沒開始熱身。

那天我練得很弱，但我活得很用力。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合 pre-workout 過量當下，蹲在置物櫃前自拍時用
variations: 後續：教練說『下次照劑量』，我說我下次會記得。下次。

en.content:
Trainer: One scoop is plenty.
Me: One scoop looks like nothing.

Twenty minutes later in the locker room,
heart pounding, hands shaking, eyes wide,
and I haven't even warmed up.

I lifted weak that day. But I lived loud.
en.editorNote: Use right after over-scooping, while crouched in front of your locker
variations: Follow-up: trainer said 'follow the label next time'. I said sure. Next time.

items.sourceNote: DOMS 延遲性痠痛 第二天 vs 第三天 在地化

zh-tw.content:
練腿當天：哇我超強，我可以再加重。
第二天：還行，只是上下樓梯有點怪。
第三天：馬桶是我的敵人，沙發是我的監獄，襪子穿不起來。

結論：痛苦不會準時來，但一定會來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合練腿後第三天早上，坐在馬桶上時的限動文案
variations: 進階版：第四天我終於可以蹲下，然後我馬上預約下次練腿。為什麼。

en.content:
Leg day: I'm a god, I should've gone heavier.
Day two: I'm fine, stairs just feel weird.
Day three: the toilet is my enemy, the couch is my prison, and socks are now optional.

Conclusion: the pain is never on time, but it always shows up.
en.editorNote: Best posted on the morning of day three, captioned from the toilet
variations: Day four: I can finally squat down. So naturally, I book another leg day. Why.

items.sourceNote: PR 紀錄 vs 限動發文 比例 反諷 register

zh-tw.content:
破 PR 那一下：三秒。
喘到不能講話：五分鐘。
錄影、剪片、加慢動作、配音樂、上字幕：四十分鐘。
選 hashtag、想文案、猶豫要不要 @ 教練：兩小時。

那一下到底是為了破紀錄，還是為了發限動，我自己都不確定。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在剛剪完 PR 影片、限動還沒按發布前的最後一秒掙扎時用
variations: 結論：我練的是肌肉，但我經營的是內容。

en.content:
The PR rep: three seconds.
Catching my breath: five minutes.
Filming, editing, slow-mo, music, captions: forty minutes.
Picking hashtags, drafting the caption, debating whether to tag my coach: two hours.

Was that rep for the record, or for the story? I genuinely don't know anymore.
en.editorNote: Best at the exact moment before you hit 'post' on the freshly cut PR reel
variations: Conclusion: I train muscles. But I run a content business.

items.sourceNote: 硬舉 = 脊椎透支 反諷 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
硬舉那一下，我聽見自己的脊椎在跟我談判：

「你最多再來兩下，第三下我就要罷工。」
我說：「再一組就好。」
它說：「那你下週去找復健科。」

結果我加了重，它說：「掰，下個月見。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合硬舉日結束、躺在地墊上滑手機時的限動文案
variations: 備註：我的脊椎跟我之間，是一段長期的勞資爭議。
後續：教練說『腰是用來代償的』。我說『代償也要先有本金』。

en.content:
Mid-deadlift, I heard my spine negotiating with me:

'You get two more reps. Third one, I strike.'
I said, 'Just one more set.'
It said, 'Then you're seeing a chiropractor next week.'

I added weight anyway. It said, 'Bye. See you next month.'
en.editorNote: Best posted while lying on the mat after deadlift day, scrolling on your phone
variations: Note: my spine and I are in an ongoing labor dispute.
Trainer said 'your back will compensate'. I said 'compensation requires equity'.

items.sourceNote: Gym bro 戴耳機四十分鐘 vs 真正組數 反差 在地化

zh-tw.content:
我在健身房戴耳機的時間：90 分鐘。
認真訓練的時間：20 分鐘。
換歌、滑歌單、調音量、找一首夠燃的：35 分鐘。
看著鏡子等那首歌進高潮：剩下全部。

我不是來練的，我是來辦一場個人演唱會。觀眾只有我自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合戴著耳機走出健身房、汗只有一點點時發限動
variations: 結論：我的播放清單比我的訓練菜單還認真。
進階版：今天歌單沒做好，整場訓練都不對。

en.content:
Time spent wearing headphones at the gym: 90 minutes.
Time spent actually lifting: 20 minutes.
Skipping tracks, scrolling playlists, adjusting volume, hunting for a song that hits: 35 minutes.
Staring in the mirror waiting for the drop: the rest.

I'm not training. I'm performing a one-man concert. Audience of one.
en.editorNote: Post while walking out of the gym, headphones still in, barely sweating
variations: Conclusion: my playlist takes more effort than my program.
Bad playlist day, bad training day. There's a correlation.

items.sourceNote: 臥推報數 vs 真實重量 灌水 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
別人問我臥推幾公斤：

當天最重那組：報。
做完馬上掉重那組：跳過。
只完成一下、輔助手插了一半：四捨五入往上加。
上次練的那次：也算我現在的。

結論：我的臥推紀錄是浮動匯率，不是公斤數。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在群組裡有人問「你現在臥推多少」之前先發制人
variations: 備註：問我深蹲也一樣，請參考國際匯率公告。
翻譯：我說一百，意思是上次八十、輔助一半、然後我選擇相信自己。

en.content:
When someone asks my bench:

The heaviest set of the day: counts.
The set I dropped right after: skipped.
The one-rep with half a spotter: rounded up generously.
Last week's PR: also counts as today's.

My bench number isn't a weight. It's a floating exchange rate.
en.editorNote: Use preemptively before someone in the group chat asks 'so what's your bench now?'
variations: Note: same conversion applies to squat. Consult the daily forex board.
Translation: I said 225. Meaning 185 last week, with a spotter, and a generous belief in myself.

items.sourceNote: 健身房水壺 = 身份證 in-group 觀察 在地化

zh-tw.content:
健身房裡，水壺就是身份證。

一公升塑膠：新手。
兩公升搖搖杯+量匙：中階。
一加侖透明水壺、貼名字、附吸管：核心會員。
沒帶水壺、只喝飲水機：你來幹嘛的？

我升級水壺那天，比我加重那天還隆重。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合剛買新水壺的隔天，配一張水壺擺在槓片上的合照
variations: 進階版：我水壺比我背包還貴，但我覺得很合理。
備註：水壺貼名字是健身房成年禮。

en.content:
At the gym, your water bottle is your ID.

1L plastic: rookie.
2L shaker with scoop: intermediate.
Gallon jug, name labeled, straw included: core member.
No bottle, drinking from the fountain: why are you here?

The day I upgraded my bottle was more ceremonial than the day I added weight.
en.editorNote: Best the day after a new bottle drop, paired with a flat lay on top of a plate
variations: Advanced: my water bottle costs more than my backpack. I stand by it.
Note: labeling your jug is the gym's coming-of-age ritual.

items.sourceNote: 一月新會員潮 vs 三月空蕩健身房 年度循環 在地化

zh-tw.content:
一月的健身房：跑步機要抽號碼牌，啞鈴區像菜市場。
二月的健身房：人剩一半，但大家都還在。
三月的健身房：你和教練，跟一個我從沒看過的阿伯。

所謂新年新希望，其實是健身房的年度租金回收計畫。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合農曆年後第一次踏進健身房、發現人變少時的限動
variations: 結論：我撐到三月，不是我變強，是別人都先放棄了。
備註：那個阿伯練了二十年，他不會走，他是健身房的牆。

en.content:
Gym in January: take a number for the treadmill, the dumbbell rack is a market.
Gym in February: half the crowd, but they're still showing up.
Gym in March: you, your trainer, and one old man you've never seen before.

New Year's resolutions aren't goals. They're the gym's annual rent collection plan.
en.editorNote: Post the first time you walk in after Lunar New Year and notice the empty floor
variations: Conclusion: I made it to March not because I got stronger, but because everyone else quit first.
Note: the old man has been lifting for twenty years. He's not leaving. He IS the gym.

items.sourceNote: 健身穿搭 vs 真實訓練 比例反差 register

zh-tw.content:
出門前換衣服：四十分鐘。
搭配運動褲、上衣、襪子、頭帶、髮圈：精心。
包包裡備一件乾的：必備。

進健身房真正訓練：二十五分鐘。
其中十五分鐘在鏡子前確認穿搭沒亂。

我練的不是身材，是當天的造型。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合穿好新買的健身套裝、發完限動後再去訓練前的內心 OS
variations: 備註：當天衣服好看，菜表自動減半。
進階版：忘記帶頭帶那天，我直接放棄今天的訓練。

en.content:
Time spent picking the outfit: 40 minutes.
Coordinating leggings, top, socks, headband, scrunchie: meticulous.
Dry change of clothes in the bag: non-negotiable.

Actual lifting at the gym: 25 minutes.
Fifteen of which were spent in the mirror checking the fit.

I'm not training my body. I'm training the look.
en.editorNote: Internal monologue after posting a story in the new set, before actually starting the workout
variations: Note: if the outfit slaps, the program is auto-halved.
Advanced: the day I forgot my headband, I cancelled the entire session.

items.sourceNote: 搖搖杯 = 生化武器 在地化 register

zh-tw.content:
搖搖杯放三天沒洗，打開那一刻，
我懷疑自己創造了一個新物種。

它已經不是蛋白粉，是生化武器。
蓋子鎖死，內壁長毛，香蕉口味變成不可名狀。

結論：肌肉沒練起來，但我室友被薰跑了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在水槽前發現遺忘的搖搖杯時，當下發限動配一張封閉的杯口
variations: 後續：我選擇直接丟掉，買新的。便宜，也安全。
備註：搖搖杯放越久，越能驗證蛋白質的存在。

en.content:
Shaker bottle left for three days. The moment I cracked the lid,
I'm pretty sure I invented a new life form.

It wasn't protein anymore. It was a biohazard.
Lid sealed shut, fuzz on the inside, banana flavor now indescribable.

Conclusion: I didn't gain muscle. But my roommate moved out.
en.editorNote: Post the moment you find the forgotten shaker by the sink, paired with a shot of the sealed lid
variations: Follow-up: I threw it out and bought a new one. Cheaper. Safer.
Note: the longer a shaker sits, the more evidence you have that protein exists.

items.sourceNote: 健身房眼神 = 重訓量 = 社交距離 觀察 register

zh-tw.content:
健身房的社交守則：

眼神對到 0.5 秒：點頭。
眼神對到 1 秒：尷尬。
眼神對到 2 秒：對方以為你要借槓片。
眼神對到 3 秒：對方以為你要搭訕。
眼神對到 4 秒：你們已經結婚。

所以我訓練全程低頭，看地板看到認識每一條刮痕。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合戴帽子練胸日結束後發限動，配一張地板特寫
variations: 結論：我不是內向，我是健身房的合法生存策略。
備註：耳機是健身房的隱形斗篷。

en.content:
Gym social code:

Eye contact 0.5 seconds: nod.
Eye contact 1 second: awkward.
Eye contact 2 seconds: they think you want their plates.
Eye contact 3 seconds: they think you're flirting.
Eye contact 4 seconds: congratulations, you're married.

So I train looking at the floor. I now recognize every scratch personally.
en.editorNote: Post after a hat-on chest day, paired with a close-up of the floor
variations: Conclusion: I'm not introverted. I'm just legally surviving the gym.
Note: headphones are the gym's invisibility cloak.

items.sourceNote: 深蹲架做二頭彎舉 經典 gym etiquette 罪行 在地化

zh-tw.content:
我在深蹲架前等了二十分鐘。
那位大哥在做二頭彎舉。
用一根二十公斤的槓，做了五下，然後滑手機。

我問他還要多久。
他說：「快了，再三組。」

那一刻我懂了，這不叫健身房，這叫繳費停車場。
zh-tw.editorNote: 尖峰時段被深蹲架佔用時，蹲在角落滑手機等待用
variations: 備註：深蹲架做彎舉，是健身房的原罪。
結論：我練的不是腿，是耐心。

en.content:
I waited twenty minutes for the squat rack.
The guy in it was doing bicep curls.
A 45-pound bar, five reps, then ten minutes of scrolling.

I asked how much longer.
He said, 'Almost done, three more sets.'

That's when I realized: this isn't a gym. It's a paid parking lot.
en.editorNote: Use during peak hours while crouched in the corner watching the squat rack get hostage-taken
variations: Note: curling in the squat rack is the original sin of gym culture.
Conclusion: I'm not training legs. I'm training patience.

items.sourceNote: 健身 app 紀錄 vs 真實表現 反差 register

zh-tw.content:
我的健身 app 顯示：
本月訓練 18 次、總組數 432、總重量 24 噸。

我的鏡子顯示：
你確定？

我的體脂計顯示：
你還好嗎？

我的褲子顯示：
建議重新檢視人生。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合月底打開 app 截圖、又被鏡子打臉時的限動
variations: 備註：數字騙不了人，但我可以騙我自己。
進階版：app 把熱身也算進總組數，我覺得很合理。

en.content:
My fitness app says:
18 sessions this month, 432 sets, 24 tons lifted.

My mirror says:
Are you sure?

My scale says:
You okay?

My jeans say:
Let's review some life choices.
en.editorNote: Best at month-end when the app screenshot meets the mirror reality check
variations: Note: numbers don't lie. But I lie to myself with great consistency.
Advanced: my app counts warm-up sets in the total. I find this acceptable.

items.sourceNote: 練完一週等 gains 不耐煩 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
練第一週：
每天早上脫上衣，對著鏡子端詳三十秒。

第三天：感覺好像有一點線條。
第五天：拍照存證，從正面側面再來一張。
第七天：對著媽媽展示成果。

媽媽說：你是不是吃太飽肚子撐起來？
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合練完第一週、興奮拍照給媽媽看的隔天用
variations: 結論：肌肉的成長速度，跟我老闆加薪的速度差不多。
備註：第二週我已經不照鏡子了。

en.content:
Week one of training:
Every morning, shirt off, thirty-second mirror inspection.

Day three: I think I see a line.
Day five: photo evidence. Front, side, another front.
Day seven: I show Mom the results.

Mom says: 'Did you just eat a big lunch?'
en.editorNote: Best the day after you proudly show your week-one progress to your mom
variations: Conclusion: muscle grows at the same speed as my boss approves raises.
Note: by week two, I had stopped looking in the mirror.

items.sourceNote: 健身房 wifi 密碼 vs 訓練紀律 反差 register

zh-tw.content:
我去健身房第一件事：
連 wifi。

第二件事：
坐在臥推椅上回訊息。
第三件事：
滑社群看別人練。
第四件事：
看時間，發現只剩二十分鐘。
第五件事：
趕快做兩組臥推，假裝今天有來。

我繳的不是會員費，是上網吃到飽。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在健身房滑手機滑到時間快到、開始恐慌時發限動
variations: 結論：健身房 wifi 比我家還快，這就是我去的理由。
進階版：那天 wifi 壞了，我直接離開，根本沒練。

en.content:
First thing I do at the gym:
Connect to the Wi-Fi.

Second: sit on the bench press, reply to texts.
Third: scroll through other people's workouts.
Fourth: check the time, realize I have twenty minutes left.
Fifth: rush two sets of bench, pretend I trained.

I'm not paying for a membership. I'm paying for unlimited data.
en.editorNote: Post mid-scroll on the bench when you suddenly notice the clock
variations: Conclusion: the gym's Wi-Fi is faster than mine at home. That's why I show up.
Advanced: the day the Wi-Fi was down, I left without training. No regrets.

items.sourceNote: 健身好友 vs 一起放棄 損友 反差 register

zh-tw.content:
我跟朋友約好一起去健身房。

我：「七點見。」
他：「好。」
六點五十：「我有點累，明天？」
我：「也是，明天好了。」

那一刻我們的友情昇華了。
我們不是一起變強，我們是一起放棄。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合臨時取消健身約後，傳給那個共犯朋友的限動
variations: 結論：能一起耍廢的朋友，才是真朋友。
備註：我們約了三個月，目前出席率 0%，但感情更好了。

en.content:
Made plans with a friend to hit the gym.

Me: 'See you at seven.'
Him: 'Sounds good.'
6:50 PM: 'I'm a little tired, tomorrow?'
Me: 'Yeah, tomorrow works.'

In that moment our friendship deepened.
We don't get stronger together. We give up together.
en.editorNote: Send to the co-conspirator friend right after you both bail on a gym session
variations: Conclusion: a friend you can flake with is a true friend.
Note: we've been scheduling this for three months. Attendance rate: 0%. Bond: stronger than ever.

items.sourceNote: 深蹲架霸佔四十分鐘 vs 排隊者吐槽 在地化 register

zh-tw.content:
我問深蹲架那位大哥：「請問還要多久？」
他抬起頭：「我已經練三十分鐘了，再十分鐘也還好吧。」

好，那這樣算起來，
我等的不是深蹲架，是一段成熟的時間觀念。

我選擇去做啞鈴硬舉。腰不會痛，但心會。
zh-tw.editorNote: 排隊深蹲架超過二十分鐘、忍不住翻白眼那一刻發限動
variations: 備註：他練的不是腿，是別人的耐性。
結論：健身房最重的器材，不是槓鈴，是別人的時間觀。

en.content:
I asked the guy at the squat rack: 'How much longer?'
He looked up: 'I've already been here 30 minutes. Another 10 won't kill you.'

Great. So I'm not waiting for the rack.
I'm waiting for a mature concept of time.

I went and did dumbbell deadlifts. My back doesn't hurt. My soul does.
en.editorNote: Post the second you roll your eyes after twenty minutes of waiting at the rack
variations: Note: he's not training legs. He's training everyone else's patience.
Conclusion: the heaviest thing in the gym isn't the barbell. It's other people's sense of time.

items.sourceNote: 「再睡五分鐘」vs 晨練鬧鐘 自我談判 register

zh-tw.content:
鬧鐘 5:30 響。
我：再五分鐘。
5:35：再五分鐘。
5:40：今天天氣好像不太好。
5:45：我感覺有點感冒。
5:50：休息也是訓練的一部分。
6:00：今天直接休息日。

我跟我自己談判的能力，比我教練還強。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合鬧鐘按掉第七次後、躺在床上滑社群時的限動
variations: 備註：我的晨練菜表，全部都在床上完成。
進階版：那天我五點起床，是因為我忘記今天是禮拜六。

en.content:
Alarm goes off at 5:30 AM.
Me: Five more minutes.
5:35: Five more minutes.
5:40: Weather looks bad.
5:45: I think I'm getting a cold.
5:50: Rest is part of training.
6:00: Today's officially a rest day.

My ability to negotiate with myself is stronger than my trainer.
en.editorNote: Post after snoozing the alarm seven times, scrolling in bed
variations: Note: my morning routine is entirely completed under the blanket.
Advanced: the one day I made it up at 5, it was because I forgot it was Saturday.

items.sourceNote: 教練檢查飲食紀錄 vs 學員選擇性誠實 register

zh-tw.content:
教練：「這週飲食紀錄給我看一下。」

我傳出去的：早餐燕麥、午餐雞胸沙拉、晚餐糙米。

我沒傳的：宵夜鹹酥雞、半夜珍奶、辦公室同事帶的蛋糕、捷運站旁邊那家蔥抓餅。

教練：「很標準耶，怎麼還是沒進度？」
我：「就，可能基因吧。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳完假版飲食紀錄給教練、心虛點開外送 App 時用
variations: 備註：我傳的飲食紀錄，是一份小說。
結論：跟教練說真話，是健身路上最難的一組。

en.content:
Trainer: 'Send me this week's food log.'

What I sent: oats for breakfast, chicken salad for lunch, brown rice for dinner.

What I didn't send: midnight fried chicken, boba at 1 AM, the cake a coworker brought in, the scallion pancake by the station.

Trainer: 'Looks clean. Why no progress?'
Me: 'Probably just genetics.'
en.editorNote: Use right after sending a fictional food log, mid-guilt while opening UberEats
variations: Note: the food log I send is technically a novel.
Conclusion: being honest with your trainer is the hardest set in lifting.

items.sourceNote: 練完照鏡子 vs 隔天起床 身材幻覺崩塌 register

zh-tw.content:
剛練完，在健身房鏡子前：
哇，我手臂線條超明顯，胸肌微微浮起，腹肌好像有兩塊。

回家洗完澡，在浴室鏡子前：
哪一塊？

隔天早上起床，在房間鏡子前：
我練了什麼？

結論：肌肉幻覺的保鮮期是三小時。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合練完隔天起床、發現昨天的線條全部消失時的限動
variations: 備註：健身房鏡子有美肌，浴室鏡子有真相。
進階版：所以我都練完直接拍照存證，免得明天否認。

en.content:
Right after training, in the gym mirror:
Wow, my arms are popping, chest is lifting, I think I see two abs.

At home after the shower, in the bathroom mirror:
Which two?

Next morning in the bedroom mirror:
What exactly did I train?

Conclusion: muscle hallucinations have a three-hour shelf life.
en.editorNote: Post the morning after a session, the moment yesterday's definition has vanished
variations: Note: gym mirrors have a beauty filter. Bathroom mirrors have the truth.
Advanced: that's why I take photos right after lifting. So tomorrow-me can't deny it happened.

items.sourceNote: 深蹲失敗 卡在最低點 同伴未救援 真實尷尬 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
我深蹲卡在最低點那一秒，腦中閃過三件事：

一、我怎麼會選這個重量。
二、那個答應幫我保護的朋友，正在滑手機。
三、明天的我，會怎麼跟教練解釋這片瘀青。

最後我用屁股把槓滾下來，安全銷救了我一命，朋友抬頭說：你剛剛是不是有講話？
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合深蹲失敗、慢動作往下溜到安全銷那一刻拍下限動
variations: 備註：保護者最大的功能，是事後幫你錄影。
進階版：那天之後我學會：信安全銷，不要信朋友。

en.content:
The second I got pinned at the bottom of a squat, three thoughts flashed through my head:

One, why did I pick this weight.
Two, the friend who said he'd spot me is currently scrolling.
Three, how do I explain this bruise to my trainer tomorrow.

I rolled the bar off my back onto the safety pins. They saved my life. My friend looked up and said: 'Wait, did you say something?'
en.editorNote: Post the moment you slowly sink onto the safeties, mid-fail
variations: Note: a spotter's main function is filming the disaster afterward.
Advanced: that day I learned to trust the safety pins, not my friends.

items.sourceNote: 教練 IG 限動 vs 私下訊息 雙面人 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
教練的限動：「自律就是自由，每一滴汗都不會背叛你。」

教練傳給我的訊息：「你上週又沒來，我已經幫你補課三次了，再放鳥這個月就重排。」

他在 IG 上是哲學家，在 LINE 上是討債公司。
而我在兩邊都選擇已讀不回。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合被教練 LINE 催課當下，順手截他剛發的勵志限動發群組
variations: 結論：勵志名言的真正受眾，是教練自己。
備註：他越文青，我越心虛。

en.content:
My trainer's IG story: 'Discipline is freedom. Every drop of sweat pays you back.'

My trainer's DM to me: 'You missed last week again. I've rescheduled three times. One more no-show and we're restarting the month.'

On Instagram he's a philosopher. On WhatsApp he's a debt collector.
And I leave both on read.
en.editorNote: Best when your trainer is texting you about a missed session, while his motivational story is still up
variations: Conclusion: the real audience for motivational quotes is the trainer himself.
Note: the more poetic his caption, the guiltier I feel.

items.sourceNote: 健身房空調故障 全員崩潰 在地化 夏天 register

zh-tw.content:
健身房冷氣壞掉的那一天，

第一組：還行，當作熱身。
第二組：流汗了，毛巾擦一下。
第三組：水壺空了。
第四組：地墊上有汗水印。
第五組：我和槓鈴黏在一起。
第六組：放棄。

那天我才知道，原來我練的不是肌肉，是冷氣訂閱方案。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合夏天健身房冷氣壞掉那天、滿身汗坐在地上發限動
variations: 備註：冷氣壞掉那天我練得最少，喝水最多。
進階版：那天我向櫃台申訴，他們說『這也是訓練的一部分』。

en.content:
The day the gym's AC broke down:

Set one: fine, counts as warm-up.
Set two: sweating, towel time.
Set three: water bottle empty.
Set four: visible puddle on the mat.
Set five: I'm now physically fused to the barbell.
Set six: giving up.

That's the day I realized I wasn't paying for lifting. I was paying for an AC subscription.
en.editorNote: Post the day the gym AC dies, mid-puddle on the floor
variations: Note: the day the AC broke, I lifted the least and drank the most.
Advanced: I complained to the front desk. They said 'that's part of the training too'.

items.sourceNote: 新買健身手套 vs 真實使用率 衝動消費 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
我買的健身裝備清單：

護腕兩副、健身手套三雙、舉重腰帶、護膝、拉力帶、止滑粉、握力器、按摩槍、筋膜球。

真正用過超過三次的：止滑粉，因為瓶蓋打不開那一次。

結論：我練的不是身材，是裝備收藏家的天職。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合剛收到蝦皮包裹、打開又是健身裝備時的限動
variations: 備註：那把按摩槍現在被我媽拿去按肩膀，她比我用得勤。
進階版：下次我打算買健身打卡機，假裝我有來。

en.content:
My gym gear collection:

Two pairs of wrist wraps, three pairs of lifting gloves, a belt, knee sleeves, resistance bands, chalk, a grip strengthener, a massage gun, a lacrosse ball.

Used more than three times: the chalk, once, because the lid was stuck.

Conclusion: I'm not training. I'm fulfilling my destiny as a gear collector.
en.editorNote: Best after another Amazon delivery turns out to be more lifting gear
variations: Note: my mom now uses the massage gun for her shoulders. She gets more reps than I do.
Advanced: next purchase: a fake gym check-in machine, to prove I was there.

items.sourceNote: 暑假倒數 衝刺練胸 vs 練腿被遺忘 在地化

zh-tw.content:
距離暑假還有四週。

我的訓練計畫：
週一：練胸。
週二：練胸。
週三：胸有點痠，補練二頭。
週四：練胸。
週五：休息（因為要約會穿緊身衣）。
週六：練胸。
週日：拍鏡子。

所謂海邊身材，原來只練上半身。下半身穿短褲遮起來就好。
zh-tw.editorNote: 暑假前四週、每天只練上半身的時候用
variations: 結論：練腿是冬天的事。
備註：六月去海邊那天，我穿了長褲。沒人懷疑。

en.content:
Four weeks until summer.

My training plan:
Monday: chest.
Tuesday: chest.
Wednesday: chest is sore, bonus biceps.
Thursday: chest.
Friday: rest (date night, tight shirt).
Saturday: chest.
Sunday: mirror photos.

Turns out the beach body is just the top half. The bottom half is covered by shorts.
en.editorNote: Use four weeks out from summer when every session is upper body
variations: Conclusion: leg day is a winter problem.
Note: I went to the beach in June wearing pants. Nobody questioned it.

items.sourceNote: 練前量體重 vs 練後量體重 各種偏方 在地化 register

zh-tw.content:
我量體重的標準流程：

早上空腹、上完廁所、脫光、吐氣到底、單腳站。
結果：68.4 公斤。

中午吃飽、穿牛仔褲、口袋有手機、剛喝完水。
結果：72.1 公斤。

我選擇相信早上那個。
那才是真正的我，其他都是干擾項。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合每天早上量完體重、開始一天健身仇恨之前的限動
variations: 備註：體重計放在不同地板會有不同數字，我家有三個地點。
進階版：我會挑數字最好看那次截圖傳給教練。

en.content:
My official weigh-in protocol:

Morning, empty stomach, post-bathroom, fully naked, full exhale, standing on one foot.
Result: 150.8 lb.

After lunch, in jeans, phone in pocket, mid-water bottle.
Result: 159 lb.

I choose to believe the first one.
That's the real me. Everything else is noise.
en.editorNote: Post first thing after the morning weigh-in, before the day's gym hatred begins
variations: Note: the scale reads differently on different floor tiles. I have three approved locations.
Advanced: I screenshot the best one and send it to my trainer.

items.sourceNote: 健身房暗戀 parasocial 同時段固定出現 在地化 register

zh-tw.content:
健身房裡有一個我每週三、五、日固定會看到的人。

我們從來沒講過話。
我知道他練什麼分化、用哪台跑步機、喝什麼牌子的水。
他不知道我存在。

上週他換時段了，我整個禮拜的訓練都沒動力。
所謂的訓練伙伴，原來可以是單方面的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合健身房暗戀對象換時段消失那週、心情低落時發限動
variations: 備註：我換會員方案的時候，第一個考慮的是他會不會還在。
結論：健身房才是現代人最大的單戀現場。

en.content:
There's a person I see at the gym every Monday, Wednesday, and Friday.

We've never spoken.
I know their split, their preferred treadmill, the brand of water they drink.
They don't know I exist.

Last week they switched time slots. I had zero motivation all week.
Turns out a training partner can be entirely one-sided.
en.editorNote: Post the week your gym crush changes their schedule and your motivation collapses
variations: Note: when I renewed my membership, my first thought was whether they'd still be there.
Conclusion: the gym is the modern era's largest one-sided romance venue.

items.sourceNote: 滾筒按摩 痛到尖叫 vs 教練說放鬆 對比 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
教練：「滾一下大腿，放鬆筋膜。」

我趴上滾筒第一秒：發出我自己都不認識的聲音。
第二秒：眼淚是真的出來了。
第三秒：我跟旁邊阿姨對到眼，她以為我在哭。

教練：「就是這個感覺，要放鬆喔。」

放鬆？這明明是私刑。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合滾完筋膜痛到不行、躺在地墊上發抖時的限動
variations: 備註：滾筒越便宜，越不痛。我已經換到第四根，越來越貴。
進階版：滾完之後我才知道，原本那個叫做痠痛，不叫放鬆。

en.content:
Trainer: 'Just roll out your quads, it'll release the fascia.'

First second on the foam roller: I make a sound I don't recognize.
Second second: actual tears.
Third second: I make eye contact with an auntie who thinks I'm crying.

Trainer: 'Yes, that's exactly the feeling. Relax into it.'

Relax? This is clearly torture.
en.editorNote: Post mid-foam-roll, lying on the mat shaking
variations: Note: the cheaper the roller, the less it hurts. I've upgraded four times. They get worse.
Advanced: after rolling I realized what I'd been calling soreness was actually tension.

items.sourceNote: 「你 natty 嗎」健身房閒聊 真實尷尬 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
健身房閒聊三大絕對不要問的問題：

一、「你 natty 嗎？」——對方練五年，被你三秒否定。
二、「你練多久了？」——對方練三個月，會以為你在嘲諷。
三、「你今天練什麼？」——對方剛做完三組，會覺得你在驗收。

所以我每次走進健身房，只問一句話：「不好意思借過。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合健身房裡剛尷尬完一段對話、躲到飲水機旁時的限動
variations: 備註：第四個禁忌：問教練本身是不是 natty。
結論：健身房最安全的對話，是沒有對話。

en.content:
Three questions you must never ask at the gym:

One, 'Are you natty?' — they've trained five years, you've insulted them in three seconds.
Two, 'How long have you been lifting?' — they've trained three months, they think you're being sarcastic.
Three, 'What are you training today?' — they just finished three sets, they think you're quizzing them.

So every time I walk into the gym, I say one sentence: 'Excuse me, just passing through.'
en.editorNote: Post after a failed gym conversation, hiding by the water fountain
variations: Note: fourth taboo question — asking the trainer himself if he's natty.
Conclusion: the safest conversation at the gym is no conversation.

items.sourceNote: 早晨補劑儀式 七顆藥丸 vs 早餐沒吃 反差 register

zh-tw.content:
我的早晨儀式：

起床第一件事：吞綜合維他命、魚油、鎂、鋅、BCAA、肌酸、咖啡因錠。
第二件事：泡乳清。
第三件事：泡咖啡。
第四件事：出門。

早餐：忘了。

結論：我每天早上吃的不是飯，是元素週期表。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合早上一邊吞七顆藥丸、一邊發現自己沒吃早餐的限動
variations: 備註：我藥盒比我便當盒還滿。
進階版：那天我忘記吃補劑，整天感覺人生失控。

en.content:
My morning ritual:

First thing after waking: multivitamin, fish oil, magnesium, zinc, BCAAs, creatine, caffeine pill.
Second: shake the whey.
Third: brew the coffee.
Fourth: leave the house.

Breakfast: forgot.

Conclusion: I don't eat breakfast. I consume the periodic table.
en.editorNote: Post while swallowing seven pills and realizing you skipped breakfast
variations: Note: my pill organizer holds more than my lunchbox.
Advanced: the day I forgot my stack, I felt my life unraveling by noon.

items.sourceNote: 「我不做有氧」經典 cardio 逃避梗 在地化 register

zh-tw.content:
我不是不做有氧。
我只是把它無限期延後。

跑步機：當衣架。
飛輪：當置物椅。
划船機：唯一去過一次，因為以為可以坐著。

結論：我跟有氧的關係，是一種純柏拉圖式的精神交流。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合走過有氧區、直接右轉去重訓區那一刻發限動
variations: 備註：我的心肺功能，建立在爬樓梯回家忘記搭電梯那幾次。
進階版：那天教練說今天練有氧，我突然想起家裡瓦斯沒關。

en.content:
It's not that I don't do cardio.
I just postpone it indefinitely.

Treadmill: a coat rack.
Spin bike: a place to put my bag.
Rowing machine: visited once, because I thought I'd be sitting.

Conclusion: my relationship with cardio is purely platonic.
en.editorNote: Post the moment you walk past the cardio zone and hard-right into the weights
variations: Note: my entire cardiovascular base comes from the times I forgot the elevator and took the stairs.
Advanced: the day coach said 'cardio today', I suddenly remembered I left the stove on.

items.sourceNote: 肌酸記得吃 vs 該記的事都忘 反差 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
我會忘記繳水電、忘記回媽媽訊息、忘記同事的名字、忘記今天禮拜幾。

但我從來沒有，一次都沒有，忘記吃肌酸。

結論：我的記憶體不夠用，但有一塊永久分配給了五公克的白色粉末。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合月底被催繳帳單、卻準時泡好肌酸時的限動
variations: 備註：我手機沒設鬧鐘提醒繳卡費，但肌酸的提醒響三次。
進階版：朋友問我幾號，我說不知道，但我知道我這罐肌酸還能喝十二天。

en.content:
I forget to pay bills, forget to text my mom back, forget coworkers' names, forget what day it is.

But never, not once, have I forgotten my creatine.

Conclusion: my memory is full, but one slot is permanently allocated to five grams of white powder.
en.editorNote: Post when a bill reminder hits at month-end but your creatine is already scooped on time
variations: Note: no alarm for my credit card, but the creatine reminder goes off three times.
Advanced: a friend asked the date. I didn't know. But I know this tub lasts twelve more days.

items.sourceNote: 「賺到休息日」激勵語 vs 連續休息現實 反諷 register

zh-tw.content:
牆上海報寫：「休息日是賺來的。」

我看著它，深受感動，
決定先休息，賺了再說。

於是我提前預支了整個五月的休息日，
額度透支到八月。

結論：我不是不努力，我只是太會理財。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合連續第五天沒去健身房、卻路過健身房海報時的限動
variations: 備註：我的休息日是循環信用，利息很高，但我還不出來。
進階版：教練問我什麼時候回來，我說等我把這些賺來的休息日用完。

en.content:
The poster on the wall says: 'Rest days are earned.'

I looked at it, deeply moved,
and decided to rest first, earn it later.

So I borrowed all of May's rest days in advance,
overdrawing the balance into August.

Conclusion: I'm not lazy. I'm just great at managing credit.
en.editorNote: Post on your fifth straight skip day, right as you walk past the gym poster
variations: Note: my rest days are a revolving credit line. High interest. Can't pay it off.
Advanced: coach asked when I'm back. I said once I use up the rest days I've earned.

items.sourceNote: 暑假倒數 vs 一年的拖延帳單 在地化 register

zh-tw.content:
距離暑假還有 30 天。
我的健身計畫進度：12%。
其中 10% 是辦會員卡，2% 是買健身服。

剩下的 88%，我打算用三招解決：
顯瘦穿搭、吸氣、以及不要去海邊。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合六月初翻日曆、突然意識到暑假快到時發限動
variations: 備註：我的夏季備戰策略，核心是『相機角度』。
進階版：今年我決定改練心態，接受自己原本的樣子。比較快。

en.content:
30 days until summer.
My fitness plan progress: 12%.
Of which 10% was signing up, and 2% was buying the outfit.

The remaining 88%, I'll handle with three moves:
slimming outfits, sucking it in, and not going to the beach.
en.editorNote: Post in early June when you flip the calendar and realize summer is almost here
variations: Note: my summer prep strategy is built entirely around camera angles.
Advanced: this year I'm training my mindset instead and accepting myself. It's faster.

items.sourceNote: 咖啡才是真正的 pre-workout 反差 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
大家都說 pre-workout 讓人爆發。

但我練前真正喝的：一杯美式。
練到一半喝的：手搖的冰拿鐵。
練完喝的：第二杯美式，慶祝自己有來。

所謂補劑功效，我用四十元便利商店咖啡就達標了。
而且不會心悸，只會手抖一點點。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合練前在便利商店買咖啡、發現自己根本沒買補劑時發限動
variations: 備註：我的爆發力來源，標示在咖啡杯側邊。
進階版：那天咖啡機壞了，我整場練得像在夢遊。

en.content:
Everyone swears pre-workout makes you explode.

What I actually drink before lifting: a black coffee.
Mid-workout: an iced latte.
After: a second coffee, to celebrate showing up.

Whatever pre-workout does, I hit it with a $2 convenience-store cup.
No heart palpitations. Just a slight hand tremor.
en.editorNote: Post while grabbing coffee before the gym and realizing you never bought actual pre-workout
variations: Note: my source of explosive power is printed on the side of a coffee cup.
Advanced: the day the coffee machine broke, I trained like a sleepwalker.

items.sourceNote: 練腿隔天走路像新生小鹿 在地化 DOMS register

zh-tw.content:
練腿隔天，我重新學會走路。

下樓梯：手扶牆，一階停三秒，像在拆炸彈。
坐下：不是坐，是自由落體。
站起來：需要桌子、扶手、跟一段心理準備。
捷運博愛座：我嚴重考慮了一下自己有沒有資格。

那天我才明白，原來雙腿是借來的，練腿就是還利息。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合練腿後隔天上班、扶著捷運欄杆一拐一拐時發限動
variations: 備註：那兩天我下樓梯都用倒退的，比較不痛。
進階版：同事問我是不是扭到，我說沒有，我是自願的。

en.content:
The day after leg day, I relearn how to walk.

Down the stairs: hand on the wall, three seconds per step, like defusing a bomb.
Sitting down: not sitting, free-falling.
Standing up: requires a table, a railing, and a moment of mental preparation.
The priority seat on the train: I genuinely considered whether I qualified.

That's when I understood: legs are borrowed. Leg day is paying the interest.
en.editorNote: Post the morning after leg day, hobbling down the train platform clutching the rail
variations: Note: for two days I took the stairs backwards. Hurts less.
Advanced: a coworker asked if I sprained something. I said no, this was voluntary.

items.sourceNote: 啞鈴 = 沉默的評審 擬人化 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
啞鈴是健身房裡最沉默的評審。

你拿起 8 公斤，它沒說話。
你換 12 公斤，做兩下放回去，它還是沒說話。
你偷偷瞄一眼 16 公斤，它什麼都沒做。

但你心裡知道，它什麼都看到了。
它不會評分，它只會讓你自己給自己打零分。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在啞鈴區換來換去、最後拿了最輕那組時的自嘲限動
variations: 備註：最重的不是啞鈴，是放回去那一刻的自尊。
進階版：我跟 16 公斤對望了三秒，然後我們都選擇尊重彼此。

en.content:
Dumbbells are the quietest judges in the gym.

You pick up the 8s, they say nothing.
You grab the 12s, do two reps, put them back. Still nothing.
You sneak a glance at the 16s. They do nothing at all.

But you know they saw everything.
They don't score you. They just let you give yourself a zero.
en.editorNote: Post after wandering the dumbbell rack and settling on the lightest pair, with full self-mockery
variations: Note: the heaviest thing isn't the dumbbell. It's your ego the moment you re-rack it.
Advanced: the 16s and I locked eyes for three seconds, then mutually agreed to respect each other.

items.sourceNote: 教練加槓片像 Oprah 送車 在地化 register

zh-tw.content:
我教練加槓片的樣子，像在發年終獎金。

「你一片！你也一片！這邊兩片！」
笑得很燦爛，手很勤快，眼神充滿愛。

問題是，獎金是我用脊椎領的。
而且沒得退，沒得拒絕，連『謝謝不用』都不行。

結論：教練的慷慨，是我隔天的痠痛預算。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合被教練無預警加重、深蹲到懷疑人生那一刻發限動
variations: 備註：他加重的時候從來不問我，只通知我。
進階版：我說『這樣會不會太重』，他說『相信我』，那是最危險的三個字。

en.content:
The way my trainer adds plates, it's like he's handing out year-end bonuses.

'A plate for you! A plate for you! Two on this side!'
Big smile, busy hands, eyes full of love.

Problem is, I collect the bonus with my spine.
No refunds, no opting out, not even a 'thanks, I'm good'.

Conclusion: my trainer's generosity is tomorrow's soreness budget.
en.editorNote: Post the moment your trainer loads more plates unannounced and you question your life under the bar
variations: Note: he never asks before adding weight. He just notifies me.
Advanced: I said 'isn't this too heavy?' He said 'trust me'. The three most dangerous words.

items.sourceNote: 保護者只負責事後錄影 沒在看 在地化 register

zh-tw.content:
我請朋友幫我臥推保護。

第一組：他認真扶著，眼神專注。
第二組：他開始滑手機。
第三組：槓壓在我胸口，我發出求救聲。
他抬頭：「喔等等，我先把這則限動發完。」

結論：所謂保護者，是一個離你最近、但救援最慢的旁觀者。
他唯一準時的時刻，是事後幫你錄影。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合臥推被槓壓住、抬頭發現朋友在滑手機那一刻發限動
variations: 備註：下次我寧願信安全架，至少它不會已讀不回。
進階版：他事後說『你剛剛聲音好好笑，我有錄下來』。謝謝喔。

en.content:
I asked a friend to spot my bench press.

Set one: hands ready, fully focused.
Set two: he starts scrolling his phone.
Set three: the bar is on my chest, I let out a distress signal.
He looks up: 'Oh hold on, let me finish posting this story.'

Conclusion: a spotter is the person closest to you and slowest to help.
The one moment he's on time is filming the disaster afterward.
en.editorNote: Post the second the bar pins you and you look up to find your friend mid-scroll
variations: Note: next time I'll trust the safety arms. At least they don't leave me on read.
Advanced: afterward he said 'the noise you made was hilarious, I got it on video'. Thanks.

items.sourceNote: 「abs loading please wait」bulking 永遠看不到腹肌 在地化 register

zh-tw.content:
我從去年冬天開始增肌。
教練說：「先把體重衝上去，腹肌之後再說。」

於是我衝了體重，衝了食量，衝了一整圈肚子。
腹肌的進度條，到現在還停在「載入中，請稍候」。

距離暑假 30 天，我決定相信它會在最後一刻讀取完成。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合暑假前一個月、低頭看自己增肌成果時的限動，配一張側面照
variations: 備註：增肌期是有期限的，但我的好像辦了無限續約。
進階版：教練說『再增最後一個月』，我聽了一年。

en.content:
I started bulking last winter.
Trainer: 'Get your weight up first, we'll find the abs later.'

So I got my weight up, my appetite up, and a whole belly up.
The abs progress bar still says 'loading, please wait'.

30 days to summer, and I've decided to believe it finishes loading at the last second.
en.editorNote: Post a month before summer while looking down at your bulk, paired with a side shot
variations: Note: a bulk is supposed to have an end date. Mine signed up for unlimited renewal.
Advanced: he said 'one final month of bulking'. I've heard that for a year.

items.sourceNote: 三溫暖 phone 進蒸氣室 信任崩潰 sauna etiquette 在地化

zh-tw.content:
蒸氣室裡的不成文規矩：

有人帶手機進來：信任瞬間歸零。
有人講電話：全場用眼神開公審。
有人每次蒸氣噴出來都「嘶——」一聲：先生，這裡不是健身區。
跟陌生人對到眼：好，我們現在是過命之交了。

而我能做的，只有閉上眼睛，假裝自己在很貴的飯店，而不是月費八百的健身房。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合三溫暖蒸完出來、被剛才那位講電話的人氣到時發限動
variations: 備註：蒸氣室最大的潛規則，是不要跟在角落快蒸熟的人講話。
進階版：那天有人帶平板進來追劇，我直接起立離場以示抗議。

en.content:
The unwritten rules of the steam room:

Someone brings a phone in: trust drops to zero instantly.
Someone takes a call: the whole room holds a silent tribunal.
Someone goes 'sssss' every time the steam hisses: sir, this is not the weight floor.
Accidental eye contact with a stranger: great, we're bonded for life now.

All I can do is close my eyes and pretend I'm at an expensive hotel, not a gym with an $25 monthly fee.
en.editorNote: Post after stepping out of the steam room, still annoyed at the guy who took a call inside
variations: Note: the biggest unspoken rule is never talk to the person clearly cooking in the corner.
Advanced: someone brought a tablet in to binge a show. I stood up and left in protest.

items.sourceNote: 重訓最後一下故意大聲放下槓 表演性 在地化 register

zh-tw.content:
健身房裡放下槓鈴的聲音，是一種社交語言。

輕輕放下：低調，沒人知道你練多重。
「咚」一聲：嗯，這位有練。
「砰——」整個地板震動：全場回頭，他想讓你知道他剛破 PR。

而我那一聲，是因為我手真的握不住了。
但全場以為我很猛。

結論：在健身房，意外跟實力，聽起來一模一樣。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合硬舉最後一下沒抓好、槓砸地全場回頭那一刻發限動
variations: 備註：我那天的『氣勢』，其實是握力先投降了。
進階版：旁邊大哥對我點頭致意，我也只好假裝那是故意的。

en.content:
The sound of dropping a barbell at the gym is a social language.

Lowered gently: humble, nobody knows your numbers.
A solid 'thud': okay, this one lifts.
A floor-shaking 'BOOM': everyone turns, he wants you to know he just hit a PR.

My boom was because my grip genuinely gave out.
But the whole room thought I was a beast.

Conclusion: at the gym, an accident and real strength sound exactly the same.
en.editorNote: Post the moment your last deadlift rep slips and the bar slams, turning every head
variations: Note: my 'intensity' that day was actually my grip surrendering first.
Advanced: a big guy nodded at me in respect. I had no choice but to pretend it was on purpose.

items.sourceNote: 毛巾佔器材 vs 真人不在 健身房佔位文化 在地化 register

zh-tw.content:
健身房的佔位術，是一門看不見的房地產。

一條毛巾搭在臥推椅上：這台有主人，請繞道。
一瓶水放在滑輪機旁：簽約中，勿擾。
一支手機加耳機在器材上：豪宅自住，永不釋出。

而那位地主，我找了二十分鐘，最後在飲水機旁滑手機那群人裡找到他。

結論：在健身房，毛巾的產權，比人還持久。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合想用某台器材、卻被一條無人毛巾擋了二十分鐘時發限動
variations: 備註：我也想用毛巾圈地，但我怕被收走，沒那個膽。
進階版：那條毛巾從我進來放到我走，全程沒看過主人本人。

en.content:
Reserving equipment at the gym is invisible real estate.

A towel draped on the bench: this one's taken, please detour.
A water bottle by the cable machine: under contract, do not disturb.
A phone with headphones on the seat: owner-occupied mansion, never for sale.

I looked for the landlord for twenty minutes. Found him eventually, in the group scrolling by the water fountain.

Conclusion: at the gym, a towel's property rights outlast the person.
en.editorNote: Post when a single unattended towel blocks the machine you want for twenty minutes
variations: Note: I'd claim territory with a towel too, but I'm too scared someone would take it.
Advanced: that towel was there before I arrived and after I left. Never met the owner.

items.sourceNote: 暑假倒數 cut 期 飲食控制 vs 朋友揪宵夜 在地化 register

zh-tw.content:
暑假前一個月，我終於開始減脂。

禮拜一：水煮餐，意志堅定。
禮拜二：水煮餐，但偷加了醬。
禮拜三：朋友傳「鹹酥雞？」
禮拜四：我說「就這一次。」
禮拜五：我說「週末本來就要放鬆。」
禮拜六：減脂計畫已重新排程至暑假後。

結論：我的減脂期，比我的感情還短。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合減脂第一週就破功、被朋友揪宵夜的隔天發限動
variations: 備註：我每年都在暑假前一個月開始減脂，每年都失敗在第三天。
進階版：今年我提早到暑假前三個月開始，這樣破功的時間也比較從容。

en.content:
A month before summer, I finally started cutting.

Monday: boiled meals, iron will.
Tuesday: boiled meals, but I snuck in some sauce.
Wednesday: a friend texts 'fried chicken?'
Thursday: I say 'just this once.'
Friday: I say 'weekends are for relaxing anyway.'
Saturday: the cut has been rescheduled to after summer.

Conclusion: my cut is shorter than my relationships.
en.editorNote: Post the day after your week-one cut collapses to a friend's late-night snack invite
variations: Note: every year I start cutting a month before summer. Every year it dies on day three.
Advanced: this year I started three months early, so I'd have more time to fail gracefully.

items.sourceNote: 暖身被跳過 vs 訓練後才想起 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
教練：練之前一定要暖身。
我：好。

然後我直接上重量、直接深蹲、直接硬舉，
暖身？等我受傷的時候自然會想起來。

結論：我不是不暖身，我是把暖身排在復健科那一天。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合沒暖身就直接練、隔天腰卡住坐在沙發上發限動時用
variations: 備註：我的暖身菜表，目前只存在於教練的想像中。
進階版：受傷那天我終於開始認真暖身，但已經來不及了。

en.content:
Trainer: Always warm up before lifting.
Me: Got it.

Then I load the bar, squat cold, deadlift cold,
and warm up? I'll remember it the moment I get hurt.

Conclusion: it's not that I skip the warm-up. I just scheduled it for the day I see a physiotherapist.
en.editorNote: Best when you lifted cold and your back locks up the next day on the couch
variations: Note: my warm-up routine currently exists only in my trainer's imagination.
Advanced: the day I got hurt, I finally started warming up properly. Too late.

items.sourceNote: 滾筒放鬆 痛到飆淚 vs 假裝很享受 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
滾筒放鬆，是健身房裡唯一一個你會痛到飆淚、但要假裝很享受的環節。

滾到大腿外側那一下，我發出的聲音，連隔壁練腿的大哥都回頭關心。

教練說：會痛代表你很緊。
我說：那我整個人應該緊到可以當吊橋。

結論：滾筒不是放鬆，是付費的酷刑。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合滾筒滾到大腿外側、痛到表情扭曲那一刻發限動
variations: 備註：滾筒越痛我越罪惡，因為代表我平常多不放鬆。
進階版：那天我滾完哭了，旁邊的人以為我剛失戀。

en.content:
Foam rolling is the one thing at the gym where you cry from pain but have to pretend you're enjoying it.

When I hit the outer thigh, the sound I made got the guy on leg day to turn around and check on me.

Trainer: It hurts because you're tight.
Me: Then I'm tight enough to function as a suspension bridge.

Conclusion: foam rolling isn't recovery. It's torture you paid for.
en.editorNote: Best the moment you roll the outer thigh and your face contorts in pain
variations: Note: the more it hurts, the guiltier I feel about how tight I normally am.
Advanced: I cried after rolling, and the guy next to me assumed I'd just been dumped.

items.sourceNote: 進度條 vs gains 等很久 不耐煩 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
如果肌肉成長有進度條，我每天都想盯著它看。

練第一個月：進度條 2%。
練第三個月：進度條 5%，還在緩衝。
吃一次宵夜：進度條 2%。
睡不好一週：進度條重新讀取中。

結論：別人的進度條用 5G，我的還在用撥接。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合練了三個月照鏡子覺得沒變、又安慰自己時發限動
variations: 備註：我的 gains 不是沒來，是塞在路上，目前預計時間未知。
進階版：那天我吃了一整桶冰淇淋，進度條直接顯示『連線逾時』。

en.content:
If muscle growth had a progress bar, I'd stare at it all day.

Month one: bar at 2%.
Month three: bar at 5%, still buffering.
One late-night snack: back to 2%.
A week of bad sleep: bar reloading.

Conclusion: other people's progress bars run on 5G. Mine is still on dial-up.
en.editorNote: Best at the three-month mark when the mirror looks the same and you talk yourself off the ledge
variations: Note: my gains aren't missing. They're stuck in traffic. ETA unknown.
Advanced: I ate a whole tub of ice cream and the bar just showed 'connection timed out'.

items.sourceNote: 教練放假代課老師 紀律全面崩潰 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
我的紀律，是租來的，房東是我的教練。

教練在：我準時、我加重、我不偷懶、我連最後一下都做完。
教練放假：我熱身十分鐘、滑手機二十分鐘、做兩組就說今天感覺不對。
教練傳訊息問近況：我立刻回「都有照表操課」。

結論：我練的不是自律，是有人盯著我的羞恥心。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合教練放假那週、自己一個人去健身房廢掉時發限動
variations: 備註：教練回來的前一天，我會臨時抱佛腳補練，像考前一晚。
進階版：那週我唯一的訓練，是練習怎麼跟教練解釋我為什麼沒練。

en.content:
My discipline is rented. My landlord is my trainer.

Trainer present: on time, going heavier, no slacking, finishing every last rep.
Trainer on vacation: ten minutes of warm-up, twenty minutes of scrolling, two sets and 'today just doesn't feel right.'
Trainer texts to check in: I instantly reply 'stuck to the program, all good.'

Conclusion: I'm not training self-discipline. I'm training the shame of being watched.
en.editorNote: Best the week your trainer is away and you fall apart at the gym alone
variations: Note: the day before my trainer returns, I cram a make-up session like it's the night before an exam.
Advanced: that week, my only training was rehearsing how to explain why I didn't train.

items.sourceNote: 「喝一次蛋白就期待長肌肉」新手不耐煩 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
我喝完第一杯乳清，站在鏡子前等了三十秒。

沒長肌肉。
我以為是杯子的問題，又喝了一杯。
還是沒長。

後來我才知道，乳清不是即溶咖啡，
不會喝下去十分鐘就見效。
它比較像分期付款，而且我還沒繳完頭期。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合剛買第一罐乳清、興奮喝完後對著鏡子失望那一刻發限動
variations: 備註：那罐乳清喝完了，肌肉沒來，但我的味覺記住了巧克力口味。
進階版：第二罐喝到一半我才接受，原來努力的不是杯子，是我。

en.content:
I finished my first protein shake and stood in front of the mirror for thirty seconds.

No muscle.
I assumed it was the cup, so I made another.
Still nothing.

Turns out whey isn't instant coffee.
It doesn't kick in ten minutes after you drink it.
It's more like an installment plan, and I haven't even cleared the down payment.
en.editorNote: Best the moment you finish your first-ever tub's first scoop and the mirror disappoints you
variations: Note: the tub is empty, the muscle never came, but my taste buds memorized the chocolate flavor.
Advanced: halfway through tub two, I accepted it. The cup wasn't the problem. I was.

items.sourceNote: 練腿後企鵝走路 vs 假裝正常 反差 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
練腿隔天，最難的不是走路，
是要假裝走路很正常。

下樓梯：我扶著牆，像在拆炸彈。
坐捷運：站起來那一刻表情管理全面失守。
同事問：你還好嗎？
我：沒事，舊傷。

所謂舊傷，就是昨天的我，對今天的我犯下的傷害。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合練腿隔天在公司像企鵝一樣走路、被同事關心時發限動
variations: 備註：練腿日真正的考驗，是隔天的馬桶與沙發。
進階版：我已經練到可以面無表情下樓梯，這也是一種進步。

en.content:
The day after leg day, the hard part isn't walking.
It's pretending the walking is normal.

Going downstairs: I cling to the wall like I'm defusing a bomb.
On the train: standing up wipes out all my facial composure.
Coworker: 'You okay?'
Me: 'Yeah, old injury.'

The 'old injury' is the damage yesterday's me did to today's me.
en.editorNote: Best the day after leg day when you're penguin-walking around the office and a coworker checks in
variations: Note: the real test of leg day is the next morning's toilet and couch.
Advanced: I can now descend stairs with a straight face. I count that as progress.

items.sourceNote: 「組間擺 pose 等不存在的攝影師」自戀 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
我發現自己組間休息時，
會對著鏡子擺 pose，
好像在等一個永遠不會來的攝影師。

轉個角度看背、收個小腹看腹肌、抬個下巴看下顎線，
然後一個路人走過，我立刻假裝在調整護腕。

結論：我練的不是肌肉，是隨時可以收工的鏡頭感。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合組間對鏡擺 pose 被人撞見、尷尬轉身那一刻發限動
variations: 備註：那個攝影師不存在，但我的腹肌也是。
進階版：被撞見那次，我假裝在看手機裡的菜表，其實在看自己的限動。

en.content:
I caught myself posing in the mirror between sets,
like I'm waiting for a photographer who is never coming.

Angle the back, suck in for the abs, lift the chin for the jawline,
then a stranger walks by and I instantly pretend to adjust my wrist wraps.

Conclusion: I'm not training muscle. I'm training a camera presence I can drop on cue.
en.editorNote: Best the moment someone catches you posing between sets and you spin around in embarrassment
variations: Note: the photographer doesn't exist. Neither do the abs.
Advanced: when caught, I pretended to check my program. I was actually checking my own story.

items.sourceNote: 別人不擦汗的器材 + 不收槓片 禮儀崩壞 觀察 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
我走到臥推椅，坐下去那一刻，
屁股傳來一陣溫熱的潮濕——是上一位的汗。

我站起來，環顧四周：
槓還掛著兩片二十，沒人收。
啞鈴散在地上，像案發現場。
毛巾就在他手邊，但他選擇用整張椅子當毛巾。

健身房真正需要的，不是更重的槓，是基本的羞恥心。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合坐到別人汗濕的椅子、忍不住起身那一刻發限動，配一張濕掉的椅墊
variations: 備註：我擦的不只是別人的汗，還有對人性的期待。
進階版：我幫他收了槓片，不是因為善良，是因為我下一組要用。

en.content:
I walked up to the bench, sat down, and the moment I did,
a warm dampness greeted me — the last guy's sweat.

I stood up and looked around:
two 45s still loaded, unre-racked.
Dumbbells scattered on the floor like a crime scene.
His towel was right there, but he chose to use the entire bench as one.

What the gym really needs isn't heavier weights. It's a basic sense of shame.
en.editorNote: Best the moment you sit in someone's sweat and shoot back up, paired with a shot of the damp pad
variations: Note: I wiped off more than his sweat. I wiped off my faith in humanity.
Advanced: I re-racked his plates. Not out of kindness — I needed them for my next set.

items.sourceNote: 「abs are loading」bulking 永遠看不到腹肌 反諷 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
我增肌增了八個月。

別人問：腹肌呢？
我說：在載入中。

進度條卡在 1%，背景一直跑「請稍候」，
而我每天多吃三餐幫它加速。

結論：增肌季沒有終點，只有不斷往上調的腰圍。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合增肌期褲子變緊那天，配一張只看得到肚子的鏡子自拍
variations: 備註：教練說『再增一點』，我聽成『再買一條褲子』。
進階版：我的腹肌一直在載入，但我懷疑伺服器根本沒開機。

en.content:
Eight months into my bulk.

Someone asks: where are the abs?
Me: they're loading.

Progress bar stuck at 1%, 'please wait' spinning in the background,
and I add three extra meals a day to speed it up.

Conclusion: bulking season has no finish line. Just a waistband that keeps adjusting up.
en.editorNote: Best on the day your bulk-season jeans stop buttoning, paired with a mirror selfie that's mostly belly
variations: Note: trainer said 'add a little more'. I heard 'buy a bigger pair of pants'.
Advanced: my abs are still loading, but I suspect the server was never turned on.

items.sourceNote: spotter「需要幫忙嗎」vs「需要的是活下去的意志」 經典 spotter 梗 在地化

zh-tw.content:
臥推到第八下，槓壓在胸口下不來。

補手俯下身，溫柔地問：「需要幫忙嗎？」
我擠出最後一口氣：「需要，我需要活下去的意志。」

他幫我抬起槓，說：「下次別逞強。」
我說：「逞強的不是我，是我發完限動才上場的自尊。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合臥推卡關被補手救起那一刻、躺在椅上喘氣時發限動
variations: 備註：好的補手不是抬槓，是抬你的尊嚴。
進階版：那天沒補手，我用滾的把槓滾下肚子，這招叫『臥推自救法』。

en.content:
Rep eight of bench, and the bar is pinned to my chest.

My spotter leans over and gently asks, 'Need some help?'
I wheeze out my last breath: 'Yeah. I need the will to live.'

He lifts the bar and says, 'Don't push it next time.'
I say, 'It wasn't me pushing it. It was the ego I built after posting the story.'
en.editorNote: Best the moment a spotter rescues you mid-bench, posted while gasping on the bench
variations: Note: a good spotter doesn't lift the bar. He lifts your dignity.
Advanced: no spotter that day, so I rolled the bar down my stomach. They call it 'the bench escape protocol'.

items.sourceNote: foam roller = 中世紀刑具 自願受刑 反諷 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
滾筒放鬆，名字很溫柔，過程很中世紀。

我躺上去那一刻，發出一種連我自己都不認識的聲音。
大腿外側滾過去：我看見了我的祖先。
臀部滾過去：我跟身體道歉了三次。

結論：這世上唯一我會自願花錢買來折磨自己的東西，就是它。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合練完用滾筒放鬆、痛到表情扭曲那一刻發限動，配一張滾筒特寫
variations: 備註：滾筒不是放鬆工具，是一台手動的痛苦點播機。
進階版：教練說『會痛代表你需要』，那我整個人都需要。

en.content:
Foam rolling. The name is gentle. The process is medieval.

The moment I lie down on it, I make a sound I don't recognize as my own.
Rolling the outer thigh: I saw my ancestors.
Rolling the glutes: I apologized to my body three times.

Conclusion: it's the only thing on earth I voluntarily pay money to torture myself with.
en.editorNote: Best while foam rolling post-workout with your face contorted, paired with a close-up of the roller
variations: Note: a foam roller isn't a recovery tool. It's a manual pain-on-demand machine.
Advanced: trainer said 'if it hurts, you need it'. So apparently I need all of it.

items.sourceNote: pre-workout 高峰過後 突然斷電 crash register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
pre-workout 的故事分三幕。

第一幕：上頭十分鐘，我覺得我能舉起整間健身房。
第二幕：高峰四十分鐘，每組都像在拍電影。
第三幕：訓練做完，回到車上，引擎打開，斷電。

我在停車場坐了二十分鐘，看著方向盤，思考人生。
那不是低潮，那是 pre-workout 的帳單。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合練完坐在車上突然斷電、動不了那一刻發限動
variations: 備註：pre-workout 借給你的力氣，是要連本帶利還的。
進階版：那天我在停車場睡著，醒來健身房都關了。

en.content:
Pre-workout is a three-act play.

Act one: ten minutes in, I could lift the entire gym.
Act two: forty minutes of peak, every set feels like a movie.
Act three: workout done, back in the car, engine on, power cut.

I sat in the parking lot for twenty minutes, staring at the wheel, contemplating my life.
That wasn't a low point. That was the pre-workout bill arriving.
en.editorNote: Best the moment you sit in the car post-workout and the crash hits, unable to move
variations: Note: the energy pre-workout lends you comes back due, with interest.
Advanced: I fell asleep in the lot that day. Woke up and the gym had closed.

items.sourceNote: 夏天減脂 vs 入秋增肌 永動循環 在地化 register

zh-tw.content:
六月：我要減脂，夏天要去海邊。
七月：減脂中，但聚餐有點多。
八月：海邊去了，穿了 T 恤。
九月：天氣涼了，該增肌了。
十月：增肌增很順，因為什麼都吃。

隔年六月：我要減脂，夏天要去海邊。

我練的不是身材，是一個沒有出口的迴圈。
zh-tw.editorNote: 暑假前傳給每年都在同一個循環裡打轉的健身房戰友
variations: 備註：我的減脂期跟增肌期，唯一的差別是藉口不一樣。
進階版：今年我決定跳過減脂，直接進入永久增肌，這樣比較誠實。

en.content:
June: I'm cutting, beach season's coming.
July: still cutting, but the dinners keep adding up.
August: made it to the beach. Kept the shirt on.
September: getting cooler, time to bulk.
October: bulk going great, because I'm eating everything.

Next June: I'm cutting, beach season's coming.

I'm not building a body. I'm running a loop with no exit.
en.editorNote: Send before summer to the gym buddy stuck in the same annual cycle as you
variations: Note: the only difference between my cut and my bulk is the excuse.
Advanced: this year I'm skipping the cut and going permanent bulk. It's just more honest.

items.sourceNote: 滾筒放鬆 = 自費酷刑 反諷 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
教練說：滾筒放鬆對身體很好。

於是我趴在滾筒上，壓到大腿外側的那一刻，
發出了我這輩子最真實的聲音。

旁邊的阿姨以為我在生孩子。

結論：所謂放鬆，就是我付費讓自己痛到流淚，然後說好爽。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合滾完髂脛束、癱在地墊上喘氣那一刻發限動，配一張滾筒特寫
variations: 備註：滾筒不是放鬆工具，是合法的自我懲罰。
進階版：真正的勇者，是滾到痛點還能保持微笑的那種人。我不是。

en.content:
Trainer: Foam rolling is great for recovery.

So I lay on the roller, hit the side of my quad,
and made the most honest sound of my entire life.

The lady next to me thought I was giving birth.

Conclusion: 'recovery' means I pay money to hurt myself until I cry, then say it feels great.
en.editorNote: Post the moment you collapse off the roller after your IT band, paired with a close-up of the roller
variations: Note: a foam roller isn't a recovery tool. It's legal self-punishment.
Advanced: the real warriors smile through the trigger point. I am not one of them.

items.sourceNote: 幫忙 spot 的人出力比舉的人還多 反諷 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
我請陌生人幫我 spot 臥推。

第一下：他扶著，我推。
第二下：他出一點力，我推。
第三下：他兩隻手往上拉，嘴裡喊「是你的、是你的」。

那不是我推的，是我們一起完成的雙人項目。

結論：我的 PR，有一半是別人的握力。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合請人 spot 後、勉強完成最後一下時發限動，搭配臥推槓的照片
variations: 備註：他喊『是你的』的時候，其實是他扛起來的。
進階版：真正的 spotter，是讓你以為自己很強的那種善良的人。

en.content:
I asked a stranger to spot my bench.

Rep one: he just holds it, I push.
Rep two: he adds a little, I push.
Rep three: both hands pulling up, yelling 'it's all you, all you.'

That wasn't my rep. That was a team event.

Conclusion: half my PR is somebody else's grip strength.
en.editorNote: Post after barely grinding out the last rep with a spotter, paired with a shot of the bench bar
variations: Note: when he yells 'it's all you', it was actually all him.
Advanced: a true spotter is a kind person who lets you believe you're strong.

items.sourceNote: 智慧手錶把日常動作算成運動 反諷 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
我的智慧手錶今天恭喜我：

解開纏在一起的耳機線：心率飆升，記錄為一場有氧。
從沙發站起來找遙控器：完成今日活動目標。
搬一箱蝦皮上樓：判定為重量訓練。

它說：今天運動表現超棒！

謝謝你，是我這週唯一相信我有在運動的存在。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合一整天沒進健身房、卻被手錶誇獎時截圖發限動
variations: 備註：我手錶比我媽還會鼓勵我。
進階版：它把我半夜起來喝水也算進步數，我選擇接受這份善意。

en.content:
My smartwatch congratulated me today:

Untangling my earbuds: heart rate spike, logged as cardio.
Standing up off the couch to find the remote: daily activity goal hit.
Carrying one Amazon box upstairs: classified as strength training.

It says: Amazing workout performance today!

Thank you. You're the only thing this week that believes I exercise.
en.editorNote: Screenshot and post when your watch praises you after a day you never touched the gym
variations: Note: my watch is more encouraging than my mom.
Advanced: it counted my midnight trip to the kitchen as progress. I accept this kindness.

items.sourceNote: 三溫暖蒸氣室 = 告解室 老司機自吹 觀察 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
健身房的蒸氣室，是世界上最誠實的告解室。

熱到第三分鐘，旁邊的大哥開始說：
他年輕時臥推多重、現在的醫生多誇張、他兒子多不孝。

沒人問，但熱氣會逼出真話。

我練的不是身材，是在四十度裡假裝認真聽的演技。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合蒸完三溫暖、滿頭大汗走出來時發限動，配一張霧氣玻璃門
variations: 備註：蒸氣室待越久，聽到的人生故事越驚人。
進階版：那位大哥的臥推紀錄，每蒸一次就重十公斤。

en.content:
The gym sauna is the most honest confessional in the world.

By minute three, the guy next to me starts talking:
how much he benched in his prime, how shocked his doctor is, how ungrateful his son is.

Nobody asked, but the heat pulls out the truth.

I'm not training my body. I'm training the art of pretending to listen at 40 degrees.
en.editorNote: Post after stumbling out of the sauna drenched, paired with a shot of the fogged-up glass door
variations: Note: the longer you stay in the sauna, the wilder the life stories get.
Advanced: that guy's bench PR goes up ten kilos every sauna session.

items.sourceNote: 纜繩機一邊有人用、能不能用另一邊 新手焦慮 在地化 register

zh-tw.content:
纜繩機另一邊有人在用，我到底能不能用這一邊？

我站在原地評估了三分鐘，內心開了一場小型公聽會。
查了 Google，問了 ChatGPT，差點打給我教練。

結論：可以。但我已經緊張到忘記今天要練什麼。

健身房最難的不是重量，是社交禮儀的灰色地帶。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在纜繩機前糾結很久後、終於開練那一刻發限動
variations: 備註：後來那位大哥比我還客氣，直接讓我先用。
進階版：等我研究完禮儀，我的休息時間已經夠長到可以再做兩組。

en.content:
Someone's on one side of the cable machine. Can I use the other side or not?

I stood there for three minutes running a full internal town hall.
Googled it, asked ChatGPT, almost texted my trainer.

Verdict: yes, you can. But by then I'd forgotten what I came to train.

The hardest thing in the gym isn't the weight. It's the gray zone of social etiquette.
en.editorNote: Post the moment you finally start lifting after agonizing in front of the cable machine
variations: Note: the guy turned out to be nicer than me and just offered me his side.
Advanced: by the time I figured out the etiquette, my rest period was long enough for two more sets.

items.sourceNote: 「來健身房想認識人」vs 真實互動 = ATM 經典梗 在地化重寫

zh-tw.content:
我問櫃台：「健身房哪一台機器最能吸引異性？」

櫃台小姐想都沒想，指著大門外面：
「先生，提款機。」

那天我練得很認真，因為我突然有了動機——
不是練身材，是練到有錢辦會員續約。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合單身又認真健身的人，自嘲在健身房交不到朋友時發限動
variations: 備註：櫃台小姐的眼神，比我的訓練還誠實。
結論：健身房最有魅力的器材，是月底還繳得起會員費的那種人。

en.content:
I asked the front desk: 'Which machine here is best for impressing the opposite sex?'

Without missing a beat, she pointed out the front door:
'The ATM, sir.'

I trained hard that day, because I suddenly had motivation —
not to build a body, but to afford renewing my membership.
en.editorNote: Best for the single, dedicated lifter joking about never making friends at the gym
variations: Note: the front desk lady's stare was more honest than my entire workout.
Conclusion: the most attractive equipment in the gym is whoever can still pay their dues at month-end.

items.sourceNote: 晨練 動機 vs 睡眠 的每日戰爭 + 想一想就累 反諷 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
每天早上，我的動機跟我的睡意都會打一場仗。

動機說：起來，去練，你會謝謝自己。
睡意說：再躺十分鐘，反正你昨天也想了一整天。

結果是，我光是「想」去健身房，就已經累到需要休息。

科學證實：思考運動，也是一種有氧。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合鬧鐘響後躺在床上、跟自己天人交戰時的限動文案
variations: 備註：我每天都在腦中完成一場完整的訓練，從不流汗。
進階版：光是想到要練腿，我的心率就已經達標了。

en.content:
Every morning my motivation and my sleepiness go to war.

Motivation: Get up, go train, you'll thank yourself.
Sleepiness: Ten more minutes, you already thought about it all day yesterday.

The result: just thinking about going to the gym leaves me too tired and in need of rest.

Science confirms it: thinking about exercise is its own form of cardio.
en.editorNote: Internal-monologue caption for lying in bed after the alarm, locked in self-negotiation
variations: Note: I complete a full workout in my head every day. Never break a sweat.
Advanced: just thinking about leg day already gets my heart rate into the target zone.

items.sourceNote: 「最愛的健身房器材是販賣機」經典梗 在地化重寫 + 訓練後合理化

zh-tw.content:
別人問我健身房最愛哪一台機器。

不是臥推，不是深蹲架，不是滑輪下拉。

是門口那台販賣機。

練完一杯運動飲料、一條蛋白棒、外加一包餅乾犒賞自己，
它從不要求我做「再一下」，也從不問我深蹲幾公斤。

它只是默默地，接受我所有的軟弱。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合練完站在販賣機前投幣那一刻發限動，配一張按鈕特寫
variations: 備註：販賣機是健身房裡唯一不會 PUA 我的器材。
結論：我練的不是肌肉，是把練完的熱量補回去的效率。

en.content:
People ask which machine is my favorite at the gym.

Not the bench, not the squat rack, not the lat pulldown.

It's the vending machine by the door.

A sports drink, a protein bar, and a bag of cookies to reward myself after a session —
it never demands 'one more rep', never asks what I squat.

It just quietly accepts all of my weakness.
en.editorNote: Post the moment you're feeding coins into the vending machine post-workout, paired with a close-up of the buttons
variations: Note: the vending machine is the only equipment in the gym that doesn't gaslight me.
Conclusion: I'm not training muscle. I'm training the efficiency of putting the calories back.

items.sourceNote: 練腿後第二天 起身坐下 全身抗議 DOMS register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
練腿隔天，我的身體把「坐下」這個動作重新定義成極限挑戰。

要坐下：先用手扶牆，控制離心，全程像在卸載一台引擎。
要起身：先深呼吸，發出聲音，然後祈禱。

同事問我是不是受傷了。
我說沒有，這叫進步。

他露出那種「我不會再約你吃午餐」的表情。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合練腿後第二天上班、坐下起身都很狼狽時發限動
variations: 備註：那天我去廁所，來回花了五分鐘，全是腿的問題。
進階版：椅子變成我的勁敵，馬桶變成我的考場。

en.content:
The day after leg day, my body redefined 'sitting down' as an extreme sport.

To sit: brace a hand on the wall, control the descent, like lowering an engine off a crane.
To stand: deep breath, make a sound, then pray.

A coworker asked if I'd hurt myself.
I said no, this is progress.

He gave me the 'I'm never inviting you to lunch again' look.
en.editorNote: Post the day after leg day at the office, when every sit and stand is a whole production
variations: Note: a round trip to the bathroom took five minutes that day. All leg-related.
Advanced: the chair became my nemesis, the toilet became my final exam.

items.sourceNote: 「腹肌是廚房做出來的」飲食現實 vs 訓練量 反差 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
教練說：腹肌是在廚房裡練出來的。

所以我每天認真練腹：仰臥起坐三十下、捲腹四組、棒式撐到發抖。
回家打開冰箱：鹹酥雞、珍奶、一片巧克力當宵夜。

結果我的腹肌確實在廚房裡，只是被埋在那層保護它的東西底下。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合練完核心、回家又開冰箱時，配一張深夜廚房的限動
variations: 備註：我的廚房比我的健身房還會抵銷我的努力。
結論：腹肌不是練不出來，是我捨不得它出來。

en.content:
Trainer: Abs are made in the kitchen.

So every day I train them hard: thirty sit-ups, four sets of crunches, planks until I shake.
Then I get home, open the fridge: fried chicken, milk tea, a square of chocolate for a midnight snack.

Turns out my abs ARE in the kitchen. Just buried under the layer that's protecting them.
en.editorNote: Best after a core session when you're back at the fridge, paired with a late-night kitchen shot
variations: Note: my kitchen undoes my effort faster than my gym builds it.
Conclusion: it's not that I can't get abs. It's that I won't let them out.

items.sourceNote: 咖啡 = 原版 pre-workout 早晨自我談判 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
他們說咖啡是世界上第一款 pre-workout。

所以我每天早上喝完咖啡，精神大振，鬥志高昂，
覺得自己可以舉起整間健身房。

然後我把這股能量，全部用在了從沙發走到咖啡機，
再走回沙發坐下。

訓練完成。今天也很努力。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合早上喝完咖啡、卻沒去健身房時，配一張空咖啡杯的限動
variations: 備註：咖啡因進得去，人出不了門。
進階版：第二杯咖啡是給『明天一定去』的我喝的。

en.content:
They say coffee was the world's first pre-workout.

So every morning I finish my coffee, fully charged, fired up,
feeling like I could lift the entire gym.

Then I channel all that energy into walking from the couch to the coffee machine,
and back to the couch.

Workout complete. Crushed it today.
en.editorNote: Best on a morning when the coffee landed but the gym didn't, paired with an empty mug
variations: Note: the caffeine got in. I never got out.
Advanced: the second cup is for the version of me that's 'definitely going tomorrow'.

items.sourceNote: 新手不會用器材 假裝在熱身 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
新手在健身房，最常做的動作不是訓練，
是站在一台器材前面，假裝在思考菜表，
其實是在偷看別人怎麼用。

看不懂的時候，我會用力拉一下，調個沒意義的高度，
然後一臉專業地走開，假裝那台不適合我今天的計畫。

我練的不是肌肉，是即興演技。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在陌生器材前裝懂、最後落跑時發限動
variations: 備註：我已經連續三週『今天剛好不練那塊』了。
結論：健身房最難的不是重量，是承認你不會用。

en.content:
The move a beginner does most at the gym isn't lifting.
It's standing in front of a machine, pretending to plan their program,
while actually watching how the next person uses it.

When I can't figure it out, I tug a lever, adjust a setting that means nothing,
then walk away looking professional, like it just didn't fit today's plan.

I'm not training muscle. I'm training improv.
en.editorNote: Best while bailing on a machine you can't figure out, posted with a straight face
variations: Note: I've been 'just not hitting that muscle today' for three straight weeks.
Conclusion: the hardest thing in the gym isn't the weight. It's admitting you don't know how to use it.

items.sourceNote: 健身房暗戀 vs 真實互動 = 共用一台器材的尷尬 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
健身房裡有個我每週都會看到的人。

我的內心小劇場：我們會在飲水機旁相遇，自然聊起來，從此一起練。
現實：我們同時想用同一台滑輪下拉，互相讓了三次，最後兩個都尷尬走開，誰都沒用到。

那是我們最深的一次交流。從此我換了訓練時段。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在健身房遇到固定班底、卻一句話都不敢講時發限動
variations: 備註：我練的不是身材，是逃避社交的腳程。
結論：健身房的暗戀，死於器材使用權的禮讓。

en.content:
There's someone I see at the gym every week.

In my head: we meet by the water fountain, fall into easy conversation, start training together.
In reality: we both reach for the same lat pulldown, wave each other ahead three times, then both retreat in embarrassment and neither of us uses it.

That was our deepest exchange. I've since changed my training schedule.
en.editorNote: Best when you keep seeing the same regular but can't say a word, posted as a quiet confession
variations: Note: I'm not training my body. I'm training my escape route from social contact.
Conclusion: the gym crush dies at the altar of equipment etiquette.

items.sourceNote: 「永遠在 bulk 跟 cut 之間搖擺、兩個都沒做到」反差 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
增肌期：我跟自己說，先吃肥一點，之後再來雕線條。
減脂期：我跟自己說，先把線條練出來，之後再來補體積。

結果一年下來，我增肌的時候在減脂，減脂的時候在增肌，
兩個目標各完成 50%，加起來剛好 0。

我練的不是身材，是在兩個藉口之間無限漂移。
zh-tw.editorNote: 暑假前傳給那個每三個月就換一次「這次認真」目標的健身房戰友
variations: 備註：我永遠停在『下個月開始正式 cut』，那個月從來沒到。
結論：bulk 跟 cut 我都沒輸，因為我從來沒真正開始過。

en.content:
Bulking phase: I tell myself eat big now, carve the lines later.
Cutting phase: I tell myself get the lines first, add the size later.

A year in, I was cutting during my bulk and bulking during my cut.
Each goal 50% done, which adds up to exactly zero.

I'm not building a body. I'm drifting endlessly between two excuses.
en.editorNote: Send before summer to the gym buddy who swaps to a new 'this time for real' goal every three months
variations: Note: I'm permanently stuck at 'official cut starts next month'. That month never comes.
Conclusion: I never lost the bulk-vs-cut war, because I never actually started fighting it.

items.sourceNote: 肌酸水腫 = 一週變壯三公斤的錯覺 反諷 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
開始吃肌酸第一週：體重多了兩公斤，鏡子裡看起來壯了一圈。
我：終於，天賦覺醒。

後來才知道，那兩公斤是水，
肌肉沒長，只是我變成一顆充飽的水球。

停吃那天，我親眼看著自己消風，像中年男人的志氣。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合剛吃肌酸一週、興奮拍照存證的隔天用，配一張側拍
variations: 備註：肌酸不會讓你變強，但會讓你誤會自己變強，這也是一種服務。
結論：我增加的不是肌肉量，是含水量。

en.content:
Week one on creatine: up two kilos, mirror says I'm visibly bigger.
Me: Finally. The gift awakens.

Turns out those two kilos were water.
No muscle gained. I just became a fully inflated water balloon.

The day I stopped, I watched myself deflate, like a middle-aged man's ambition.
en.editorNote: Best the day after a hyped week-one creatine progress photo, paired with a side shot
variations: Note: creatine doesn't make you stronger, but it makes you THINK you're stronger. That's a service too.
Conclusion: I didn't increase my muscle mass. I increased my water content.

items.sourceNote: 暑假倒數臨時抱佛腳 cut 焦慮 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
距離去海邊還有兩週。

我的計畫：每天有氧一小時、戒糖、減鹽、戒酒、一天六餐雞胸肉。
我的執行：今天先吃完冰箱裡的蛋糕，明天再開始。

那塊蛋糕，是我夏天身材的最後一根稻草，
而我親手把它放進嘴裡。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合暑假前看到泳裝照、開始焦慮卻又打開零食那一刻發限動
variations: 備註：兩週練不出腹肌，但夠我焦慮十四天。
結論：我的暑假身材，每年都在『下週開始』裡夭折。

en.content:
Two weeks until the beach trip.

My plan: an hour of cardio daily, no sugar, low sodium, no alcohol, six chicken-breast meals a day.
My execution: finish the cake in the fridge today, start tomorrow.

That slice of cake was the last straw for my summer body,
and I placed it in my mouth with my own hands.
en.editorNote: Post the moment a swimsuit photo triggers the panic, right as you reach for a snack anyway
variations: Note: two weeks won't build abs, but it's enough for fourteen days of anxiety.
Conclusion: my summer body dies inside 'starting next week' every single year.

items.sourceNote: 乾吃 pre-workout 逞強 vs 下場 反差 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
看別人乾吃 pre-workout，帥到不行，一臉殺氣走進槓鈴區。
我：這我也會。

於是我乾吞了一匙，沒配水。
粉末黏在喉嚨，嗆到咳了三分鐘，眼淚鼻涕一起出來，
隔壁阿姨以為我在哭。

那天我沒練出力量，只練出一場大型表演。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合逞強乾吃 pre-workout 嗆到、蹲在飲水機旁邊那一刻發限動
variations: 備註：帥三秒，咳三分鐘，CP 值極低。
結論：有些粉末，是用來配水的，不是用來證明什麼的。

en.content:
Watching someone dry-scoop pre-workout looks unreal — pure menace, striding into the barbell zone.
Me: I can do that.

So I dry-swallowed a scoop, no water.
Powder caked my throat, I coughed for three minutes, tears and snot everywhere,
and the lady next to me thought I was crying.

That day I didn't build strength. I staged a full theatrical production.
en.editorNote: Best the moment you choke on a dry scoop and end up crouched by the water fountain
variations: Note: three seconds of cool, three minutes of coughing. Terrible exchange rate.
Conclusion: some powders are meant to be mixed with water, not used to prove a point.

items.sourceNote: 練完第一天就找腹肌的新手過度自信 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
健身第一天，做完二十下捲腹，
我立刻衝去廁所掀衣服找腹肌。

沒有。
我吸氣、打燈、調角度、用力繃緊，
勉強看到一條，那是肋骨。

結論：腹肌不是一天練出來的，但失望可以。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合練第一天、滿懷期待掀衣服卻撲空那一刻的限動文案
variations: 備註：我以為差一下捲腹就有六塊，結果差的是兩年。
進階版：第二天我繼續找，腹肌沒來，但腰痠來了。

en.content:
Day one at the gym, finished twenty crunches,
and I sprinted to the bathroom to lift my shirt and find the abs.

Nothing.
I sucked in, adjusted the lighting, found my angle, flexed hard,
and barely saw one line. That was a rib.

Conclusion: abs aren't built in a day, but disappointment is.
en.editorNote: Post the moment you hopefully lift your shirt on day one and find absolutely nothing
variations: Note: I thought I was one crunch away from a six-pack. Turns out I was two years away.
Day two: I kept looking. The abs didn't show up, but the back soreness did.

items.sourceNote: 練腿後滾筒放鬆 比訓練本身還痛 反差 register

zh-tw.content:
教練說：練完一定要滾筒放鬆，會比較不痠。

於是我練腿練了四十分鐘，咬牙撐過。
滾筒滾大腿那三分鐘，我發出的聲音，
比整場訓練加起來還大聲。

結論：滾筒不是放鬆工具，是教練派來的第二位教練。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合練完腿、趴在地墊上跟滾筒搏鬥那一刻發限動，配一張滾筒特寫
variations: 備註：滾筒滾到痛點那一下，全健身房都聽得到我在懺悔。
進階版：第二天我連滾筒都不敢碰了，我們需要冷靜一下。

en.content:
Trainer: Always foam roll after, it'll hurt less tomorrow.

So I survived forty minutes of leg day, gritting my teeth.
Then three minutes rolling my quads,
and the noises I made were louder than the entire workout combined.

Conclusion: a foam roller isn't a recovery tool. It's the second trainer my trainer sent.
en.editorNote: Best while sprawled on the mat wrestling the foam roller post-leg-day, paired with a close-up of it
variations: Note: the moment I hit the knot, the whole gym heard me confessing my sins.
Day two: I can't even look at the foam roller. We need some space.

items.sourceNote: 肌酸水腫 = 一週變壯的錯覺 迷思自嘲 register

zh-tw.content:
開始吃肌酸第三天：
手臂看起來變大了，鏡子裡的我容光煥發。
我跟朋友說：肌酸真的有效，我變壯了。

後來才知道，那不是肌肉，是水。
我不是變壯，是變成一顆有在重訓的水球。

結論：肌酸最大的功效，是讓我多了一週的自信。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合剛開始補肌酸、興奮跟朋友炫耀變化的那一週發限動
variations: 備註：停掉肌酸那週，我的自信跟著一起退水。
進階版：我喝肌酸喝最勤的，是體重計上的數字。

en.content:
Day three on creatine:
My arms look bigger, the mirror version of me is glowing.
I tell my friends: creatine actually works, I got jacked.

Later I learn: that's not muscle. That's water.
I didn't get stronger. I became a water balloon that lifts.

Conclusion: creatine's main benefit is one extra week of confidence.
en.editorNote: Best in that first hyped week of creatine when you're showing friends the 'gains'
variations: Note: the week I stopped creatine, my confidence drained right along with the water.
Advanced: the thing that responded most to my creatine was the bathroom scale.

items.sourceNote: 「欺騙餐是我的愛的語言」cheat meal register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
別人的愛的語言：肯定、陪伴、送禮。
我的愛的語言：欺騙餐。

週一到週六，我跟雞胸肉地瓜談一場克制的戀愛。
週日，我跟鹽酥雞炸物珍奶，轟轟烈烈愛一場。

結論：我不是沒有自律，我只是把自律全部存到週日一次花。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合週日欺騙餐當下、罪惡又幸福地打開外送 app 時發限動
variations: 備註：教練說欺騙餐要適量，我說愛怎麼可以適量。
進階版：我週日的熱量赤字，是負的。但我的幸福指數是正的。

en.content:
Other people's love language: words, quality time, gifts.
Mine: cheat meals.

Monday through Saturday, I'm in a restrained relationship with chicken and sweet potato.
Sunday, I fall passionately in love with fried chicken and milk tea.

Conclusion: I'm not undisciplined. I just save up all my discipline and spend it in one Sunday.
en.editorNote: Best during the Sunday cheat meal, guilty and blissful, opening the delivery app
variations: Note: my trainer said cheat meals should be moderate. I said love can't be moderate.
Advanced: my Sunday calorie deficit is negative. My happiness index is not.

items.sourceNote: 「我要一打甜甜圈的六塊肌」bulking 矛盾 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
目標：練出六塊肌。
方法：增肌期，所以要多吃。

於是我每天多吃，吃到甜甜圈一次點六個。
六個月後，我確實擁有了六塊——
是甜甜圈，不是腹肌。

結論：增肌期最危險的不是重量，是『多吃』這兩個字的解釋空間。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合增肌期吃到懷疑人生、站上體脂計前一秒發限動
variations: 備註：教練說增肌不是亂吃。我說我聽到的是『增肌就是要吃』。
進階版：我的增肌期很成功，成功增的是脂肪。

en.content:
Goal: build a six-pack.
Method: it's a bulk, so eat big.

So I ate big every day, big enough to order six donuts at a time.
Six months later, I did get a six-pack —
of donuts, not abs.

Conclusion: the most dangerous thing in a bulk isn't the weight. It's how loosely you interpret 'eat big'.
en.editorNote: Best during a bulk that's gone off the rails, the second before you step on the body-fat scale
variations: Note: my trainer said bulking isn't eating whatever. I heard 'bulking means eating'.
Advanced: my bulk was a huge success. The thing I bulked was fat.

items.sourceNote: 「找補手 = 焦慮」spotter anxiety register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
我請陌生人幫我看補。
他：「沒問題，盡力推。」

第八下，槓卡在胸口，我喊：「幫一下。」
他在滑手機。
第二聲：「幫、一、下。」
他抬頭：「喔抱歉，剛在回訊息。」

結論：補手不是來救我的，是來見證我最後一口氣的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合請陌生人看補、心裡毛毛的當下，配一張仰望天花板的照片
variations: 備註：自己練最大的風險，是沒有人幫你寫遺囑。
進階版：從那天起，我臥推永遠少加五公斤，留給活著的尊嚴。

en.content:
I asked a stranger to spot me.
Him: 'No problem, send it.'

Rep eight, the bar pins to my chest, I call out: 'A little help.'
He's on his phone.
Second call: 'A. Little. Help.'
He looks up: 'Oh sorry, I was texting.'

Conclusion: a spotter isn't there to save you. He's there to witness your last breath.
en.editorNote: Best the moment you ask a stranger for a spot and instantly regret it, paired with a shot of the ceiling
variations: Note: the biggest risk of training alone is having no one to write your will.
Advanced: since that day, I bench five kilos lighter. It's the dignity tax for staying alive.

items.sourceNote: 「滾筒放鬆 = 中世紀刑具」foam roller torture register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
教練說：滾筒放鬆很舒服，回家記得滾一下。

我躺在滾筒上，把大腿外側壓上去那一刻，
發出了一個我自己都沒聽過的聲音。
