所謂放鬆，是用十分鐘的劇痛，換你之後相信自己沒事。

結論：滾筒不是放鬆工具，是中世紀刑具，只是健身房合法持有。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合練完腿、在家滾筒滾到飆淚那一刻發限動，配一張臉部猙獰特寫
variations: 備註：滾到髂脛束那條的時候，我跟我祖先都打過招呼了。
進階版：教練說『會痛代表你需要』。那我整條腿都需要，包括我的尊嚴。

en.content:
Trainer: Foam rolling feels great, do some at home.

The second I lay on the roller and pressed my outer thigh down,
I made a sound I'd never heard myself make before.

'Recovery' is ten minutes of pure agony in exchange for later believing you're fine.

Conclusion: a foam roller isn't a recovery tool. It's a medieval torture device the gym is legally allowed to own.
en.editorNote: Best post-leg-day, mid-roll at home with tears forming, paired with a close-up of your grimace
variations: Note: when I hit the IT band, I said hello to all my ancestors.
Advanced: trainer said 'if it hurts, you need it'. Then my whole leg needs it, including my dignity.

items.sourceNote: 「按摩槍 = 後悔的電鑽」massage gun register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
我買了按摩槍，想說回家自己放鬆，很專業。

打小腿：太爽，停不下來。
打大腿：還行。
不小心打到骨頭：整個人彈起來，槍飛出去，差點打到貓。
打到那條最痠的：我發出了求救訊號。

結論：按摩槍不是放鬆神器，是會後悔的電鑽，附帶你自找的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合剛買按摩槍、第一次打到骨頭慘叫那天發限動
variations: 備註：按摩槍最痛的不是力道，是你以為自己撐得住的那份自信。
進階版：我的貓現在看到按摩槍會躲起來。牠比我聰明。

en.content:
I bought a massage gun, figured I'd recover at home like a pro.

On my calves: heaven, can't stop.
On my quads: tolerable.
Accidentally on bone: I launched off the couch, the gun flew, nearly hit the cat.
On the sorest spot: I sent out a distress signal.

Conclusion: a massage gun isn't a recovery miracle. It's a jackhammer of regret, self-inflicted.
en.editorNote: Best the day you buy a massage gun and hit bone for the first time
variations: Note: the most painful part of a massage gun isn't the force. It's your confidence that you could handle it.
Advanced: my cat now hides when it sees the massage gun. She's smarter than me.

items.sourceNote: 「跑步機三十秒信心崩盤」treadmill cardio collapse register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
上跑步機前，我把速度調到 12。
心想：今天來練個十公里。

第三十秒：呼吸開始亂。
第一分鐘：偷偷把速度調回 8。
第兩分鐘：調回 6，假裝是熱身。
第三分鐘：改用快走，看著前面那個跑很穩的人，懷疑自己是不是另一個物種。

結論：有氧最累的不是腿，是現實打臉的速度。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合上跑步機高估自己、三十秒就投降那刻，喘到不行時發限動
variations: 備註：我調速度的次數，比我跑的步數還多。
進階版：旁邊阿姨快走兩公里面不改色，我跑兩百公尺人生跑馬燈。

en.content:
Before I got on the treadmill, I set the speed to 12.
Thinking: let's do ten km today.

Thirty seconds in: breathing falls apart.
One minute: quietly drop it to 8.
Two minutes: down to 6, pretending it's a warm-up.
Three minutes: switch to a brisk walk, watch the guy ahead running smooth, and wonder if I'm a different species.

Conclusion: the hardest part of cardio isn't your legs. It's how fast reality slaps you.
en.editorNote: Best the moment you overestimate yourself on the treadmill and surrender in thirty seconds, gasping
variations: Note: I adjusted the speed more times than I took steps.
Advanced: the auntie next to me power-walked two km, unbothered. I ran 200 meters and saw my whole life flash by.

items.sourceNote: 「練完照鏡子充血錯覺 vs 隔天現實」pump illusion register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
練完手臂，當下照鏡子：哇，這手臂是誰的，我練起來了。
拍照、存檔、想說等等發限動。

洗完澡，充血退了，再照一次：
剛剛那個人呢？

隔天早上：手臂回到原廠設定，附贈痠痛。

結論：健身房的鏡子會給你三十分鐘的幻覺，然後連本帶利收回。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合練完手臂充血滿滿、拍完照沾沾自喜那刻發限動，但別等洗完澡
variations: 備註：充血是健身房發的試用版，過期自動失效。
進階版：我所有的好看照片，都是練完當下三十分鐘內拍的。這是商業機密。

en.content:
Right after arm day, I check the mirror: whoa, whose arms are these, I made it.
Snap a photo, save it, plan to post later.

After the shower, the pump fades, I look again:
where did that guy go?

Next morning: arms back to factory settings, soreness included.

Conclusion: the gym mirror grants you thirty minutes of illusion, then reclaims it with interest.
en.editorNote: Best right after arm day when you're full of pump and proud of the photo, but post before you shower
variations: Note: the pump is a free trial the gym hands out. It expires automatically.
Advanced: every good photo I have was taken within thirty minutes of finishing. It's a trade secret.

items.sourceNote: 「夏天到了才開始衝刺」夏季備戰焦慮 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
六月中，我打開衣櫃，翻出那件去年夏天說好要穿的背心。

它沒變。
我也沒變。
我們很有默契。

於是我做了所有人都會做的事：
上網查「兩週快速減脂」、買了一罐新的乳清、然後加碼點了一份炸雞慶祝新計畫的開始。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合夏天剛開始、突然想練但又拖延的那一刻，傳給每年都這樣的朋友
variations: 結論：我的夏季備戰計畫，每年都停在「買乳清」這一步。
備註：那件背心已經連續三年見證我的決心了。

en.content:
Mid-June, I open the closet and dig out the tank top I swore I'd wear last summer.

It hasn't changed.
Neither have I.
We have great chemistry.

So I did what everyone does:
googled 'lose fat in two weeks', bought a fresh tub of whey, and ordered fried chicken to celebrate the start of my new plan.
en.editorNote: Best at the start of summer when the urge to train collides with the urge to procrastinate — send to the friend who does this every year
variations: Conclusion: my summer prep plan stalls at 'buy whey' every single year.
Note: that tank top has witnessed my resolve for three summers running.

items.sourceNote: 「休息日是我最強的肌群」rest day 合理化 經典梗 在地化

zh-tw.content:
教練問我，最近哪個部位進步最多。

我認真想了想，誠實回答：
休息日。

它是我練得最勤、最有紀律、出席率最高的肌群。
從不缺席，從不喊累，越練越強。

教練沉默了三秒，把我的課表多加了一組。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合連休好幾天後第一次回健身房、被教練關心時自嘲用
variations: 結論：別人練二頭三頭，我專練休息日。
備註：我的休息日肌肥大效果非常顯著，肉眼可見。

en.content:
My trainer asked which muscle group has improved the most lately.

I thought hard and answered honestly:
rest days.

It's the group I train most consistently, with the most discipline and the highest attendance.
Never skips, never complains, only gets stronger.

The trainer went quiet for three seconds, then added a set to my program.
en.editorNote: Best for self-deprecating use when you return after several rest days and the trainer checks in
variations: Conclusion: other people train biceps and triceps. I specialize in rest day.
Note: the hypertrophy on my rest days is visible to the naked eye.

items.sourceNote: 「作弊餐變成作弊週」cheat meal 失控 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
教練說：一週可以有一餐作弊餐。

星期六中午，我吃了那一餐。
星期六晚上，我想說一天還沒過完。
星期天，我想說週末本來就是一體的。
星期一，我想說都吃了不差這頓，乾脆重來一週。

結論：作弊餐這個東西，最大的問題是它會交朋友。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合放縱整個週末後、星期一站上體重計前的內心 OS
variations: 備註：一餐作弊餐，演化成一個作弊的人生階段。
進階版：我已經不記得上一餐沒作弊是什麼時候了。

en.content:
Trainer said: one cheat meal a week is fine.

Saturday lunch, I had that meal.
Saturday night, I figured the day wasn't over yet.
Sunday, I figured the weekend is really one unit.
Monday, I figured I've come this far, might as well restart the week.

Conclusion: the real problem with a cheat meal is that it makes friends.
en.editorNote: Internal monologue after a full weekend of indulgence, right before stepping on the scale Monday
variations: Note: one cheat meal evolved into an entire cheat life phase.
Advanced: I genuinely can't remember the last meal that wasn't a cheat meal.

items.sourceNote: 「請人幫補的尷尬」spotter 社交焦慮 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
臥推想加重，但需要有人幫補。

環顧四週，每個人看起來都很忙、很兇、很不想被打擾。
我練習了三次開口的台詞，深呼吸，走過去——
結果還是默默回到原本的重量，自己做了十二下輕的。

結論：我練不大的真正原因，不是天賦，是社交恐懼。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合一個人去健身房、想加重卻不敢開口求幫補時的限動
variations: 備註：我寧可少推二十公斤，也不要跟陌生人講話。
進階版：那天我終於開口了，對方超親切。是我自己想太多。

en.content:
I wanted to go heavier on bench, but I needed a spotter.

I scanned the room. Everyone looked busy, intense, and deeply unwilling to be interrupted.
I rehearsed my opening line three times, took a breath, walked over —
and quietly went back to my original weight and did twelve easy reps instead.

Conclusion: the real reason I'm not bigger isn't genetics. It's social anxiety.
en.editorNote: Best when you train solo, want to add weight, but can't bring yourself to ask for a spot
variations: Note: I'd rather bench forty pounds lighter than talk to a stranger.
Advanced: the day I finally asked, the guy was super nice. It was all in my head.

items.sourceNote: 「練第一天的過度自信 vs 第二天現實」day-one overconfidence register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
重訓第一天，我做完全身菜表，意氣風發。

那天晚上：我覺得我天生神力，明天還要加量。
隔天早上：我連牙刷都拿不穩。
隔天中午：坐下是一個重大決定，站起來是一場儀式。
隔天晚上：我發誓再也不碰健身房。

第三天：傷好了，我又去報了團課。人類就是學不會。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合第一次練全身、隔天痠到懷疑人生時發限動
variations: 結論：新手最大的敵人不是重量，是第一天的自己。
備註：那種『我可以加量』的自信，是身體還沒收到帳單前的錯覺。

en.content:
Day one of lifting, I finished a full-body program, riding high.

That night: I'm clearly built different, I'll go heavier tomorrow.
Next morning: I can't even hold my toothbrush steady.
Next noon: sitting down is a major decision, standing up is a ceremony.
Next night: I swear I'll never touch a gym again.

Day three: the soreness fades, and I sign up for a group class. Humans never learn.
en.editorNote: Best after your first full-body session, when day-two soreness makes you question everything
variations: Conclusion: a beginner's worst enemy isn't the weight. It's their day-one self.
Note: that 'I could go heavier' confidence is just the illusion before your body gets the bill.

items.sourceNote: 「上跑步機像運動員、三十秒後抓扶手」cardio confidence collapse register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
上跑步機那一刻：抬頭挺胸，速度調到十，自我感覺像奧運選手。

三十秒後：手死命抓著扶手，速度偷偷調回六，假裝在欣賞窗外風景。

一分鐘後：我跟跑步機說，我們今天先做朋友就好。

結論：唯一在跑的，是我的心跳。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合剛從跑步機下來、喘到說不出話時發限動，配一張心率錶截圖
variations: 備註：那個調速度的按鈕，我用得比任何器材都熟練。
進階版：隔壁阿姨快走都比我穩，而她還在看報紙。

en.content:
The moment I step on the treadmill: chin up, speed at 10, feeling like an Olympic athlete.

Thirty seconds later: white-knuckling the handrails, secretly dialing it back to 6, pretending to admire the view.

One minute in: I tell the treadmill we should just be friends for now.

Conclusion: the only thing actually running is my heart rate.
en.editorNote: Best right after stumbling off the treadmill, too winded to talk, paired with a heart-rate screenshot
variations: Note: I've mastered that speed-down button better than any actual machine.
Advanced: the lady next to me power-walks steadier than I run, and she's reading a newspaper.

items.sourceNote: 「最愛的運動是 lunge+crunch=lunch」食物即 pre-workout 在地化

zh-tw.content:
教練問我最喜歡哪個動作。

我說：弓箭步加捲腹的綜合版。
他眼睛一亮：是哪個進階訓練？

我說：那叫吃午餐。

備註：我真正的 pre-workout，是一碗滷肉飯。喝下去整個人都對了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合練前默默吃完便當、被教練抓包時發限動
variations: 結論：我不挑器材，我挑餐廳。
進階版：吃飽才有力氣決定今天要不要練。

en.content:
My trainer asked which move is my favorite.

I said: a cross between a lunge and a crunch.
His eyes lit up: which advanced exercise is that?

I said: it's called lunch.

Note: my real pre-workout is a bowl of rice. One spoonful and the whole world makes sense.
en.editorNote: Best when your trainer catches you finishing a meal right before training
variations: Conclusion: I don't pick machines. I pick restaurants.
Advanced: I need to eat first to find the energy to decide whether I'm training today.

items.sourceNote: 「我跟健身房是一段複雜的關係」love-hate dynamic register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
我跟健身房，是一段很複雜的關係。

沒去的時候：想念它、愧疚、發誓明天一定去。
去的路上：後悔、想轉頭、找一百個理由。
練到一半：恨它、問自己為什麼來。
練完之後：愛它、發限動、約下次。

我們分分合合三年了，但誰也離不開誰。主要是會員費還沒到期。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合又一次掙扎著去健身房、進門前在門口深呼吸時發限動
variations: 結論：這不是運動習慣，這是一段感情。
備註：我們最甜蜜的時刻，是我癱在地上、它什麼都不要求的時候。

en.content:
Me and the gym? It's complicated.

When I don't go: I miss it, feel guilty, swear I'll go tomorrow.
On the way there: regret, the urge to turn back, a hundred excuses.
Mid-workout: I hate it, I ask why I came.
After: I love it, I post a story, I plan the next visit.

We've been breaking up and getting back together for three years. Neither of us can leave. Mostly because the membership hasn't expired.
en.editorNote: Best after dragging yourself there again, mid-deep-breath at the door before going in
variations: Conclusion: this isn't a fitness habit. It's a relationship.
Note: our sweetest moment is when I'm collapsed on the floor and it asks nothing of me.

items.sourceNote: 「遲到也算有氧」日常熱量赤字自我安慰 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
我今天沒去健身房，但我其實有運動。

趕公車那段：衝刺，算有氧。
扛兩袋外帶上樓：負重訓練。
半夜起來找宵夜：核心穩定。
為了搶限時特價瘋狂滑手機：手指爆發力。

結論：我把健身融入生活了。雖然體脂沒融。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合休息日想偷懶、又想說服自己沒荒廢時發限動
variations: 備註：通勤就是我的團課，車門就是我的計時器。
進階版：今天爬了三層樓梯，這週的腿日我提前下班了。

en.content:
I didn't go to the gym today, but I did work out.

Sprinting for the bus: cardio.
Hauling two bags of takeout upstairs: weighted carries.
Getting up for a midnight snack: core stability.
Frantically scrolling for a flash sale: explosive finger power.

Conclusion: I've integrated fitness into my life. My body fat hasn't integrated anything.
en.editorNote: Best on a rest day when you want to slack but still convince yourself you didn't slip
variations: Note: my commute is my group class, the closing bus doors are my timer.
Advanced: I climbed three flights today, so I'm clocking out of leg day early this week.

items.sourceNote: 「滾筒放鬆比訓練還痛」自虐反諷 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
教練說：練完記得用滾筒放鬆，會舒服很多。

於是我躺在滾筒上滾大腿外側，
發出了我這輩子沒發過的聲音。
旁邊阿姨以為我在求救。

結論：放鬆比訓練還痛。我滾的不是筋膜，是我的尊嚴。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合訓練後第一次認真用滾筒、痛到表情扭曲時發限動
variations: 備註：滾筒不是恢復工具，是測試你還活著的儀器。
進階版：我滾完比練完還喘，這合理嗎。

en.content:
Trainer: Foam roll after training, you'll feel so much better.

So I lay on the roller and ran it along my outer thigh,
and made a sound I have never made before in my life.
The lady next to me thought I needed help.

Conclusion: recovery hurts more than the workout. I'm not rolling out fascia. I'm rolling out my dignity.
en.editorNote: Best the first time you seriously foam roll post-session and your face contorts in pain
variations: Note: the foam roller isn't a recovery tool. It's a device that confirms you're still alive.
Advanced: I was more out of breath after rolling than after lifting. Is this normal.

items.sourceNote: 肌酸水腫 vs 真實增肌 自我安慰 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
吃肌酸第一週：
體重多了兩公斤，我超興奮，以為我在變壯。

查了一下：那是水。

所以我不是長肌肉，我是長水庫。
但沒關係，鏡子裡看起來大一點，我就先信了。

結論：增肌的第一步，是學會跟自己說謊。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合剛開始補肌酸、站上體重計開心又困惑時發限動
variations: 備註：停肌酸那週，我的兩公斤肌肉一夜消失，原來真的是水。
進階版：我練的不是肌肉量，是含水量。

en.content:
Week one on creatine:
Up two kilos. I was thrilled, sure I was getting jacked.

Looked it up: it's water.

So I'm not building muscle. I'm building a reservoir.
But whatever, I look slightly bigger in the mirror, so I'll take it.

Conclusion: step one of bulking is learning to lie to yourself.
en.editorNote: Best when you've just started creatine and step on the scale, equal parts happy and confused
variations: Note: the week I stopped creatine, my two kilos of 'muscle' vanished overnight. Turns out it really was water.
Advanced: I'm not tracking muscle mass. I'm tracking water retention.

items.sourceNote: 「補手 = 情緒支柱」臥推求生 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
臥推到力竭那一下，我跟補手的關係，
比我跟任何人都還親密。

他喊「再一下，我有抓」，
我用全身的信任把槓壓回去。
結果他根本沒碰，只是在旁邊精神喊話。

所謂補手，不是來幫你出力的，是來見證你活下來的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合臥推被補手「精神輔助」救一命後，氣喘吁吁時發限動
variations: 備註：補手最常說的謊，是『放心，最後那下都你自己推的』。
結論：在健身房，能託付性命的人，叫補手；會謊報你出力的人，也叫補手。

en.content:
On the last rep to failure, my relationship with my spotter
is more intimate than with anyone else in my life.

He yells 'one more, I've got you',
and I press the bar up on pure trust.
Turns out he never touched it. He was just cheering.

A spotter isn't there to lift for you. He's there to witness you survive.
en.editorNote: Best after a 'moral support only' spotter save on bench, gasping for air
variations: Note: a spotter's favorite lie is 'relax, you pressed that last one all by yourself'.
Conclusion: at the gym, the person you'd trust with your life is your spotter. So is the person who lies about how much you lifted.

items.sourceNote: 健身房暗戀 vs 全程逃避眼神 內心戲 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
健身房有一個我每次都會遇到的人。

我練的菜表：早就改成跟他同一個時段。
我選的器材：永遠在他斜後方四十五度。
我鼓起勇氣要打招呼：剛好他戴上耳機。
我終於要開口：他練完走了。

三個月來我唯一練出的，是精準掌握他到健身房的時間。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在健身房默默暗戀、卻一句話都不敢說的人發限動自嘲
variations: 備註：我練的不是身材，是偶遇的機率。
進階版：那天他主動跟我點頭，我太緊張，深蹲忘了卸槓直接走人。

en.content:
There's someone at the gym I always seem to run into.

My training schedule: quietly shifted to match his.
My machine choice: always forty-five degrees behind him.
I work up the courage to say hi: he puts his headphones on.
I finally open my mouth: he's done and walking out.

In three months, the only thing I've trained is knowing exactly when he arrives.
en.editorNote: Best for the person silently crushing at the gym who hasn't said a single word
variations: Note: I'm not training my body. I'm training the odds of a chance encounter.
Advanced: the day he nodded at me first, I got so flustered I walked off mid-squat without unloading the bar.

items.sourceNote: 暖身拖延 vs 正式組數 比例反差 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
我的暖身流程：

空槓推兩下：很好，熱了。
坐著滑手機回訊息：醞釀。
看別人練，分析人家動作：學習。
再滑一下，等狀態到：必要。
上廁所、裝水、調歌單：流程。

四十分鐘後，我終於做了今天的第一組正式重量。然後就到下班時間了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在健身房暖身暖了半小時、突然發現時間不夠時發限動
variations: 備註：我暖身的時間，比認真訓練的時間還長。
結論：我不是去訓練的，我是去那邊培養運動的氣氛。

en.content:
My warm-up routine:

Two reps with the empty bar: good, I'm warm.
Sit down, scroll, reply to texts: building momentum.
Watch others lift, analyze their form: learning.
Scroll again, wait to feel ready: necessary.
Bathroom, refill water, fix the playlist: the process.

Forty minutes later, I finally do my first working set. And then it's time to go home.
en.editorNote: Best when you've warmed up for half an hour and suddenly realize there's no time left
variations: Note: my warm-up takes longer than my actual training.
Conclusion: I don't go to the gym to train. I go to cultivate the general atmosphere of exercise.

items.sourceNote: pre-workout 混肌酸 過量震動 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
今天我把 pre-workout 加了肌酸，又多挖了半匙，因為我覺得我值得。

進場前：我是一隻在停車場震動的手機。
暖身時：心跳有自己的鼓點，手抖到掃不開更衣室的鎖。
第一組：力大無窮，眼神死，靈魂出竅。
第二組：我已經在跟天花板對話了。

結論：我沒有變強，我只是充電過頭。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合 pre-workout 過量、蹲在器材旁等心跳回正常時發限動
variations: 備註：那半匙是當天唯一的進步。
後續：回家後我清醒到凌晨三點，重新檢視了整個人生。

en.content:
Today I mixed creatine into my pre-workout, then added an extra half scoop, because I felt I'd earned it.

Before I walked in: I was a phone vibrating in the parking lot.
During warm-up: my heartbeat had its own drumline, hands too shaky to scan into the locker room.
First set: superhuman strength, dead eyes, soul leaving the body.
Second set: I was already negotiating with the ceiling.

Conclusion: I didn't get stronger. I just overcharged.
en.editorNote: Best when you've over-scooped and you're crouched by a machine waiting for your heart rate to come down
variations: Note: that half scoop was the only gain of the day.
Follow-up: I got home and stayed awake until 3 a.m. reconsidering my entire life.

items.sourceNote: 泡棉滾筒 愛恨情仇 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
我跟泡棉滾筒的關係很複雜。

它說：「我是來幫你放鬆的。」
滾下去那一刻，我發出了我這輩子沒發過的聲音。
它愛我，愛到我大腿外側淤青。
我恨它，恨到每次練完還是乖乖把它拿出來。

所謂筋膜放鬆，就是用十分鐘的痛，換取明天少一點的痛。我選擇兩個都要。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合練完癱在地上滾滾筒、痛到表情扭曲時發限動
variations: 備註：滾到 IT band 那條的人，我們是同一種倖存者。
結論：它不是放鬆工具，是付費的酷刑。

en.content:
My relationship with the foam roller is complicated.

It says: 'I'm here to help you relax.'
The moment I roll onto it, I make a sound I've never made before in my life.
It loves me — so much that my outer thigh bruises.
I hate it — so much that I still dutifully pull it out after every session.

Myofascial release is just trading ten minutes of pain today for slightly less pain tomorrow. I'll take both, thanks.
en.editorNote: Best collapsed on the floor mid-roll, face contorted in pain
variations: Note: if you've rolled out your IT band, we're the same kind of survivor.
Conclusion: it's not a recovery tool. It's paid torture.

items.sourceNote: 休息日 身體變形錯覺 body dysmorphia register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
練的那天：鏡子裡的我精壯有線條，pump 滿滿，世界是我的。
休息一天：怎麼好像縮水了。
休息兩天：肌肉去哪了，我是不是練假的。
休息三天：對著鏡子確認自己還在不在。

結論：我的自信，跟充血一樣，只能維持四十分鐘。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合連續休息兩三天、開始懷疑自己白練的人發限動
variations: 備註：休息日不是讓肌肉長大，是讓焦慮長大。
進階版：所以我根本不敢休息，我休的不是身體，是心理防線。

en.content:
Training day: the mirror shows lean, defined, full of pump — the world is mine.
One rest day: wait, did I shrink?
Two rest days: where did the muscle go, was it ever real?
Three rest days: I check the mirror to confirm I still exist.

Conclusion: my confidence, like my pump, lasts about forty minutes.
en.editorNote: Best for anyone two or three rest days deep, starting to suspect they trained for nothing
variations: Note: rest days don't grow your muscles. They grow your anxiety.
Advanced: which is why I'm scared to rest at all — it's not my body recovering, it's my psyche under siege.

items.sourceNote: 練後拉伸 跳過 vs 隔天後果 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
教練說：練完一定要拉伸喔。
我說：好，我會的。

然後我直接收包包、喝完蛋白、滑著手機走出健身房，拉伸這件事被我留在了平行時空。

隔天早上，我的腿後肌像兩條被打死結的耳機線，蹲下去的瞬間，整個人理解了什麼叫因果報應。

結論：拉伸跟存錢一樣，都是現在懶、以後哭。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合練腿隔天蹲不下去、後悔沒拉伸時發限動
variations: 備註：我從不拉伸，但我每次都跟教練說我有。
進階版：那天我終於認真拉了五分鐘，結果隔天還是一樣痛。我選擇怪基因。

en.content:
Trainer: Make sure you stretch after training.
Me: Of course, I will.

Then I packed my bag, downed my shake, and scrolled my way out the door, leaving the stretching in a parallel universe.

Next morning, my hamstrings felt like two pairs of knotted headphones, and the moment I tried to crouch, I finally understood what karma means.

Conclusion: stretching is like saving money — skip it now, cry about it later.
en.editorNote: Best the morning after leg day, when you can't crouch and you regret skipping the stretch
variations: Note: I never stretch, but I always tell my trainer I did.
Advanced: the one day I actually stretched for five minutes, I was still just as sore. I've decided to blame genetics.

items.sourceNote: 「只 bulk 到冬天，結果第三個冬天」無限增肌迴圈 在地化重寫

zh-tw.content:
我跟自己說：增肌到冬天就好，之後再減。

那是三個冬天前的事了。

現在我的增肌期長到可以申請成為一種人生哲學。
減脂期則短到像便利商店的限定商品，每次都說下週上架，每次都缺貨。

結論：我不是在 bulk，我是在用科學的名義，合法地一直吃。
zh-tw.editorNote: 暑假前一週，傳給那個每年都說「先增肌、夏天再減」的健身房戰友
variations: 備註：我的減脂期，跟我的存錢計畫一樣，永遠停在『下個月開始』。
進階版：教練問我何時減脂，我說等我心理準備好。他說那就是永遠。

en.content:
I told myself: I'll bulk until winter, then cut.

That was three winters ago.

My bulk has now lasted long enough to qualify as a life philosophy.
My cut, meanwhile, is like a convenience-store limited edition — always 'dropping next week', always sold out.

Conclusion: I'm not bulking. I'm just eating forever, legally, in the name of science.
en.editorNote: Send a week before summer to the lifting buddy who says 'bulk now, cut in summer' every single year
variations: Note: my cut, like my savings plan, is permanently stuck on 'starting next month'.
Advanced: trainer asked when I'm cutting. I said when I'm mentally ready. He said so, never.

items.sourceNote: 「週日備餐，週一吃完一半」備餐意志力崩盤 在地化重寫

zh-tw.content:
週日下午：我熱血備餐。
煮了七餐雞胸、七份地瓜、七盒花椰菜，整整齊齊裝盒，貼上週一到週日的標籤。
那一刻我覺得自己是個自律的人。

週一中午：吃掉週一、週二、週三的份。
週一晚上：剩下的全進冰箱，三天後長出新文明。

結論：我備的不是餐，是一場每週固定上演的悲劇。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合週四打開冰箱、發現週日備的餐還在發呆時發限動
variations: 備註：我家冰箱裡的雞胸肉，比我還懂什麼叫長期投資失利。
進階版：自從我改成『隨便買隨便吃』，反而每天都有照吃，這很值得反思。

en.content:
Sunday afternoon: I meal prep like a hero.
Seven chicken breasts, seven sweet potatoes, seven boxes of broccoli, neatly packed, labeled Monday through Sunday.
In that moment I feel like a disciplined human being.

Monday lunch: I eat Monday, Tuesday, and Wednesday.
Monday night: the rest goes in the fridge and grows a new civilization three days later.

Conclusion: I don't prep meals. I stage a weekly tragedy.
en.editorNote: Best on a Thursday when you open the fridge and find Sunday's prep still sitting there, judging you
variations: Note: the chicken in my fridge understands long-term investment losses better than I do.
Advanced: ever since I switched to 'buy whatever, eat whatever', I actually eat every day. Worth reflecting on.

items.sourceNote: 「跟肌酸正在認真交往」補劑擬人化 register 在地化重寫

zh-tw.content:
我跟肌酸的關係，比我跟任何人都穩定。

每天固定見面，從不已讀不回，
它讓我多撐兩下，我幫它買新口味，
我們已經連續三百天沒吵架。

唯一的問題是，它讓我重了兩公斤。
但對方說那是水腫，是體貼的謊，所以我選擇相信。

結論：補劑不會背叛你，背叛你的是你週末的宵夜。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合每天泡肌酸的固定時段，配一張杯子裡白色粉末半溶的照片
variations: 備註：我可以三天不回訊息，但我不能一天不喝肌酸。
進階版：分手最痛的不是人，是那罐喝到底、忘記回購的肌酸。

en.content:
My relationship with creatine is more stable than any I've had with a person.

We meet every day, it never leaves me on read,
it gets me two extra reps, I buy it new flavors,
and we've gone three hundred days without a single fight.

The only issue is it made me two kilos heavier.
But it told me that's just water weight — a kind little lie — so I choose to believe it.

Conclusion: supplements never betray you. Your weekend midnight snacks do.
en.editorNote: Best at your daily creatine ritual, paired with a shot of the half-dissolved scoop in the glass
variations: Note: I can go three days without replying to texts, but not one day without my creatine.
Advanced: the most painful breakup isn't with a person. It's finishing the tub and forgetting to reorder.

items.sourceNote: 「健身房暗戀對象一進門就突然變強」gym crush 隱藏力量 在地化重寫

zh-tw.content:
平常臥推六十公斤就要喊救命。

但健身房那個我偷偷在意的人一走進來，
我的身體突然解鎖了隱藏關卡：
腰桿挺直、表情從容、加重五公斤、組數做好做滿、連喘氣都改成優雅地深呼吸。

等對方走了，我直接趴在臥推椅上，像被拔掉電池的玩具。

結論：我最強的補劑，不是 pre-workout，是隔壁那個人的存在。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合那天剛好遇到 gym crush、回家還在回味自己今天怎麼那麼強時發限動
variations: 備註：我的 PR 都是在有觀眾的時候破的，沒人看時我連空槓都嫌重。
進階版：那天對方跟別人來，我直接縮短訓練，提早回家躺平。

en.content:
Normally I'm crying for help at a 135-pound bench.

But the moment my secret gym crush walks in,
my body unlocks a hidden level:
spine straight, expression calm, plus ten pounds, every set completed in full, even my panting upgraded to elegant deep breathing.

The second they leave, I collapse face-down on the bench like a toy with the batteries pulled out.

Conclusion: my strongest pre-workout isn't a scoop. It's the presence of the person two racks over.
en.editorNote: Best on the day you happen to cross paths with your gym crush and you're still amazed at how strong you suddenly were
variations: Note: all my PRs happen with an audience. When no one's watching, even the empty bar feels heavy.
Advanced: the day they showed up with someone else, I cut my session short and went home to lie down.

items.sourceNote: 「bulk 永遠不會停、abs is loading」迷因在地化重寫成增肌期合理化 register

zh-tw.content:
我從去年冬天開始增肌。
冬天說：天氣冷，多吃才有力氣。
春天說：基礎還沒打穩，再吃一陣子。
夏天到了，我說：欸，我好像增過頭了。

別人的腹肌叫做六塊。
我的腹肌叫做「載入中，請稍候」。

結論：我不是在增肌，我是在收集脂肪當紀念品。
zh-tw.editorNote: 暑假前照鏡子發現「減脂日期」一直往後延的人，看完會默默點頭
variations: 備註：教練問我什麼時候開始減脂，我說等我捨得放下那碗滷肉飯。
進階版：我的腹肌一直在載入，但網路是我自己斷的。

en.content:
I started bulking last winter.
Winter said: it's cold, eat more to stay warm.
Spring said: foundation's not solid yet, keep eating.
Summer arrived and I said: huh, I think I overshot.

Other people have a six-pack.
Mine is labeled 'loading, please wait'.

Conclusion: I'm not building muscle. I'm collecting fat as a souvenir.
en.editorNote: For anyone who keeps pushing their 'cut start date' back and just noticed it's summer
variations: Note: my trainer asked when I'm cutting. I said when I can bear to put down the rice bowl.
Advanced: my abs are still loading. I'm the one who cut the connection.

items.sourceNote: 「練完腿走路變跑酷」DOMS 迷因在地化重寫，加捷運手扶梯場景

zh-tw.content:
練完腿的隔天，我下捷運手扶梯，
看起來像在挑戰高難度跑酷。

旁邊的阿姨以為我腳受傷，問我要不要扶。
我說不用，我這叫訓練成果。
她說：那你訓練得很失敗喔。

結論：能不能走路，不是看你練得多認真，是看你昨天有沒有逞強加重。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合練腿後第二天通勤、扶著手扶梯一階一階下時發限動
variations: 備註：坐下要靠扶手，站起來要靠信仰。
進階版：那天我選擇搭電梯，但電梯也要走過去，人生好難。

en.content:
The day after leg day, I rode the subway escalator down
like I was attempting advanced parkour.

An older lady thought I'd hurt my leg and offered me a hand.
I said no thanks, this is my training results.
She said: then your training is a failure.

Conclusion: whether you can walk isn't about how hard you trained. It's about whether you stupidly added weight yesterday.
en.editorNote: Best on the second-day commute, captioned while gripping the escalator rail one step at a time
variations: Note: sitting down requires a handrail. Standing up requires faith.
Advanced: I took the elevator that day. But you still have to walk to the elevator. Life is hard.

items.sourceNote: 「我跟健身房是複雜的感情關係」love-hate register 在地化重寫

zh-tw.content:
我跟健身房是一段複雜的感情關係。

沒去的時候，我想它，覺得愧疚，每天都說明天回去。
真的去了，我恨它，做到一半就想分手。
練完之後，我又愛它，發限動曬甜蜜。
隔天痠到不能動，我又開始懷疑這段關係。

結論：這不是運動習慣，這是長期遠距離戀愛。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合糾結要不要去健身房、在沙發上躺了四十分鐘那個當下發限動
variations: 備註：我們在一起三年了，但見面次數一隻手數得完。
進階版：分手復合超過二十次，會員費就是我們的贍養費。

en.content:
Me and the gym are in a complicated relationship.

When I don't go, I miss it, I feel guilty, I say I'll go back tomorrow.
When I actually go, I hate it, I want to break up mid-set.
After I finish, I love it, I post the afterglow on my story.
The next day I can't move, and I start questioning the whole thing again.

Conclusion: this isn't an exercise habit. It's a long-distance relationship.
en.editorNote: Best while agonizing over whether to go, forty minutes deep into lying on the couch
variations: Note: we've been together three years, but I can count our dates on one hand.
Advanced: we've broken up and reconciled twenty times. The membership fee is our alimony.

items.sourceNote: 暑假海邊行程 vs 三個月臨時抱佛腳減脂 反差 register 在地化原創

zh-tw.content:
朋友：下個月一起去墾丁海邊喔！
我：好啊！（內心：完蛋）

於是我開始為期三週的臨時抱佛腳：
第一週：每天兩練，少吃，超有動力。
第二週：剩一練，開始想念珍奶。
第三週：放棄掙扎，決定海邊穿寬鬆 T 恤。

結論：每年夏天我都在練「下水之前不脫衣服」這項專業技能。
zh-tw.editorNote: 暑假揪團去海邊的群組訊息一跳出來，就可以丟這則進去
variations: 備註：我練的不是身材，是穿著衣服下水也很自然的勇氣。
進階版：到了海邊我全程坐在遮陽傘下顧包包，自願的。

en.content:
Friend: Let's hit the beach next month!
Me: Sure! (Inside: I'm doomed.)

So I started a three-week cram session:
Week one: two-a-days, eating clean, fully motivated.
Week two: down to one session, already missing milk tea.
Week three: gave up the fight, decided to wear a loose tee at the beach.

Conclusion: every summer I train one elite skill — never taking my shirt off before getting in the water.
en.editorNote: Drop this in the group chat the moment someone proposes a beach trip
variations: Note: I'm not training a body. I'm training the courage to swim fully clothed and act natural.
Advanced: at the beach I volunteered to sit under the umbrella guarding the bags. All day.

items.sourceNote: 「lunch = lunge + crunch」雙關在地化轉成練完報復性進食 register

zh-tw.content:
我最擅長的動作，是弓步加捲腹的合體。
它的名字叫做：午餐。

練完那一刻，我的身體進入一種叫做「報復性進食」的狀態：
雞胸兩份、白飯三碗、再來一杯全糖珍奶慶祝今天有運動。

教練說這樣練了等於沒練。
我說：那至少我吃得很開心，這也是一種收穫。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合練完直奔自助餐、夾到盤子滿出來那一刻發限動
variations: 備註：我練一小時，吃三小時，CP 值極高。
進階版：我的減脂菜單最後一行寫著『欺騙餐：每天』。

en.content:
My best exercise is a cross between a lunge and a crunch.
It's called lunch.

The second I finish training, my body enters a state called 'revenge eating':
two servings of chicken, three bowls of rice, plus a full-sugar milk tea to celebrate that I exercised today.

Trainer says training like this is the same as not training.
I said: at least I ate happily. That counts as a gain too.
en.editorNote: Best the moment you hit the buffet post-workout and your plate is overflowing
variations: Note: I train for one hour and eat for three. Incredible value.
Advanced: the last line of my cutting meal plan reads 'cheat meal: daily'.

items.sourceNote: 「滾筒是放著 spa 音樂的中世紀刑具」愛恨關係梗在地化原創展開

zh-tw.content:
滾筒是一個放著輕音樂的中世紀刑具。

教練說：練完一定要滾，幫助恢復。
我把大腿壓上去那一刻，發出的聲音連隔壁深蹲的大哥都回頭。

它愛我，它用盡全力地愛我。
而我恨它，每一吋都恨。

結論：所謂放鬆，是花十分鐘體驗比訓練還痛的痛。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合練完腿、趴在滾筒上痛到表情扭曲時自拍發限動
variations: 備註：滾到 IT 束帶那條，我看見了我阿嬤。
進階版：教練說『會痛代表你需要』。那我可能全身都需要。

en.content:
A foam roller is a medieval torture device with spa music playing.

Trainer: Always roll out after training, it helps recovery.
The sound I made the second my quad hit it turned heads at the squat rack.

It loves me. It loves me with everything it's got.
And I hate it. Every single inch of it.

Conclusion: 'recovery' is spending ten minutes in pain worse than the actual workout.
en.editorNote: Best as a selfie, face contorted, draped over the foam roller post-leg day
variations: Note: rolling out the IT band, I briefly saw my late grandmother.
Advanced: trainer says 'if it hurts, you need it'. So apparently I need it everywhere.

items.sourceNote: 「伸展像開頑固的醃菜罐」觀察在地化轉成練前敷衍伸展 register

zh-tw.content:
教練要我練前好好伸展。

我的伸展：手往天花板隨便伸一下、左右各甩兩下腿、轉一圈脖子發出喀的一聲，結束。

總時長：十一秒。
認真程度：跟我假裝在聽會議的程度差不多。

結論：我的身體像一罐頑固的醃菜罐，而我每次都只轉半下就放棄。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在更衣室鏡子前隨便伸個懶腰就準備開練那一刻發限動
variations: 備註：我唯一伸展徹底的部位，是滑手機的大拇指。
進階版：受傷那天我才認真伸展，叫做亡羊補牢。

en.content:
Trainer told me to stretch properly before lifting.

My stretch: arms toward the ceiling once, two careless leg swings each side, one neck roll that goes crack, done.

Total duration: eleven seconds.
Effort level: roughly equal to pretending I'm paying attention in a meeting.

Conclusion: my body is a stubborn pickle jar, and I give the lid half a twist before giving up.
en.editorNote: Post the moment you do a lazy stretch in front of the locker mirror and call it a warm-up
variations: Note: the only thing I stretch thoroughly is my scrolling thumb.
Advanced: I finally stretched seriously the day I got hurt. Better late than never, I guess.

items.sourceNote: 「休息日是穿著壓力襪的午睡」梗在地化展開成休息日儀式 register

zh-tw.content:
休息日，是穿著壓力襪的午睡。

早上：穿上壓力襪、貼上痠痛貼布、把腿抬高靠在牆上，發限動寫『主動恢復中』。
中午：睡。
下午：睡。
晚上：吃高蛋白晚餐，告訴自己這也是訓練。

結論：我休息日的儀式感，比訓練日還足。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合休息日當天，腿抬牆上、配一張壓力襪特寫發限動
variations: 備註：休息日我最大的訓練量，是把腿從沙發移到床上。
進階版：教練問我恢復做得如何，我說我恢復得非常認真。

en.content:
A rest day is a nap wearing compression socks.

Morning: pull on the compression socks, slap on a recovery patch, prop my legs up the wall, post a story captioned 'active recovery'.
Noon: sleep.
Afternoon: sleep.
Evening: high-protein dinner, tell myself this counts as training.

Conclusion: my rest-day rituals are more elaborate than my training-day ones.
en.editorNote: Best on a rest day, legs up the wall, paired with a close-up of the compression socks
variations: Note: my biggest lift on a rest day is moving my legs from the couch to the bed.
Advanced: trainer asked how my recovery was going. I said I was recovering very seriously.

items.sourceNote: 「會員卡靜靜躺在錢包裡每月默默審判你」梗在地化展開 register

zh-tw.content:
我的健身房會員卡，靜靜躺在錢包最深處。

它不說話，但每個月一號，它會默默地從我帳戶扣走一筆錢，
像在提醒我：『嘿，記得我嗎？我們已經兩個月沒見了。』

我跟健身房的關係，是目前我所有訂閱裡，續約最穩定、使用率最低的那一個。

結論：我不是在健身，我是在贊助一間我很少去的店。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合月初看到扣款通知、又一個月沒去時的限動文案
variations: 備註：我這張卡的功能，主要是讓我有罪惡感。
進階版：去退會那天是我半年來第一次踏進健身房。

en.content:
My gym membership card sits quietly in the depths of my wallet.

It never speaks, but on the first of every month it silently pulls money from my account,
as if to remind me: 'Hey, remember me? It's been two months.'

My relationship with the gym is, of all my subscriptions, the most reliably renewed and the least actually used.

Conclusion: I'm not working out. I'm sponsoring a business I rarely visit.
en.editorNote: Best as a story at the start of the month, when the charge hits after another month of not going
variations: Note: the main function of this card is to make me feel guilty.
Advanced: the day I went to cancel was the first time I'd set foot in the gym in six months.

items.sourceNote: 「進健身房只用烤箱」sauna-as-only-cardio 迷因在地化展開 register

zh-tw.content:
我今天去健身房，完整流程如下：

刷卡進場、換衣服、拍一張鏡子照。
然後直接走進烤箱，坐了四十分鐘。
出來大汗淋漓，體重瞬間少了一公斤。

我跟自己說：這叫排毒，這叫有氧，這叫高溫代謝訓練。

結論：全場唯一被我操到極限的器材，是那台飲水機。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合烤箱坐到頭暈、走出來癱在長椅上時發限動，配一張霧氣模糊的自拍
variations: 備註：那一公斤晚餐後就回來了，帶著它的三個朋友。
進階版：教練問我今天練什麼，我說『核心體溫』。

en.content:
Went to the gym today. Full itinerary:

Badge in, change clothes, take one mirror photo.
Then walk straight into the sauna and sit for forty minutes.
Come out drenched, one kilo lighter on the spot.

I tell myself: this is detox, this is cardio, this is high-heat metabolic training.

Conclusion: the only equipment I truly maxed out today was the water fountain.
en.editorNote: Best after sitting in the sauna until dizzy, collapsed on the bench, paired with a fogged-up selfie
variations: Note: that kilo came back after dinner, and it brought three friends.
Advanced: trainer asked what I trained today. I said 'core body temperature'.

items.sourceNote: 健身房入會合約 隱藏費用 vs 銷售話術 反差 觀察 register 在地化原創

zh-tw.content:
銷售說：月費只要一千，超划算。

然後：入會費、開卡費、保證金、置物櫃費、教練評估費、毛巾服務費。
合約：綁約一年，提前解約要賠。
小字：續約自動生效，取消需親臨櫃檯，附三個月前書面通知。

那張「月費一千」的海報，是全健身房舉得最重的東西。
因為它扛著我接下來一整年的悔恨。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合剛簽完約走出健身房、看著合約發呆時的限動文案
variations: 備註：唯一沒收我錢的，是那面鼓勵我『相信自己』的牆。
進階版：我健身還沒開始，荷包已經先做完了力竭訓練。

en.content:
The salesperson said: just $30 a month, unbeatable value.

Then: joining fee, activation fee, deposit, locker fee, trainer assessment fee, towel service fee.
Contract: locked in for a year, early cancellation penalty applies.
Fine print: auto-renews, cancel in person only, with ninety days' written notice.

That '$30/month' poster is the heaviest thing anyone lifts in the whole gym.
Because it's carrying a full year of my regret.
en.editorNote: Best right after signing the contract and walking out, staring at the paperwork in a daze
variations: Note: the only thing that didn't charge me was the wall telling me to 'believe in myself'.
Advanced: I haven't started training yet, but my wallet already hit failure.

items.sourceNote: 「教練是私人碎念師 personal nagger」笑話在地化展開成遠距關係 register

zh-tw.content:
我跟我的私人教練，是一段遠距離關係。

他每週傳課表給我：已讀。
他問我這週練了幾次：已讀。
他傳飲食提醒：我按了愛心，然後點了炸雞。
他說『下週見喔』：我回『一定』，語氣真誠得連我自己都信了。

我付錢請他當我的私人教練，結果他變成我的私人碎念師。
而且他念得很對，這才是最痛的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合連續兩週沒去、被教練關心後，心虛地發限動用
variations: 備註：教練唯一沒念我的一次，是我終於出現那天。他太震驚了。
進階版：我跟教練的訊息紀錄，是一部只有他在說話的長篇小說。

en.content:
My personal trainer and I are in a long-distance relationship.

He sends me my program each week: read, no reply.
He asks how many times I trained: read, no reply.
He sends a nutrition reminder: I heart it, then order fried chicken.
He says 'see you next week': I say 'for sure', so sincerely I almost believe myself.

I pay him to be my personal trainer, and he's become my personal nagger.
And he's always right. That's the part that stings.
en.editorNote: Best after ghosting the gym for two weeks and getting a check-in text, posted with a guilty conscience
variations: Note: the one time he didn't nag me was the day I actually showed up. He was too shocked.
Advanced: my chat history with my trainer is a novel where only one character speaks.

items.sourceNote: 智慧手錶運動環 gamification vs 真實訓練 反差 register 在地化原創

zh-tw.content:
自從戴了運動手錶，我的訓練動機變成了：

把三個環補滿。

差一點沒補滿：睡前在客廳原地踏步兩百下。
手錶說『你今天很棒！』：我感動到差點哭。
手錶說『你已連續達標 30 天』：我把它截圖發限動，像領到畢業證書。

結論：我練的不是身材，是一隻手錶的認同。
它一句稱讚，比我教練整個月的鼓勵還有用。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合睡前為了補滿最後一個環、在房間裡狂走時發限動，配一張手錶介面截圖
variations: 備註：手錶沒電那天，我覺得今天的運動全部白費了。
進階版：我最怕的不是變胖，是手錶說『你今天的活動量比昨天少』。

en.content:
Ever since I got a smartwatch, my entire training motivation became:

Close all three rings.

One ring not quite closed: two hundred marches in place in the living room before bed.
Watch says 'Great job today!': I nearly cry.
Watch says 'You've hit your goal 30 days in a row': I screenshot it for my story like a diploma.

Conclusion: I'm not training my body. I'm training for a watch's approval.
One word of praise from it beats a whole month of my trainer's encouragement.
en.editorNote: Best while pacing the room before bed to close the last ring, paired with a screenshot of the watch face
variations: Note: the day my watch died, I felt like the entire workout was wasted.
Advanced: my biggest fear isn't gaining weight. It's the watch saying 'you moved less than yesterday'.

items.sourceNote: 深蹲 form check 焦慮 vs 自我懷疑 內心戲 register 在地化原創

zh-tw.content:
我在深蹲的時候，腦中有一整個評審團：

『膝蓋是不是內扣？』『背有沒有拱？』『重心是不是跑掉了？』
『後面那個人是不是在看我動作很醜？』
『我到底是在蹲，還是在單純地上下移動？』

於是我在最底部停了三秒，不是為了張力，
是因為我突然忘記正確的動作長怎樣。

結論：我練的不是腿，是在眾目睽睽下懷疑人生的能力。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合深蹲架前緊張到忘記怎麼蹲、做完立刻查影片對照時的限動
variations: 備註：我錄影檢查動作，結果整段都在看背景那個人練得比我好。
進階版：教練說『動作很標準』的那天，我開心到隔天全身痠得像被車撞。

en.content:
When I squat, there's an entire panel of judges in my head:

'Are my knees caving in?' 'Is my back rounding?' 'Did my weight shift forward?'
'Is that person behind me watching how ugly my form is?'
'Am I even squatting, or just moving up and down?'

So I pause three seconds at the bottom, not for tension,
but because I suddenly forgot what proper form looks like.

Conclusion: I'm not training legs. I'm training the ability to doubt my entire life in public.
en.editorNote: Best when you freeze at the squat rack forgetting how to squat, then immediately check a form video
variations: Note: I filmed myself for a form check and spent the whole clip watching the guy behind me lift better.
Advanced: the day my trainer said 'good form', I was so happy I woke up the next day sore like I got hit by a car.

items.sourceNote: 夏天流汗量 vs 冷氣依賴 反差 register 在地化原創

zh-tw.content:
七月的健身房，我最強的一組不是臥推，是搶冷氣出風口正下方那台跑步機。

那台一被佔走，我的訓練意志直接掉三成。
有人不知情走去關小冷氣，我看他的眼神，像在看一個背叛人類的叛徒。

結論：夏天我練的不是心肺，是對空調的忠誠度。
汗流得越多，越證明我不是來運動的，是來這裡免費吹冷氣的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合七月剛進健身房、直奔冷氣口那台器材時發限動，配一張出風口特寫
variations: 備註：冷氣壞掉那天，全場只剩三個人，而且大家都很有默契地提早走。
進階版：我會挑器材，不是看能練哪裡，是看離出風口幾公尺。

en.content:
In July, my strongest set isn't the bench. It's securing the treadmill directly under the AC vent.

The second someone takes it, my will to train drops thirty percent.
When a stranger walks over to turn the AC down, I look at them like they betrayed the entire human race.

Conclusion: in summer I'm not training my cardio. I'm training my loyalty to air conditioning.
The more I sweat, the more it proves I'm not here to work out. I'm here for the free AC.
en.editorNote: Best when you walk into the gym in July and beeline for the machine under the vent, paired with a close-up of the AC outlet
variations: Note: the day the AC broke, only three of us stayed, and we all left early by silent agreement.
Advanced: I pick machines not by what they train, but by how many meters they are from the vent.

items.sourceNote: 健身房體重計 vs 早晚差異 自我安慰 register 在地化原創

zh-tw.content:
我踩健身房體重計的儀式：

先脫鞋。
再脫外套。
把手機、鑰匙、水壺全部放地上。
吐一口氣。
站上去，數字比預期多兩公斤。

於是我下來，再試一次，換一隻腳先上，好像這樣會不一樣。

結論：我不是在量體重，是在跟一台機器談判，而它從來不讓步。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合訓練後站上體重計、被數字打臉時的限動，配一張體重計面板照
variations: 備註：早上量跟晚上量差一公斤，我當然選相信早上那個。
進階版：喝水前量、上廁所後量，我把體重管理變成一門玄學。

en.content:
My ritual for stepping on the gym scale:

Shoes off.
Jacket off.
Phone, keys, and water bottle all set on the floor.
Exhale everything.
Step on. The number is two kilos higher than expected.

So I step off, try again, lead with the other foot this time, as if that changes anything.

Conclusion: I'm not weighing myself. I'm negotiating with a machine that never gives an inch.
en.editorNote: Best after a session when the scale humbles you, paired with a shot of the readout
variations: Note: morning weight and evening weight differ by a kilo. Naturally, I believe the morning one.
Advanced: weigh before water, weigh after the bathroom. I've turned weight management into a dark art.

items.sourceNote: 「今天只做一個部位」計畫 vs 實際只熱身完就走 反差 register 在地化原創

zh-tw.content:
出門前的計畫：今天練胸、練三頭、加十分鐘有氧，最後拉筋收操。

實際流程：
熱身十五分鐘。
滑手機二十分鐘。
推了兩組，覺得今天狀態不好。
跟旁邊朋友聊十分鐘。
拍一張限動，寫「今天有練」。
回家。

結論：我的訓練計畫跟我的存錢計畫一樣，寫得很認真，執行率零。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合走出健身房、發現自己根本沒怎麼練時的限動，配一張空菜單截圖
variations: 備註：熱身是我全場最認真的部分，因為它不痛。
進階版：狀態不好這四個字，是我一週用五次的萬用免死金牌。

en.content:
The plan before leaving home: chest, triceps, ten minutes of cardio, then a proper cooldown stretch.

What actually happened:
Fifteen minutes of warm-up.
Twenty minutes of scrolling.
Two sets in, decided my form felt off today.
Ten minutes chatting with a friend nearby.
One story photo captioned 'got a workout in'.
Went home.

Conclusion: my training plan is like my savings plan. Written with total sincerity, executed zero percent.
en.editorNote: Best walking out of the gym realizing you barely trained, paired with a screenshot of an empty log
variations: Note: the warm-up is the part I take most seriously, because it doesn't hurt.
Advanced: 'not feeling it today' is the all-purpose free pass I use five times a week.

items.sourceNote: 教練請假換代課教練 陌生感 register 在地化原創

zh-tw.content:
我的教練請假，換了一個代課教練。

他不知道我上週練到哪、不知道我膝蓋舊傷、不知道我做深蹲一定要人在旁邊喊。
他看我的菜單，沉默三秒，說：「你之前都這樣練喔？」

那一刻我像個轉學生，站在陌生的班上，連自己幾公斤都突然不確定。

結論：健身房最需要培養的，不是肌肉，是跟教練之間那份誰也取代不了的默契。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合原教練請假、被代課教練檢視菜單當下的限動，配一張空蕩訓練區照
variations: 備註：代課教練加的重量比較保守，那天我意外練得很開心。
進階版：我原教練回來後看我的紀錄，說『你這週怎麼這麼乖』。

en.content:
My trainer took the day off, so I got a substitute.

He doesn't know where I left off last week, doesn't know about my old knee, doesn't know I need someone shouting next to me on squats.
He looks at my program, goes silent for three seconds, and says, 'This is how you've been training?'

In that moment I felt like a transfer student standing in a new classroom, suddenly unsure of my own numbers.

Conclusion: the hardest thing to build at the gym isn't muscle. It's the irreplaceable rhythm you have with your own trainer.
en.editorNote: Best when your regular trainer is out and the sub audits your program, paired with a shot of the empty training floor
variations: Note: the sub went lighter on the weights, and I unexpectedly had a great session.
Advanced: when my real trainer came back and saw my log, he said, 'Why were you so well-behaved this week?'

items.sourceNote: 泡沫滾筒＝中世紀刑具 反差 register 在地化原創

zh-tw.content:
教練說：練完滾一下筋膜，會很放鬆。

於是我把大腿壓上滾筒，滾了第一下，
發出一種我自己都不認識的聲音。

這哪是放鬆，這是中世紀刑具。
別人滾完像做完 SPA，我滾完像剛招供。

結論：滾筒不是幫我放鬆，是幫我認清人生有多緊繃。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合練完腿趴在滾筒上、表情扭曲時發限動，配一張咬毛巾的自拍
variations: 備註：教練說『會痛代表你需要』，那我全身都需要。
進階版：滾到小腿那段，我直接原地跟祖先報到。

en.content:
Trainer: Roll out after training, it'll help you relax.

So I lowered my quad onto the foam roller, rolled once,
and made a sound I didn't know I could make.

This isn't relaxation. This is a medieval torture log.
Other people finish rolling like they had a spa day. I finish like I just confessed to a crime.

Conclusion: the roller doesn't relax me. It shows me exactly how tense my whole life is.
en.editorNote: Best posted while sprawled on the roller after leg day, face contorted, paired with a towel-biting selfie
variations: Note: trainer says 'if it hurts, you need it'. So apparently I need it everywhere.
Advanced: I got to my calves and briefly reported to my ancestors.

items.sourceNote: 「跟肌酸認真交往中」擬人化 register 在地化原創

zh-tw.content:
我現在跟我的肌酸，是認真交往的關係。

每天早上第一件事：先餵它一匙。
出門忘記帶：整天心神不寧。
它快吃完：比我戶頭見底還焦慮。

我對它比對前任還專一，每天準時、從不放鴿子、還會查它的成分表。

結論：這輩子我最長久的一段感情，對象是一罐白色粉末。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合早上沖肌酸水那一刻發限動，配一張搖搖杯跟量匙的合照
variations: 備註：它從不已讀不回，這點勝過我所有前任。
進階版：那天忘記吃，我一整天都覺得對不起它。

en.content:
Me and my creatine? We're in a serious relationship now.

First thing every morning: I feed it a scoop.
Forget it when I leave the house: unsettled all day.
Running low on the tub: more anxious than when my bank account runs low.

I'm more loyal to it than to any ex. On time every day, never ghost it, and I actually read its ingredient label.

Conclusion: the longest relationship of my life is with a tub of white powder.
en.editorNote: Best posted while mixing your morning creatine, paired with a shaker-and-scoop flat lay
variations: Note: it never leaves me on read. That alone beats every ex.
Advanced: the day I forgot to take it, I felt guilty about it all afternoon.

items.sourceNote: 「只 bulk 到冬天」結果三個冬天 拖延 register 在地化原創

zh-tw.content:
我當初跟自己說：只 bulk 到冬天，冬天就開始減脂。

那已經是三個冬天前的事了。

每年冬天到，我說：太冷了，等回暖再減。
每年夏天到，我說：都夏天了，減也來不及，明年冬天再一起 bulk。

結論：我不是在增肌，我是在用四季當藉口，永遠停在同一個體脂。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合換季買大一號褲子那天，對著鏡子發限動用
variations: 備註：我的 bulk 已經 bulk 成一種生活態度。
進階版：教練問我什麼時候減脂，我說『等我準備好』，他笑了，笑得很心疼。

en.content:
I told myself: I'll only bulk until winter, then I'll cut.

That was three winters ago.

Every winter I say: too cold, I'll cut when it warms up.
Every summer I say: it's already summer, too late to cut, I'll just bulk again next winter.

Conclusion: I'm not building muscle. I'm using the four seasons as an excuse to stay at the exact same body fat forever.
en.editorNote: Best posted the day you buy pants one size up, addressed to the mirror
variations: Note: my bulk has bulked into an entire lifestyle.
Advanced: my trainer asked when I'd start cutting. I said 'when I'm ready'. He laughed. It was a pitying laugh.

items.sourceNote: 上半身壯 vs 小腿細 練腿逃避 反差 register 在地化原創

zh-tw.content:
我的上半身：胸、肩、手臂，練得有模有樣。
我的下半身：兩根從國中就沒變過的細腿。

穿短褲那天，朋友看了三秒，說：「你上面是健身房，下面是圖書館。」

我不是不練腿，我是每次排到練腿日，就『剛好』有事、狀態不好、或決定改練核心。

結論：我的身材是一座上半身很努力、下半身在放暑假的建築。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合夏天第一次穿短褲、被朋友吐槽比例時發限動
variations: 備註：我上半身練了三年，小腿還停在轉大人前。
進階版：教練說『該練腿了』，我聽成『該逃跑了』。

en.content:
My upper body: chest, shoulders, arms, all looking respectable.
My lower body: two skinny legs that haven't changed since middle school.

First day in shorts, a friend stared for three seconds and said: 'Up top you're a gym. Down below you're a library.'

It's not that I skip legs. It's that every time leg day comes up, I 'conveniently' have plans, feel off, or decide to do core instead.

Conclusion: my physique is a building where the upper floors work hard and the basement is on summer vacation.
en.editorNote: Best posted the first time you wear shorts in summer and a friend roasts your proportions
variations: Note: three years on the upper body, and my calves are still pre-puberty.
Advanced: my trainer said 'time for legs'. I heard 'time to flee'.

items.sourceNote: 三腳貓訓練 vs 蒸氣室待整場 register 在地化原創

zh-tw.content:
我去健身房兩個小時。

真正訓練：二十分鐘。
蒸氣室：一個半小時。

我在裡面閉眼、流汗、放空，出來的時候紅光滿面，看起來像剛完成高強度訓練的猛男。

沒人知道，我流的汗不是練出來的，是蒸出來的。

結論：蒸氣室是健身房最偉大的發明，它讓偷懶看起來像努力。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合蒸氣室出來、紅通通走過訓練區時發限動，配一張霧氣濛濛的玻璃門照
variations: 備註：我的核心沒進步，但我的耐熱度突飛猛進。
進階版：那天蒸氣室維修，我才發現我根本不知道怎麼填滿那兩個小時。

en.content:
I spent two hours at the gym.

Actual training: twenty minutes.
Steam room: an hour and a half.

In there I close my eyes, sweat, zone out, and walk out flushed and glowing like a beast who just crushed a high-intensity session.

Nobody knows the sweat wasn't earned. It was steamed.

Conclusion: the steam room is the gym's greatest invention. It makes slacking off look like hard work.
en.editorNote: Best posted walking past the weights floor after the steam room, flushed and glowing, paired with a foggy glass-door shot
variations: Note: my core hasn't improved, but my heat tolerance is off the charts.
Advanced: the day the steam room was under maintenance, I realized I had no idea how to fill those two hours.

items.sourceNote: internal only

zh-tw.content: 你不會每天都想練，但每天想不想練，跟你會不會變強沒關係。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合貼在健身房打卡貼文，鼓勵撐過低潮日
variations: 有沒有動力是一回事，有沒有出現又是另一回事。
你不必每次都準備好，你只需要開始就好。

en.content: You won't feel like training every day. Good thing feelings aren't in charge of your progress.
en.editorNote: Great caption for a low-energy gym check-in
variations: Motivation is optional. Showing up isn't.
You don't have to feel ready. You just have to start.

items.sourceNote: internal only

zh-tw.content: 重量不會變輕，是你會變強；這就是為什麼我們不停下來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合舉重、重訓主題貼文的收尾金句
variations: 槓鈴不會對你手下留情，所以你也別對自己手下留情。
你追的不是更輕的重量，而是更強的自己。

en.content: The weight never gets lighter. You just get stronger. That's the whole point.
en.editorNote: Closing line for a lifting or strength-training post
variations: The bar doesn't go easy on you, so don't go easy on yourself.
You're not chasing lighter weights. You're chasing a stronger you.

items.sourceNote: internal only

zh-tw.content: 進步藏在你以為沒用的那些日子裡，一天一天疊起來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合分享訓練歷程、鼓勵長期堅持的貼文
variations: 你今天看不出差別，一年後的你會謝謝現在的你。
小小的堅持，累積起來就是別人追不上的距離。

en.content: Progress hides inside the days you think didn't count. They all add up.
en.editorNote: For a progress-journey post encouraging the long game
variations: You won't see the change today. A year from now, you'll thank today's you.
Small reps stack into a lead nobody else can catch.

items.sourceNote: 「六塊肌藏在一層巧克力底下」自嘲梗重寫成夏天版

zh-tw.content:
有人問我：夏天到了，腹肌練出來了嗎？

練出來了，六塊都在。
只是外面包了一層珍奶、一層鹽酥雞、還有一層我對自己的溫柔。

它們很安全，被保護得很好。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合夏天去海邊前一週，被朋友問身材時的限動回應
variations: 備註：我的腹肌是限量款，一年只在冬天穿很多的時候出現一次。
結論：不是沒有腹肌，是它選擇低調。

en.content:
Someone asked me: summer's here, did the abs come in?

They did. All six.
They're just under a layer of boba, a layer of fried chicken, and a layer of being kind to myself.

They're safe. Very well protected.
en.editorNote: Best a week before a beach trip, when a friend asks about your progress
variations: Note: my abs are a limited edition. They appear once a year, in winter, under three shirts.
Conclusion: it's not that I don't have abs. They just prefer to stay humble.

items.sourceNote: 組間休息滑手機越滑越久 觀察 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
組間休息教練說：休息 60 秒。

我拿起手機，本來只想看一下時間。
然後看了訊息、回了訊息、看了限動、看了留言、看了一支影片、又看了三支。

抬頭一看，休息了八分鐘。
那一組，是我今天唯一認真做的一組。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合坐在器材上滑手機、突然驚覺時間過很久時發限動
variations: 結論：我的組間休息，跟我的午休一樣，永遠不夠又永遠超時。
備註：手機才是我最常訓練的那塊肌肉。

en.content:
Trainer says: rest 60 seconds between sets.

I pick up my phone, just to check the time.
Then a text, a reply, a story, the comments, one video, three more.

I look up. Eight minutes have passed.
That set was the only one I did seriously all day.
en.editorNote: Post while parked on a machine mid-scroll, when you suddenly notice the clock
variations: Conclusion: my rest periods are like my lunch breaks. Never enough, always over.
Note: my phone is the muscle I train most consistently.

items.sourceNote: 「肌肉貸款，一輩子還利息」重寫成關節版 register

zh-tw.content:
重訓就像跟身體借錢。

二十幾歲的時候，借得很爽，加重加得很快。
三十歲那年，身體開始跟我收利息。
膝蓋一筆、肩膀一筆、下背一筆。

而且它從不通融，逾期還會用痠痛加計違約金。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合訓練後關節在響、貼痠痛貼布時的限動文案
variations: 結論：肌肉是我借來的，關節是我抵押的。
備註：我現在暖身的時間，比我當年整場訓練還長。

en.content:
Lifting is like borrowing money from your body.

In your twenties, the loans feel free. You add weight fast.
Around thirty, your body starts charging interest.
One payment on the knees, one on the shoulders, one on the lower back.

And it never grants an extension. Miss one and it adds soreness as a late fee.
en.editorNote: Best when your joints are clicking post-workout and you're slapping on a pain patch
variations: Conclusion: the muscle is borrowed. The joints are the collateral.
Note: my warm-up now takes longer than my entire workout used to.

items.sourceNote: 夏天健身房冷氣壞掉 集體受難 觀察 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
夏天健身房冷氣壞掉那天，
是我看過最團結的一次。

平常各練各的、眼神都不對到的一群人，
那天全部集中在唯一一台立扇前面，
像難民一樣，眼神空洞，汗如雨下，彼此無言，卻心靈相通。

那天沒人破 PR，但我們一起活了下來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合夏天悶熱訓練後、癱在風扇前時發限動
variations: 結論：真正把健身房凝聚起來的，不是槓鈴，是一台立扇。
備註：那天我練的組數最少，喝的水最多。

en.content:
The day the gym's AC broke in summer
was the most united I've ever seen that place.

A crowd that usually trains alone, never making eye contact,
all gathered in front of the one standing fan,
like refugees, hollow-eyed, dripping, silent, yet spiritually connected.

Nobody hit a PR that day. But we survived together.
en.editorNote: Best after a sweltering summer session, collapsed in front of the fan
variations: Conclusion: what really unites a gym isn't the barbell. It's one standing fan.
Note: fewest sets I've ever done, most water I've ever drunk.

items.sourceNote: 「明天開始練」拖延 vs 買裝備先行 反差 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
我決定要認真健身的方式：

先買了健身手套。
再買了舉重腰帶。
然後買了護腕、綁腿、搖搖杯、兩罐蛋白粉。
最後買了一套很潮的訓練服。

裝備到齊那天，我覺得自己已經很強了，於是先休息一天，明天再開始。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合網購健身裝備箱子堆滿房間、還沒去健身房時發限動
variations: 結論：我不是新手，我是裝備收藏家。
備註：那套訓練服到現在還是全新的，只穿去照過鏡子。

en.content:
How I decided to get serious about training:

First I bought lifting gloves.
Then a weightlifting belt.
Then wrist wraps, knee sleeves, a shaker, two tubs of protein.
Finally, a very stylish training fit.

The day all the gear arrived, I felt strong enough already, so I rested one day and planned to start tomorrow.
en.editorNote: Post when the shipping boxes of gym gear pile up and you still haven't gone
variations: Conclusion: I'm not a beginner. I'm a gear collector.
Note: that training fit is still brand new. It's only ever seen a mirror.

items.sourceNote: 練腿後上廁所變成戰術行動 DOMS 梗 register 在地化重寫

zh-tw.content:
練腿隔天，我終於理解什麼叫做「每一步都是決定」。

下樓梯：一階規劃三秒。
坐馬桶：像在拆炸彈，慢慢降落。
站起來：發出一種只有阿公才會有的聲音。

教練說這叫成長痛。
我說這叫我以後再也不敢跟你對到眼。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合練腿後第二天、走路像機器人時發限動
variations: 結論：真正的核心訓練，是練腿隔天從椅子上站起來。
備註：今天公司的樓梯，我是用信仰爬上去的。

en.content:
The day after leg day, I finally understood what 'every step is a decision' means.

Going down stairs: three seconds of planning per step.
Sitting on the toilet: like defusing a bomb, lowering myself very slowly.
Standing up: making a noise only my grandpa should be allowed to make.

The trainer calls it growing pains.
I call it the reason I'll never make eye contact with him again.
en.editorNote: Post the second day after leg day, when you're walking like a robot
variations: Conclusion: real core training is standing up from a chair the day after leg day.
Note: I climbed the office stairs today on pure faith.

items.sourceNote: 雙倍前訓 400mg 咖啡因結果練 15 分鐘手臂 反差 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
我今天挖了兩勺前訓，還乾吞一口肌酸。

十分鐘後：心跳飆到一百六、頭皮發麻、全身刺癢、眼神像要拆房子。

然後我用這股足以推翻政府的能量，
練了十五分鐘的二頭，
拍完鏡子就回家了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合前訓喝太兇、在停車場等心跳降下來時發限動
variations: 結論：我不是來練的，我是來體驗心悸的。
備註：那股能量後來都拿去晚上兩點盯著天花板了。

en.content:
Today I scooped two servings of pre-workout and chased it with a mouthful of dry creatine.

Ten minutes later: heart rate at 160, scalp tingling, whole body itching, eyes ready to demolish a building.

Then I took all that government-toppling energy
and did fifteen minutes of biceps,
snapped a mirror pic, and went home.
en.editorNote: Post when you overdid the pre-workout, waiting in the parking lot for your heart rate to drop
variations: Conclusion: I'm not here to train, I'm here to experience heart palpitations.
Note: all that energy later went into staring at the ceiling at 2am.

items.sourceNote: 休息日比訓練還長 rest day 梗 register 在地化重寫

zh-tw.content:
教練問我這週練幾天。

我：一天。
教練：那其他六天呢？
我：那叫超補償，是恢復計畫的一環。

他點點頭，寫了一句：
「該會員的休息日，比職業選手的賽季還長。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合連續耍廢一整週、被問到訓練進度時發限動自首
variations: 結論：我不是不練，我是把恢復做到極致。
備註：我的休息日長到肌肉都忘記我是誰了。

en.content:
The trainer asked how many days I trained this week.

Me: One.
Trainer: And the other six?
Me: That's supercompensation. It's part of the recovery plan.

He nodded and wrote down:
'This member's rest days are longer than a pro athlete's off-season.'
en.editorNote: Post to confess after a full week of doing nothing, when someone asks about your progress
variations: Conclusion: I'm not skipping training. I'm maximizing recovery.
Note: my rest days are so long my muscles forgot who I am.

items.sourceNote: 有人佔器材滑手機不練 別人在旁邊等 gym etiquette 觀察 register 在地化

zh-tw.content:
健身房最強的重訓動作，
是坐在器材上滑手機二十分鐘。

他做三下、滑五分鐘、再做三下、又滑十分鐘，
旁邊排隊的三個人已經從陌生人變成戰友，
交換眼神、輕嘆、認命地拉筋暖身第八次。

最後他站起來，我以為終於輪到我，
結果他只是換個角度自拍。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在健身房乾等器材、快要修煉成佛時偷拍背影發限動
variations: 結論：他練的不是肌肉，是我的耐心。
備註：我暖身暖到都可以直接去比賽了。

en.content:
The strongest exercise at the gym
is sitting on a machine scrolling your phone for twenty minutes.

Three reps, five minutes of scrolling, three more reps, ten more minutes.
The three of us waiting have gone from strangers to comrades,
trading glances, quiet sighs, stretching and warming up for the eighth time.

Finally he stands up. I think it's my turn at last.
Turns out he just wanted a new angle for a selfie.
en.editorNote: Post a sneaky back-shot when you've waited so long for a machine you've nearly reached enlightenment
variations: Conclusion: he's not training muscles, he's training my patience.
Note: I warmed up so thoroughly I could've entered a competition.

items.sourceNote: leg day 隔天下樓梯像 parkour / 坐下變高難度運動 梗在地化重寫

zh-tw.content:
練完腿的隔天，
下樓梯是極限運動，
坐下是高難度技巧，
馬桶則是我這輩子最恐懼的深蹲。

同事問我怎麼走路一跛一跛，
我說沒事，這叫肌肉在跟我收利息。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合腿日隔天用機器人步伐進辦公室、被關心時發限動自首
variations: 結論：教練說要突破舒適圈，現在我連坐下都不舒適了。
備註：我不是走路，我是在用整條腿跟地心引力談判。

en.content:
The day after leg day,
going down the stairs is an extreme sport,
sitting down is an advanced technique,
and the toilet is the scariest squat of my entire life.

A coworker asked why I was limping.
I said it's fine — this is just my muscles charging me interest.
en.editorNote: Post when you waddle into the office the morning after leg day and someone checks if you're okay
variations: Conclusion: my trainer said leave the comfort zone. Now even sitting down isn't comfortable.
Note: I'm not walking, I'm negotiating with gravity one leg at a time.

items.sourceNote: 腹肌很棒但起司蛋糕更棒 / 我舉鐵是為了叉子不會累 飲食藉口梗重寫

zh-tw.content:
教練說：飲食佔七成，訓練佔三成。

所以我很認真：
訓練那三成，我一週練一次；
飲食那七成，我全部拿去點珍奶加起司蛋糕。

我這叫抓大放小，重點永遠放在最有份量的地方。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合減脂期偷吃、被朋友抓包時理直氣壯地引用
variations: 結論：腹肌總有一天會有，但這塊起司蛋糕現在就想要。
備註：我舉那麼重，就是為了讓拿叉子的手永遠不會累。

en.content:
My trainer said: diet is seventy percent, training is thirty.

So I take it very seriously.
The thirty percent training — I do that once a week.
The seventy percent diet — I spend all of it on bubble tea and cheesecake.

I call it focusing on what matters. I always put the weight where it counts.
en.editorNote: Quote this with a straight face when a friend catches you cheating on your cut
variations: Conclusion: abs will happen someday, but this cheesecake wants to happen now.
Note: I lift this heavy so the hand holding my fork never gets tired.

items.sourceNote: 一月辦卡三月教練不認得我 / 新年決心被遺忘 梗重寫

zh-tw.content:
一月：辦了會員卡，買了三套運動服，發文說今年一定變不一樣。
三月：櫃檯人員看到我，客氣地問要不要辦張新卡。

我沒有放棄我的目標，
是我的目標放棄了等我。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合放很久沒去、下定決心「這週一定回去」時發限動先自首
variations: 結論：我唯一持之以恆的，是每年一月的那份決心。
備註：我的健身卡比我還規律，它每個月都準時扣款。

en.content:
January: signed up for a membership, bought three sets of workout clothes, posted that this year would be different.
March: the front desk sees me and politely asks if I'd like to sign up for a membership.

I didn't give up on my goals.
My goals gave up on waiting for me.
en.editorNote: Post to confess before you swear 'this is the week I go back' after a long absence
variations: Conclusion: the only thing I do consistently is make the same resolution every January.
Note: my gym card is more disciplined than me — it shows up on time every single month.

items.sourceNote: 我不是懶是省電模式 / 像智慧手錶低電量 self-deprecating 梗重寫

zh-tw.content:
朋友：你今天要去健身房嗎？
我：我在省電模式。
朋友：你又沒在動，是要省什麼電？
我：就是因為不動，才能撐到暑假還有電。

我把自己保養得像一支永遠只剩百分之二十電量的手機，
捨不得用，也捨不得充。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合躺在沙發上被揪運動、想禮貌拒絕又不想承認在耍廢時使用
variations: 結論：我不是不練，我是在為一個永遠不會來的訓練日蓄力。
備註：我的意志力跟手機一樣，一到健身房門口就自動關機。

en.content:
Friend: are you hitting the gym today?
Me: I'm in power-saving mode.
Friend: you're not even moving. What are you saving power for?
Me: exactly because I don't move, I'll still have battery by summer.

I maintain myself like a phone forever stuck at twenty percent —
too precious to use, too lazy to charge.
en.editorNote: Use when you're sprawled on the couch, someone invites you to work out, and you want to decline politely without admitting you're just being lazy
variations: Conclusion: I'm not skipping training, I'm conserving energy for a workout day that never comes.
Note: my willpower is like my phone — it powers off the second I reach the gym door.

items.sourceNote: 腿日隔天樓梯變敵人 / 走路像企鵝 迷因原創角度重寫

zh-tw.content:
練完腿的隔天，我才發現我家住四樓。
下樓要用倒退嚕，上樓要扶著牆哭。

別人是走路，我是在跟地心引力談判。
每一階都在問我：你當初到底在深蹲什麼？
zh-tw.editorNote: 練完腿隔天發限動，配一張你扶著樓梯扶手的背影
variations: 結論：教練說腿日會讓我變強，結果我連坐馬桶都要用祈禱的姿勢。
備註：我現在最崇拜的運動員，是那個發明電梯的人。

en.content:
The day after leg day, I discovered I live on the fourth floor.
Going down means reversing, going up means clinging to the wall and crying.

Everyone else is walking. I'm negotiating with gravity.
Every step asks me: what exactly were you squatting for?
en.editorNote: Post the day after leg day with a shot of your back as you death-grip the stair rail
variations: Conclusion: my trainer said leg day would make me stronger, yet now I sit on the toilet like I'm praying.
Note: the athlete I admire most is whoever invented the elevator.

items.sourceNote: DOMS 遲發性肌肉痠痛 / 「跟痠痛穩定交往中」self-deprecating 梗重寫

zh-tw.content:
肌肉痠痛都是隔一天才來。
練的當下爽得像神，隔天痛得像被討債。

我現在跟痠痛是穩定交往的關係，
它每次都比我約好的訓練日還準時出現。
zh-tw.editorNote: 重訓後第二天早上起床痛到笑不出來時發，讓大家一起共感
variations: 結論：健身房的帳單當天扣，痠痛的帳單隔天才寄到。
備註：我不是鐵人，我是隔天才生鏽的那種人。

en.content:
The soreness never shows up until the next day.
During the workout you feel like a god; the morning after, you feel like a debt collector found you.

I'm in a committed relationship with soreness now.
It always shows up more reliably than the training day I actually scheduled.
en.editorNote: Post when you wake up on day two too sore to laugh, so everyone can suffer together
variations: Conclusion: the gym charges you on the day; the soreness mails its invoice the morning after.
Note: I'm not made of iron — I'm the kind that rusts a day later.

items.sourceNote: 「蛋白飲喝起來像後悔」/ 用披薩換蛋白粉是爛交易 梗重寫

zh-tw.content:
我把宵夜的雞排換成了乳清蛋白。
第一口，我就知道這筆交易我虧大了。

它喝起來像一杯融化的後悔，
包裝上卻寫著「香草口味」——香草聽了應該也想告它。
zh-tw.editorNote: 半夜嘴饞、勉強自己喝蛋白飲代替宵夜時發，配一張你皺眉的自拍
variations: 結論：健身教會我最重要的一件事，是原諒。原諒我自己花錢買這種味道。
備註：我不是在補蛋白，我是在為我的身材繳贖罪券。

en.content:
I swapped my midnight fried chicken for whey protein.
One sip in, I knew I got the worse end of this deal.

It tastes like a glass of melted regret,
yet the label says 'vanilla' — vanilla should sue.
en.editorNote: Post when you force down a shake instead of a midnight snack, with a selfie of you wincing
variations: Conclusion: the most important thing fitness taught me is forgiveness — forgiving myself for paying money for this flavor.
Note: I'm not supplementing protein, I'm buying an indulgence for my body shape.

items.sourceNote: 「健身夥伴取消我就順便放假」excuse 梗原創對話重寫

zh-tw.content:
健身夥伴：抱歉今天不能去了。
我：（假裝失望）啊真的假的，那太可惜了。
（掛掉電話後轉身躺回沙發）

他取消的那一秒，是我這禮拜最有活力的時刻。
原來我需要的不是夥伴，是一個能幫我一起放棄的人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 健身約被取消、內心偷偷歡呼時發，懂的人自然會標記自己
variations: 結論：我們約好一起變強，最後只練成了一起找藉口的默契。
備註：我最強的核心肌群，是取消行程時那份毫不猶豫。

en.content:
Gym buddy: Sorry, I can't make it today.
Me: (feigning disappointment) Oh no, really? What a shame.
(hangs up, turns around, collapses back onto the couch)

The second he canceled was the most energized I've felt all week.
Turns out I didn't need a workout partner — I needed someone to quit with me.
en.editorNote: Post when your gym plans fall through and you're secretly cheering; the right people will tag themselves
variations: Conclusion: we promised to get strong together and only trained our shared talent for excuses.
Note: my strongest core muscle is how instantly I commit to canceling plans.

items.sourceNote: 「abs loading please wait」增肌看不到腹肌梗，改成手機讀取條情境原創

zh-tw.content:
朋友：你的腹肌呢？
我：正在載入中，請稍候……
（進度條卡在 12%，已經卡三年了）

我不是沒有腹肌，是它藏在一個我很尊重的地方。
zh-tw.editorNote: 被問「練這麼久怎麼還沒腹肌」時回這句，配一張吃到飽的照片最有說服力
variations: 更新：進度條動了，往回跑到 8%。
備註：我的腹肌跟外星人一樣，很多人相信它存在，但沒人親眼看過。

en.content:
Friend: Where are your abs?
Me: Loading, please wait…
(progress bar stuck at 12%, and it's been three years)

I'm not missing abs — they're just stored somewhere I deeply respect.
en.editorNote: Fire this back when someone asks why you still have no abs after all that gym time; a buffet photo seals it
variations: Update: the bar finally moved — backwards, to 8%.
Note: my abs are like aliens; plenty of people believe in them, nobody's seen one.

items.sourceNote: 「the bulk is never going to stop」永遠增肌永遠不減脂梗重寫成四季對話

zh-tw.content:
春天：先增肌，夏天再來減脂。
夏天：太熱了，秋天再減。
秋天：都要冷了增一波比較實在。
冬天：過年欸，你懂的。

我不是在增肌，我是在收集季節限定的藉口。
zh-tw.editorNote: 轉給那個嘴上永遠說「下個月開始減脂」的健身朋友，他會沉默
variations: 結論：我的減脂期是一種傳說，大家都聽過，但從沒真正到來。
備註：一年四季只有一個季節，叫做增肌季。

en.content:
Spring: Bulk now, cut in summer.
Summer: Too hot, I'll cut in fall.
Fall: It's getting cold, might as well pack on more.
Winter: It's the holidays, you get it.

I'm not bulking — I'm collecting seasonal-edition excuses.
en.editorNote: Send to the gym friend who's been 'starting my cut next month' for two years; watch them go quiet
variations: Conclusion: my cutting phase is a legend — everyone's heard of it, it never actually arrives.
Note: there's only one season all year and it's called bulking season.

items.sourceNote: 「walking like a newborn giraffe」練腿後走路梗原創情境化

zh-tw.content:
練腿的隔天，我走路像一隻剛出生的長頸鹿。
下樓梯是一場我對重力的正式談判。
坐上馬桶的瞬間，我聽見自己發出教練沒教過的音效。

別人是走路，我是在示範什麼叫緩慢下墜。
zh-tw.editorNote: 練腿後鐵腿到不行時發，樓梯前猶豫三秒的畫面配這句最真實
variations: 更新：我今天不是不想站起來，是我跟膝蓋的合約還在協商。
備註：練腿最難的不是深蹲，是隔天假裝自己走路很正常。

en.content:
The day after leg day, I walk like a newborn giraffe.
Going down the stairs is a formal negotiation with gravity.
Sitting down on the toilet, I make a sound effect my trainer never covered.

Other people walk — I demonstrate what a slow, controlled collapse looks like.
en.editorNote: Post when your legs are wrecked after squats; the three-second hesitation before a staircase is the whole mood
variations: Update: I'm not refusing to stand up, my knees and I are still in contract talks.
Note: the hardest part of leg day isn't the squats — it's pretending to walk normally the next morning.

items.sourceNote: 「rest days are my strongest muscle」休息日最強梗改成宗教信仰式自嘲

zh-tw.content:
教練：一週練幾天？
我：主要是休息日。
教練：那你什麼時候練？
我：休息日之間的空檔。

如果休息也算一塊肌肉，我早就是奧林匹亞冠軍了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 被問訓練頻率、但這禮拜只去過一次的時候發，懂的人會默默按讚
variations: 結論：我最發達的肌群，是躺著也能維持的那種決心。
備註：休息日不是偷懶，是我對身體最虔誠的信仰。

en.content:
Trainer: How many days a week do you train?
Me: Mostly rest days.
Trainer: So when do you actually train?
Me: In the gaps between the rest days.

If resting counted as a muscle, I'd have won the Olympia years ago.
en.editorNote: Use when someone asks your training frequency but you've been to the gym exactly once this week; the right people quietly like it
variations: Conclusion: my most developed muscle is the kind of dedication you can hold while lying down.
Note: rest day isn't laziness — it's the most devout faith I practice.

items.sourceNote: 「exercise before my brain figures out what I'm doing」早晨偷襲大腦梗原創重寫

zh-tw.content:
我都趁早上運動，趕在大腦搞清楚我在幹嘛之前。
只要它一醒過來問「你認真的？」，這場健身就結束了。

意志力對我來說不是力量，是一種偷襲的技巧。
zh-tw.editorNote: 分享給那個靠鬧鐘和自我欺騙硬撐晨練的朋友，他正在跟被窩搏鬥
variations: 更新：今天大腦提早醒了，於是我又躺回去了。
備註：我不是有紀律，我只是動作比藉口快 0.5 秒。

en.content:
I work out in the morning — before my brain figures out what I'm doing.
The second it wakes up and asks 'are you serious right now?', the whole workout is over.

Willpower isn't strength for me, it's a sneak-attack technique.
en.editorNote: Share with the friend surviving morning workouts on an alarm and pure self-deception; they're mid-battle with the blanket
variations: Update: my brain woke up early today, so I climbed right back into bed.
Note: I'm not disciplined, I'm just 0.5 seconds faster than my own excuses.

items.sourceNote: 「my warmup is searching for my AirPods」找耳機當熱身梗延伸重寫

zh-tw.content:
我的熱身動作是：翻遍整個包包找耳機。
找了十分鐘、繞健身房兩圈、還問了櫃檯，最後發現它一直掛在我耳朵上。

正式訓練還沒開始，我的意志力已經先做完一組了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個進健身房第一件事是喬歌單、二十分鐘後才碰到器材的朋友
variations: 更新：找到耳機那一刻，我覺得今天已經很努力，可以回家了。
備註：我最持久的有氧，是在置物櫃前來回踱步。

en.content:
My warmup is tearing my whole bag apart looking for my earbuds.
Ten minutes, two laps of the gym, one trip to the front desk — they were on my ears the entire time.

The real workout hasn't even started and my willpower already finished a set.
en.editorNote: For the friend whose first gym task is curating a playlist and doesn't touch a weight for twenty minutes
variations: Update: the second I found them, I felt I'd tried hard enough and could go home.
Note: my most sustained cardio is pacing back and forth in front of my locker.

items.sourceNote: 「I run because I really like bagels」為了吃而跑步梗改成台味鹹酥雞版

zh-tw.content:
朋友：你為什麼跑步？
我：因為我很愛吃鹹酥雞。

有氧不是為了健康，是為了消業障。跑個三公里，才勉強抵一份加辣不加蔥。
zh-tw.editorNote: 跟那個邊跑步機邊滑美食外送的朋友分享，他最懂這種矛盾
variations: 結論：我不是在燃脂，我是在還債。
備註：跑步機上的里程數，等於我對鹹酥雞的愛有多長。

en.content:
Friend: Why do you run?
Me: Because I really love fried chicken.

Cardio isn't for my health, it's damage control. Three kilometers barely covers one order — extra spicy.
en.editorNote: Share with the friend who scrolls a food-delivery app while on the treadmill; they get the contradiction
variations: Conclusion: I'm not burning fat, I'm paying off a debt.
Note: the mileage on the treadmill is exactly as long as my love for fried chicken.

items.sourceNote: 補劑比伙食費還貴的自嘲梗，原創帳單重寫

zh-tw.content:
我的乳清、肌酸、乳清餅乾、乳清咖啡加起來，比我一個月的伙食費還貴。
身體沒變壯，錢包倒是先瘦了一圈。

原來增肌最先練到的，是刷卡的那根手指。
zh-tw.editorNote: 暑假前傳給那個購物車全是補劑、冰箱只剩蛋白飲的朋友
variations: 更新：我戒掉一種補劑，改成兩種，因為新的在特價。
備註：我的肌肉還在載入中，帳單倒是即時到帳。

en.content:
My whey, my creatine, my protein cookies, my protein coffee — together they cost more than a month of groceries.
My body didn't get any bigger, but my wallet sure got leaner.

Turns out the first muscle bulking trains is the finger that taps the card.
en.editorNote: Send before summer to the friend whose cart is all supplements and whose fridge is all protein drinks
variations: Update: I quit one supplement and replaced it with two, because the new ones were on sale.
Note: my muscles are still loading, but the bill posted instantly.

items.sourceNote: leg day 隔天下樓梯像拆炸彈的痠痛梗原創重寫

zh-tw.content:
練完腿的隔天，下樓梯像在拆炸彈，每一階都是一次生死抉擇。
電梯壞掉那天，我認真考慮直接住在公司。

別人腿日練的是肌肉，我練的是對重力的恐懼。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個腿日隔天扶著牆走、坐下前要先深呼吸的朋友
variations: 更新：今天我坐下的聲音，隔壁同事以為我踩到貓。
備註：馬桶對腿日隔天的我來說，是深蹲的期末考。

en.content:
The day after leg day, going down stairs is like defusing a bomb — every step a life-or-death decision.
The day the elevator broke, I seriously considered just moving into the office.

Other people train muscle on leg day; I train a fear of gravity.
en.editorNote: For the friend who walks along the wall the day after leg day and braces themselves before sitting down
variations: Update: the noise I made sitting down today made a coworker think I stepped on a cat.
Note: the toilet is the final exam of squats for post-leg-day me.

items.sourceNote: 「I'm not lazy, I'm on energy-saving mode」節能模式梗改成儲存深蹲能量版

zh-tw.content:
我不是懶，我是開了節能模式。
鬧鐘響到第七次的時候，我的身體正在為晚上那組深蹲儲存能量。

躺著養精蓄銳，也是一種對訓練的尊重——我都這樣說服自己的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 分享給那個週末計畫要健身、結果躺到天黑的朋友，他此刻正躺著
variations: 更新：能量儲存得太成功，直接進入待機一整天。
備註：我的六塊肌還在充電中，請勿打擾。

en.content:
I'm not lazy, I've just switched to energy-saving mode.
By the seventh alarm, my body is busy storing up power for tonight's squats.

Lying around to conserve energy is a form of respect for the training — at least that's how I sell it to myself.
en.editorNote: Share with the friend who planned a weekend workout and lay in bed till dark; they're horizontal right now
variations: Update: energy storage went so well I stayed in standby the entire day.
Note: my six-pack is still charging, please do not disturb.

items.sourceNote: 「My pre-workout has me feeling like I could fight a bear」熊梗改成一杯下去人格分裂版

zh-tw.content:
訓前一杯下去，我覺得我可以徒手打贏一隻熊。
三十分鐘後，我連水壺蓋都轉不開。

原來那不是力量，是我的心臟在替我借高利貸。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個訓前喝完在停車場繞三圈、進場只練了兩組的朋友
variations: 更新：熊沒來，但我的手抖到滑手機像在通靈。
備註：訓前給我的自信，跟我的次數呈完美反比。

en.content:
One scoop of pre-workout and I'm convinced I could fight a bear bare-handed.
Thirty minutes later I can't even twist open my water bottle.

Turns out that wasn't strength — that was my heart taking out a loan on my behalf.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who paces the parking lot after their pre-workout and then does exactly two sets inside
variations: Update: the bear never showed, but my hands were shaking so hard scrolling my phone looked like a séance.
Note: the confidence pre-workout gives me is in perfect inverse proportion to my actual reps.

items.sourceNote: 「I do squats so my snacks have a nice shelf」深蹲練翹臀放零食梗重寫

zh-tw.content:
我認真練深蹲，不是為了健康，
是為了讓我的臀部有個平台放洋芋片。

人家練的是曲線，我練的是零食收納空間——目標一直都很明確。
zh-tw.editorNote: 配一張健身後直接開洋芋片的照片發限動，主打誠實
variations: 更新：平台蓋好了，第一個放上去的是雞排。
備註：練成之後我才發現，最重的一組是把手伸進零食櫃。

en.content:
I take squats seriously — not for my health,
but so my backside has a nice shelf to rest a bag of chips on.

Everyone else is building curves; I'm building snack storage. The goal was always crystal clear.
en.editorNote: Post to your story with a photo of you cracking open chips right after the gym — honesty is the theme
variations: Update: shelf's finished, and the first thing I set on it was fried chicken.
Note: after all that training, the heaviest set turned out to be reaching into the snack cupboard.

items.sourceNote: 「I love cardio, said no one ever」有氧沒人愛梗改成跑步機上內心談判版

zh-tw.content:
「我超愛有氧。」——這句話從來沒有人真心說過。
跑步機開始的三分鐘，我就已經在跟自己談判：
再五分鐘，五分鐘我就當作有來過了。

最後我用走的走完，還很有成就感地拍了張照。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個把跑步機速度調到 4.0 還喘的朋友，他會懂
variations: 更新：談判破局，我提早下機，理由是「今天以肌力為主」。
備註：我在跑步機上跑掉的，主要是耐心。

en.content:
"I love cardio." — a sentence no human has ever meant.
Three minutes into the treadmill I'm already negotiating with myself:
five more minutes, and I'll count today as showing up.

I finished it at a walk and still took a very proud photo.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who sets the treadmill to 4.0 and is still out of breath — they'll get it
variations: Update: negotiations collapsed, I got off early, official reason: 'today was a strength-focus day.'
Note: the main thing I burned off on that treadmill was my patience.

items.sourceNote: 「My protein shake tastes like regret」乳清味道自嘲梗重寫成硬吞版

zh-tw.content:
我的乳清喝起來像後悔的味道。
說是餅乾口味，實際上像餅乾在很遠的地方跟我揮手。

但我還是每天喝——一半是為了長肌肉，一半是因為它比會員費便宜，我得把兩千塊喝回來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個為了不浪費、把難喝乳清硬喝完的朋友，主打共感
variations: 更新：今天搖太用力，蓋子沒關，我用臉接住了整杯後悔。
備註：世界上最遠的距離，是包裝上的餅乾和杯子裡的味道。

en.content:
My protein shake tastes like regret in liquid form.
The label says cookies-and-cream; in reality it's a cookie waving at me from very far away.

But I drink it every day — half to build muscle, half because it's cheaper than my membership and I've got money to drink back.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who forces down the nasty tub just so it doesn't go to waste — pure solidarity
variations: Update: shook it too hard today, lid wasn't on, and I caught a full cup of regret with my face.
Note: the greatest distance in the world is between the cookie on the packaging and the taste in the cup.

items.sourceNote: 「I love the gym, but I love leaving the gym even more」離場最爽梗重寫

zh-tw.content:
我很愛健身房。
但我更愛離開健身房的那一刻。

刷卡進場我心情複雜，刷卡出場我步伐輕盈——
原來這張會員卡，最療癒的功能是出場逼逼那一聲。
zh-tw.editorNote: 配一張走出健身房大門、天空很美的照片發，主打如釋重負
variations: 更新：今天在門口逼逼的那一聲，是我整場最有力量的一下。
備註：我不是討厭運動，我只是特別享受「運動結束」這個項目。

en.content:
I love the gym.
But I love the moment I leave the gym even more.

Tapping in, my feelings are complicated; tapping out, my step is light —
turns out the most healing feature of this membership card is that little beep on the way out.
en.editorNote: Post with a shot of you walking out the front doors under a pretty sky — the theme is sweet relief
variations: Update: that beep at the exit today was the most powerful rep of my whole session.
Note: I don't hate exercise, I just really excel at the event called 'finishing exercise.'

items.sourceNote: 「Burpees? I thought you said Slurpees」把運動聽成食物的梗延伸重寫

zh-tw.content:
教練：等等我們來做波比跳。
我：好喔，我聽成珍珠奶茶。
教練：那平板撐呢？
我：我也聽成鹽酥雞。

原來我的耳朵早就選好邊了——它站在美食那一隊，從來沒背叛過我。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個一聽到運動名稱就自動翻譯成食物的朋友，主打選擇性失聰
variations: 更新：今天教練說深蹲，我腦中浮現的是深夜的鹹酥雞攤，畫面過於逼真。
備註：我的耳朵很誠實，它只想聽關於吃的資訊。

en.content:
Coach: next up, we're doing burpees.
Me: sweet, I heard Slurpees.
Coach: how about planks?
Me: heard franks. With extra mustard.

Turns out my ears picked a side a long time ago — they play for Team Snacks and they've never once betrayed me.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend whose brain auto-translates every exercise into food — the theme is selective hearing
variations: Update: coach said squats today and my mind went straight to a late-night snack stand. The image was far too vivid.
Note: my ears are very honest. They only want to hear news about food.

items.sourceNote: pre-workout 咖啡因手抖梗，改成氮泵一杯下去精力用錯地方的自嘲

zh-tw.content:
我喝了一杯氮泵準備大練一場。
結果整場精力充沛地——把置物櫃擦了三遍，回了三個月沒回的訊息，還差點傳給前任。

最後真正拿去舉的重量，剛好等於那杯氮泵的空罐。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個喝完氮泵手抖到握不住槓、卻把家裡打掃乾淨的朋友，主打精力用錯地方
variations: 更新：今天氮泵下肚後我心跳很快，但那是焦慮，不是力量。
備註：氮泵給了我一整場的能量，我拿去做的事情一件都跟健身無關。

en.content:
I slammed a scoop of pre-workout, ready to move mountains.
Instead I spent the whole session buzzing — wiped down my locker three times, replied to texts I'd ignored for months, and almost messaged my ex.

The only weight I actually lifted all night was the empty pre-workout tub.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who takes pre-workout and cleans their whole apartment instead of training — the theme is energy in all the wrong places
variations: Update: my heart was racing after the scoop today, but that was anxiety, not strength.
Note: pre-workout gave me a full session of energy and I spent every drop of it on things unrelated to the gym.

items.sourceNote: 「This year, I'm resolving to outlift my excuses」新年決心梗重寫

zh-tw.content:
今年的新年目標：把我的藉口舉起來。
它們比我這輩子練過的任何重量都重——早上太冷、晚上太累、假日要放鬆、平日沒時間。

如果藉口算一個訓練部位，我大概是全健身房最壯的那個。
zh-tw.editorNote: 跨年或月初傳給那個決心滿滿、藉口更滿的朋友，主打自嘲但帶點鼓勵
variations: 更新：新年第三天，我的藉口已經完成了一整套循環訓練，我還沒。
備註：唯一被我穩定操練的，是找不去健身房的理由。

en.content:
New Year's goal: finally outlift my excuses.
They're heavier than anything I've ever put on a bar — too cold in the morning, too tired at night, weekends are for resting, weekdays have no time.

If excuses counted as a muscle group, I'd be the most jacked person in the whole gym.
en.editorNote: Post around New Year or the first of the month for the friend who's full of resolve and even more full of excuses — self-deprecating but a little encouraging
variations: Update: it's day three of the new year and my excuses have already finished a full circuit. I haven't.
Note: the only thing I train consistently is my list of reasons not to go.

items.sourceNote: 「I do squats so my snacks have a nice shelf」翹臀當零食架的梗重寫

zh-tw.content:
有人問我為什麼那麼認真練深蹲。
我說：我在幫我的零食蓋一個架子。

練了三個月，翹臀還沒長出來，但我對零食的愛已經穩如泰山——地基打得很紮實，只是上面還沒蓋東西。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個練臀動機不純、其實是為了吃的朋友，主打歪理但可愛
variations: 更新：今天深蹲時我想的不是肌肉，是等下要放上這座架子的雞排。
備註：翹臀是手段，零食才是目的，我從來沒搞錯過重點。

en.content:
Someone asked why I take squats so seriously.
I said: I'm building a shelf for my snacks.

Three months in, the shelf hasn't shown up yet, but my devotion to snacks is rock solid — the foundation is poured, there's just nothing on top of it so far.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend whose glute-day motivation is secretly all about food — ridiculous logic, but cute
variations: Update: mid-squat today I wasn't thinking about muscles, I was picturing the fried chicken going on this shelf later.
Note: the glutes are the means, the snacks are the goal, and I've never once confused the two.

items.sourceNote: 「I have mixed feelings about lifting—mostly sore ones」雙關梗重寫

zh-tw.content:
